45APLOG_USE_MODULE(proxy);
46
47/*
48 * Opaque structure containing infos for CONNECT-ing an origin server through a
49 * remote (forward) proxy. Saved in the (opaque) proxy_conn_rec::forward pointer
50 * field for backend connections kept alive, allowing for reuse when subsequent
51 * requests should be routed through the same remote proxy.
52 */ view japanese
53typedef struct {
54    const char   *proxy_auth;      /* Proxy authorization */ view japanese
55    const char   *target_host;     /* Target/origin hostname */ view japanese
56    apr_port_t   target_port;      /* Target/origin port */ view japanese
57} remote_connect_info;
58
59/*
60 * Opaque structure containing a refcounted and TTL'ed address.
61 */ view japanese
62typedef struct proxy_address {
63    apr_sockaddr_t *addr;       /* Remote address info */ view japanese
64    const char *hostname;       /* Remote host name */ view japanese
65    apr_port_t hostport;        /* Remote host port */ view japanese
66    apr_uint32_t refcount;      /* Number of conns and/or worker using it */ view japanese
67    apr_uint32_t expiry;        /* Expiry timestamp (seconds to proxy_start_time) */ view japanese
68} proxy_address;
69
70/* Global balancer counter */ view japanese
71int PROXY_DECLARE_DATA proxy_lb_workers = 0;
72static int lb_workers_limit = 0;
73const apr_strmatch_pattern PROXY_DECLARE_DATA *ap_proxy_strmatch_path;
74const apr_strmatch_pattern PROXY_DECLARE_DATA *ap_proxy_strmatch_domain;
75
76extern apr_global_mutex_t *proxy_mutex;
77
78static const apr_time_t *proxy_start_time; /* epoch for expiring addresses */ view japanese

80static int proxy_match_ipaddr(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r);
81static int proxy_match_domainname(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r);
82static int proxy_match_hostname(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r);
83static int proxy_match_word(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r);

85static proxy_worker *proxy_balancer_get_best_worker(proxy_balancer *balancer,
86                                                    request_rec *r,
87                                                    proxy_is_best_callback_fn_t *is_best,
88                                                    void *baton);

90APR_IMPLEMENT_OPTIONAL_HOOK_RUN_ALL(proxy, PROXY, int, create_req,

94PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_strncpy(char *dst, const char *src,
95                                             apr_size_t dlen)
96{
97    char *thenil;
98    apr_size_t thelen;
99
100    /* special case handling */ view japanese
101    if (!dlen) {
102        /* XXX: APR_ENOSPACE would be better */ view japanese
103        return APR_EGENERAL;
104    }
105    if (!src) {
106        *dst = '\0';
107        return APR_SUCCESS;
108    }
109    thenil = apr_cpystrn(dst, src, dlen);
110    thelen = thenil - dst;
111    if (src[thelen] == '\0') {
112        return APR_SUCCESS;
113    }
114    return APR_EGENERAL;
115}
116
117/* already called in the knowledge that the characters are hex digits */ view japanese
118PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_hex2c(const char *x)
119{
120    int i;
121
122#if !APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC
123    int ch = x[0];
124
125    if (apr_isdigit(ch)) {
126        i = ch - '0';
127    }
128    else if (apr_isupper(ch)) {
129        i = ch - ('A' - 10);
130    }
131    else {
132        i = ch - ('a' - 10);
133    }
134    i <<= 4;
135
136    ch = x[1];
137    if (apr_isdigit(ch)) {
138        i += ch - '0';
139    }
140    else if (apr_isupper(ch)) {
141        i += ch - ('A' - 10);
142    }
143    else {
144        i += ch - ('a' - 10);
145    }
146    return i;
147#else /*APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC*/
148    /*
149     * we assume that the hex value refers to an ASCII character
150     * so convert to EBCDIC so that it makes sense locally;
151     *
152     * example:
153     *
154     * client specifies %20 in URL to refer to a space char;
155     * at this point we're called with EBCDIC "20"; after turning
156     * EBCDIC "20" into binary 0x20, we then need to assume that 0x20
157     * represents an ASCII char and convert 0x20 to EBCDIC, yielding
158     * 0x40
159     */ view japanese
160    char buf[1];
161
162    if (1 == sscanf(x, "%2x", &i)) {
163        buf[0] = i & 0xFF;
164        ap_xlate_proto_from_ascii(buf, 1);
165        return buf[0];
166    }
167    else {
168        return 0;
169    }
170#endif /*APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC*/
171}
172
173PROXY_DECLARE(void) ap_proxy_c2hex(int ch, char *x)
174{
175#if !APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC
176    int i;
177
178    x[0] = '%';
179    i = (ch & 0xF0) >> 4;
180    if (i >= 10) {
181        x[1] = ('A' - 10) + i;
182    }
183    else {
184        x[1] = '0' + i;
185    }
186
187    i = ch & 0x0F;
188    if (i >= 10) {
189        x[2] = ('A' - 10) + i;
190    }
191    else {
192        x[2] = '0' + i;
193    }
194#else /*APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC*/
195    static const char ntoa[] = { "0123456789ABCDEF" };
196    char buf[1];
197
198    ch &= 0xFF;
199
200    buf[0] = ch;
201    ap_xlate_proto_to_ascii(buf, 1);
202
203    x[0] = '%';
204    x[1] = ntoa[(buf[0] >> 4) & 0x0F];
205    x[2] = ntoa[buf[0] & 0x0F];
206    x[3] = '\0';
207#endif /*APR_CHARSET_EBCDIC*/ view japanese
208}
209
210/*
211 * canonicalise a URL-encoded string
212 */ view japanese
213
214/*
215 * Convert a URL-encoded string to canonical form.
216 * It decodes characters which need not be encoded,
217 * and encodes those which must be encoded, and does not touch
218 * those which must not be touched.
219 */
220PROXY_DECLARE(char *)ap_proxy_canonenc_ex(apr_pool_t *p, const char *x, int len,
221                                          enum enctype t, int flags,
222                                          int proxyreq)
223{
224    int i, j, ch;
225    char *y;
226    char *allowed;  /* characters which should not be encoded */ view japanese
227    char *reserved; /* characters which much not be en/de-coded */ view japanese
228    int forcedec = flags & PROXY_CANONENC_FORCEDEC;
229    int noencslashesenc = flags & PROXY_CANONENC_NOENCODEDSLASHENCODING;
230
231/*
232 * N.B. in addition to :@&=, this allows ';' in an http path
233 * and '?' in an ftp path -- this may be revised
234 *
235 * Also, it makes a '+' character in a search string reserved, as
236 * it may be form-encoded. (Although RFC 1738 doesn't allow this -
237 * it only permits ; / ? : @ = & as reserved chars.)
238 */ view japanese
239    if (t == enc_path) {
240        allowed = "~$-_.+!*'(),;:@&=";
241    }
242    else if (t == enc_search) {
243        allowed = "$-_.!*'(),;:@&=";
244    }
245    else if (t == enc_user) {
246        allowed = "$-_.+!*'(),;@&=";
247    }
248    else if (t == enc_fpath) {
249        allowed = "$-_.+!*'(),?:@&=";
250    }
251    else {            /* if (t == enc_parm) */ view japanese
252        allowed = "$-_.+!*'(),?/:@&=";
253    }
254
255    if (t == enc_path) {
256        reserved = "/";
257    }
258    else if (t == enc_search) {
259        reserved = "+";
260    }
261    else {
262        reserved = "";
263    }
264
265    y = apr_palloc(p, 3 * len + 1);
266
267    for (i = 0, j = 0; i < len; i++, j++) {
268/* always handle '/' first */ view japanese
269        ch = x[i];
270        if (strchr(reserved, ch)) {
271            y[j] = ch;
272            continue;
273        }
274/*
275 * decode it if not already done. do not decode reverse proxied URLs
276 * unless specifically forced
277 */ view japanese
278        if ((forcedec || noencslashesenc
279            || (proxyreq && proxyreq != PROXYREQ_REVERSE)) && ch == '%') {
280            if (!apr_isxdigit(x[i + 1]) || !apr_isxdigit(x[i + 2])) {
281                return NULL;
282            }
283            ch = ap_proxy_hex2c(&x[i + 1]);
284            if (ch != 0 && strchr(reserved, ch)) {  /* keep it encoded */ view japanese
285                y[j++] = x[i++];
286                y[j++] = x[i++];
287                y[j] = x[i];
288                continue;
289            }
290            if (noencslashesenc && !forcedec && (proxyreq == PROXYREQ_REVERSE)) {
291                /*
292                 * In the reverse proxy case when we only want to keep encoded
293                 * slashes untouched revert back to '%' which will cause
294                 * '%' to be encoded in the following.
295                 */ view japanese
296                ch = '%';
297            }
298            else {
299                i += 2;
300            }
301        }
302/* recode it, if necessary */ view japanese
303        if (!apr_isalnum(ch) && !strchr(allowed, ch)) {
304            ap_proxy_c2hex(ch, &y[j]);
305            j += 2;
306        }
307        else {
308            y[j] = ch;
309        }
310    }
311    y[j] = '\0';
312    return y;
313}
314
315/*
316 * Convert a URL-encoded string to canonical form.
317 * It decodes characters which need not be encoded,
318 * and encodes those which must be encoded, and does not touch
319 * those which must not be touched.
320 */ view japanese
321PROXY_DECLARE(char *)ap_proxy_canonenc(apr_pool_t *p, const char *x, int len,
322                                       enum enctype t, int forcedec,
323                                       int proxyreq)
324{
325    int flags;
326
327    flags = forcedec ? PROXY_CANONENC_FORCEDEC : 0;
328    return ap_proxy_canonenc_ex(p, x, len, t, flags, proxyreq);
329}
330
331/*
332 * Parses network-location.
333 *    urlp           on input the URL; on output the path, after the leading /
334 *    user           NULL if no user/password permitted
335 *    password       holder for password
336 *    host           holder for host
337 *    port           port number; only set if one is supplied.
338 *
339 * Returns an error string.
340 */ view japanese
341PROXY_DECLARE(char *)
342     ap_proxy_canon_netloc(apr_pool_t *p, char **const urlp, char **userp,
343            char **passwordp, char **hostp, apr_port_t *port)
344{
345    char *addr, *scope_id, *strp, *host, *url = *urlp;
346    char *user = NULL, *password = NULL;
347    apr_port_t tmp_port;
348    apr_status_t rv;
349
350    if (url[0] != '/' || url[1] != '/') {
351        return "Malformed URL";
352    }
353    host = url + 2;
354    url = strchr(host, '/');
355    if (url == NULL) {
356        url = "";
357    }
358    else {
359        *(url++) = '\0';    /* skip separating '/' */ view japanese
360    }
361
362    /* find _last_ '@' since it might occur in user/password part */ view japanese
363    strp = strrchr(host, '@');
364
365    if (strp != NULL) {
366        *strp = '\0';
367        user = host;
368        host = strp + 1;
369
370/* find password */ view japanese
371        strp = strchr(user, ':');
372        if (strp != NULL) {
373            *strp = '\0';
374            password = ap_proxy_canonenc(p, strp + 1, strlen(strp + 1), enc_user, 1, 0);
375            if (password == NULL) {
376                return "Bad %-escape in URL (password)";
377            }
378        }
379
380        user = ap_proxy_canonenc(p, user, strlen(user), enc_user, 1, 0);
381        if (user == NULL) {
382            return "Bad %-escape in URL (username)";
383        }
384    }
385    if (userp != NULL) {
386        *userp = user;
387    }
388    if (passwordp != NULL) {
389        *passwordp = password;
390    }
391
392    /*
393     * Parse the host string to separate host portion from optional port.
394     * Perform range checking on port.
395     */ view japanese
396    rv = apr_parse_addr_port(&addr, &scope_id, &tmp_port, host, p);
397    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS || addr == NULL || scope_id != NULL) {
398        return "Invalid host/port";
399    }
400    if (tmp_port != 0) { /* only update caller's port if port was specified */ view japanese
401        *port = tmp_port;
402    }
403
404    ap_str_tolower(addr); /* DNS names are case-insensitive */ view japanese
405
406    *urlp = url;
407    *hostp = addr;
408
409    return NULL;
410}
411
412static int proxyerror_core(request_rec *r, int statuscode, const char *message,
413                           apr_status_t rv)
414{
415    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00898)
416                  "%s returned by %s", message, r->uri);
417
418    apr_table_setn(r->notes, "error-notes",
419        apr_pstrcat(r->pool,
420            "The proxy server could not handle the request<p>"
421            "Reason: <strong>", ap_escape_html(r->pool, message),
422            "</strong></p>",
423            NULL));
424
425    /* Allow "error-notes" string to be printed by ap_send_error_response() */ view japanese
426    apr_table_setn(r->notes, "verbose-error-to", "*");
427
428    r->status_line = apr_psprintf(r->pool, "%3.3u Proxy Error", statuscode);
429    return statuscode;
430}
431
432PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxyerror(request_rec *r, int statuscode, const char *message)
433{
434    return proxyerror_core(r, statuscode, message, 0);
435}
436
437static const char *
438     proxy_get_host_of_request(request_rec *r)
439{
440    char *url, *user = NULL, *password = NULL, *err, *host = NULL;
441    apr_port_t port;
442
443    if (r->hostname != NULL) {
444        return r->hostname;
445    }
446
447    /* Set url to the first char after "scheme://" */ view japanese
448    if ((url = strchr(r->uri, ':')) == NULL || url[1] != '/' || url[2] != '/') {
449        return NULL;
450    }
451
452    url = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, &url[1]);    /* make it point to "//", which is what proxy_canon_netloc expects */ view japanese
453
454    err = ap_proxy_canon_netloc(r->pool, &url, &user, &password, &host, &port);
455
456    if (err != NULL) {
457        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00899) "%s", err);
458    }
459
460    r->hostname = host;
461
462    return host;        /* ought to return the port, too */ view japanese
463}
464
465/* Return TRUE if addr represents an IP address (or an IP network address) */
466PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_ipaddr(struct dirconn_entry *This, apr_pool_t *p)
467{
468    const char *addr = This->name;
469    long ip_addr[4];
470    int i, quads;
471    long bits;
472
473    /*
474     * if the address is given with an explicit netmask, use that
475     * Due to a deficiency in apr_inet_addr(), it is impossible to parse
476     * "partial" addresses (with less than 4 quads) correctly, i.e.
477     * 192.168.123 is parsed as 192.168.0.123, which is not what I want.
478     * I therefore have to parse the IP address manually:
479     * if (proxy_readmask(This->name, &This->addr.s_addr, &This->mask.s_addr) == 0)
480     * addr and mask were set by proxy_readmask()
481     * return 1;
482     */ view japanese
483
484    /*
485     * Parse IP addr manually, optionally allowing
486     * abbreviated net addresses like 192.168.
487     */ view japanese
488
489    /* Iterate over up to 4 (dotted) quads. */ view japanese
490    for (quads = 0; quads < 4 && *addr != '\0'; ++quads) {
491        char *tmp;
492
493        if (*addr == '/' && quads > 0) {  /* netmask starts here. */ view japanese
494            break;
495        }
496
497        if (!apr_isdigit(*addr)) {
498            return 0;       /* no digit at start of quad */ view japanese
499        }
500
501        ip_addr[quads] = strtol(addr, &tmp, 0);
502
503        if (tmp == addr) {  /* expected a digit, found something else */
504            return 0;
505        }
506
507        if (ip_addr[quads] < 0 || ip_addr[quads] > 255) {
508            /* invalid octet */ view japanese
509            return 0;
510        }
511
512        addr = tmp;
513
514        if (*addr == '.' && quads != 3) {
515            ++addr;     /* after the 4th quad, a dot would be illegal */ view japanese
516        }
517    }
518
519    for (This->addr.s_addr = 0, i = 0; i < quads; ++i) {
520        This->addr.s_addr |= htonl(ip_addr[i] << (24 - 8 * i));
521    }
522
523    if (addr[0] == '/' && apr_isdigit(addr[1])) {   /* net mask follows: */ view japanese
524        char *tmp;
525
526        ++addr;
527
528        bits = strtol(addr, &tmp, 0);
529
530        if (tmp == addr) {   /* expected a digit, found something else */ view japanese
531            return 0;
532        }
533
534        addr = tmp;
535
536        if (bits < 0 || bits > 32) { /* netmask must be between 0 and 32 */ view japanese
537            return 0;
538        }
539
540    }
541    else {
542        /*
543         * Determine (i.e., "guess") netmask by counting the
544         * number of trailing .0's; reduce #quads appropriately
545         * (so that 192.168.0.0 is equivalent to 192.168.)
546         */ view japanese
547        while (quads > 0 && ip_addr[quads - 1] == 0) {
548            --quads;
549        }
550
551        /* "IP Address should be given in dotted-quad form, optionally followed by a netmask (e.g., 192.168.111.0/24)"; */ view japanese
552        if (quads < 1) {
553            return 0;
554        }
555
556        /* every zero-byte counts as 8 zero-bits */ view japanese
557        bits = 8 * quads;
558
559        if (bits != 32) {     /* no warning for fully qualified IP address */ view japanese
560            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00900)
561                         "Warning: NetMask not supplied with IP-Addr; guessing: %s/%ld",
562                         inet_ntoa(This->addr), bits);
563        }
564    }
565
566    This->mask.s_addr = htonl(APR_INADDR_NONE << (32 - bits));
567
568    if (*addr == '\0' && (This->addr.s_addr & ~This->mask.s_addr) != 0) {
569        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00901)
570                     "Warning: NetMask and IP-Addr disagree in %s/%ld",
571                     inet_ntoa(This->addr), bits);
572        This->addr.s_addr &= This->mask.s_addr;
573        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00902)
574                     "         Set to %s/%ld", inet_ntoa(This->addr), bits);
575    }
576
577    if (*addr == '\0') {
578        This->matcher = proxy_match_ipaddr;
579        return 1;
580    }
581    else {
582        return (*addr == '\0'); /* okay iff we've parsed the whole string */ view japanese
583    }
584}
585
586/* Return TRUE if addr represents an IP address (or an IP network address) */ view japanese
587static int proxy_match_ipaddr(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r)
588{
589    int i, ip_addr[4];
590    struct in_addr addr, *ip;
591    const char *host = proxy_get_host_of_request(r);
592
593    if (host == NULL) {   /* oops! */
594       return 0;
595    }
596
597    memset(&addr, '\0', sizeof addr);
598    memset(ip_addr, '\0', sizeof ip_addr);
599
600    if (4 == sscanf(host, "%d.%d.%d.%d", &ip_addr[0], &ip_addr[1], &ip_addr[2], &ip_addr[3])) {
601        for (addr.s_addr = 0, i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {
602            /* ap_proxy_is_ipaddr() already confirmed that we have
603             * a valid octet in ip_addr[i]
604             */ view japanese
605            addr.s_addr |= htonl(ip_addr[i] << (24 - 8 * i));
606        }
607
608        if (This->addr.s_addr == (addr.s_addr & This->mask.s_addr)) {
609#if DEBUGGING
610            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00903)
611                         "1)IP-Match: %s[%s] <-> ", host, inet_ntoa(addr));
612            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00904)
613                         "%s/", inet_ntoa(This->addr));
614            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00905)
615                         "%s", inet_ntoa(This->mask));
616#endif
617            return 1;
618        }
619#if DEBUGGING
620        else {
621            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00906)
622                         "1)IP-NoMatch: %s[%s] <-> ", host, inet_ntoa(addr));
623            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00907)
624                         "%s/", inet_ntoa(This->addr));
625            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00908)
626                         "%s", inet_ntoa(This->mask));
627        }
628#endif
629    }
630    else {
631        struct apr_sockaddr_t *reqaddr;
632
633        if (apr_sockaddr_info_get(&reqaddr, host, APR_UNSPEC, 0, 0, r->pool)
634            != APR_SUCCESS) {
635#if DEBUGGING
636            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00909)
637             "2)IP-NoMatch: hostname=%s msg=Host not found", host);
638#endif
639            return 0;
640        }
641
642        /* Try to deal with multiple IP addr's for a host */
643        /* FIXME: This needs to be able to deal with IPv6 */ view japanese
644        while (reqaddr) {
645            ip = (struct in_addr *) reqaddr->ipaddr_ptr;
646            if (This->addr.s_addr == (ip->s_addr & This->mask.s_addr)) {
647#if DEBUGGING
648                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00910)
649                             "3)IP-Match: %s[%s] <-> ", host, inet_ntoa(*ip));
650                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00911)
651                             "%s/", inet_ntoa(This->addr));
652                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00912)
653                             "%s", inet_ntoa(This->mask));
654#endif
655                return 1;
656            }
657#if DEBUGGING
658            else {
659                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00913)
660                             "3)IP-NoMatch: %s[%s] <-> ", host, inet_ntoa(*ip));
661                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00914)
662                             "%s/", inet_ntoa(This->addr));
663                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00915)
664                             "%s", inet_ntoa(This->mask));
665            }
666#endif
667            reqaddr = reqaddr->next;
668        }
669    }
670
671    return 0;
672}
673
674/* Return TRUE if addr represents a domain name */ view japanese
675PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_domainname(struct dirconn_entry *This, apr_pool_t *p)
676{
677    char *addr = This->name;
678    int i;
679
680    /* Domain name must start with a '.' */ view japanese
681    if (addr[0] != '.') {
682        return 0;
683    }
684
685    /* rfc1035 says DNS names must consist of "[-a-zA-Z0-9]" and '.' */
686    for (i = 0; apr_isalnum(addr[i]) || addr[i] == '-' || addr[i] == '.'; ++i) {
687        continue;
688    }
689
690#if 0
691    if (addr[i] == ':') {
692    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_STARTUP, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(03234)
693                     "@@@@ handle optional port in proxy_is_domainname()");
694    /* @@@@ handle optional port */ view japanese
695    }
696#endif
697
698    if (addr[i] != '\0') {
699        return 0;
700    }
701
702    /* Strip trailing dots */
703    for (i = strlen(addr) - 1; i > 0 && addr[i] == '.'; --i) {
704        addr[i] = '\0';
705    }
706
707    This->matcher = proxy_match_domainname;
708    return 1;
709}
710
711/* Return TRUE if host "host" is in domain "domain" */ view japanese
712static int proxy_match_domainname(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r)
713{
714    const char *host = proxy_get_host_of_request(r);
715    int d_len = strlen(This->name), h_len;
716
717    if (host == NULL) {      /* some error was logged already */ view japanese
718        return 0;
719    }
720
721    h_len = strlen(host);
722
723    /* @@@ do this within the setup? */ view japanese
724    /* Ignore trailing dots in domain comparison: */ view japanese
725    while (d_len > 0 && This->name[d_len - 1] == '.') {
726        --d_len;
727    }
728    while (h_len > 0 && host[h_len - 1] == '.') {
729        --h_len;
730    }
731    return h_len > d_len
732        && strncasecmp(&host[h_len - d_len], This->name, d_len) == 0;
733}
734
735/* Return TRUE if host represents a host name */ view japanese
736PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_hostname(struct dirconn_entry *This, apr_pool_t *p)
737{
738    struct apr_sockaddr_t *addr;
739    char *host = This->name;
740    int i;
741
742    /* Host names must not start with a '.' */ view japanese
743    if (host[0] == '.') {
744        return 0;
745    }
746    /* rfc1035 says DNS names must consist of "[-a-zA-Z0-9]" and '.' */ view japanese
747    for (i = 0; apr_isalnum(host[i]) || host[i] == '-' || host[i] == '.'; ++i);
748
749    if (host[i] != '\0' || apr_sockaddr_info_get(&addr, host, APR_UNSPEC, 0, 0, p) != APR_SUCCESS) {
750        return 0;
751    }
752
753    This->hostaddr = addr;
754
755    /* Strip trailing dots */ view japanese
756    for (i = strlen(host) - 1; i > 0 && host[i] == '.'; --i) {
757        host[i] = '\0';
758    }
759
760    This->matcher = proxy_match_hostname;
761    return 1;
762}
763
764/* Return TRUE if host "host" is equal to host2 "host2" */ view japanese
765static int proxy_match_hostname(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r)
766{
767    char *host = This->name;
768    const char *host2 = proxy_get_host_of_request(r);
769    int h2_len;
770    int h1_len;
771
772    if (host == NULL || host2 == NULL) {
773        return 0; /* oops! */ view japanese
774    }
775
776    h2_len = strlen(host2);
777    h1_len = strlen(host);
778
779#if 0
780    struct apr_sockaddr_t *addr = *This->hostaddr;
781
782    /* Try to deal with multiple IP addr's for a host */ view japanese
783    while (addr) {
784        if (addr->ipaddr_ptr == ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?)
785            return 1;
786        addr = addr->next;
787    }
788#endif
789
790    /* Ignore trailing dots in host2 comparison: */ view japanese
791    while (h2_len > 0 && host2[h2_len - 1] == '.') {
792        --h2_len;
793    }
794    while (h1_len > 0 && host[h1_len - 1] == '.') {
795        --h1_len;
796    }
797    return h1_len == h2_len
798        && strncasecmp(host, host2, h1_len) == 0;
799}
800
801/* Return TRUE if addr is to be matched as a word */ view japanese
802PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_word(struct dirconn_entry *This, apr_pool_t *p)
803{
804    This->matcher = proxy_match_word;
805    return 1;
806}
807
808/* Return TRUE if string "str2" occurs literally in "str1" */ view japanese
809static int proxy_match_word(struct dirconn_entry *This, request_rec *r)
810{
811    const char *host = proxy_get_host_of_request(r);
812    return host != NULL && ap_strstr_c(host, This->name) != NULL;
813}
814
815/* Backwards-compatible interface. */ view japanese
816PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_checkproxyblock(request_rec *r, proxy_server_conf *conf,
817                             apr_sockaddr_t *uri_addr)
818{
819    return ap_proxy_checkproxyblock2(r, conf, uri_addr->hostname, uri_addr);
820}
821
822#define MAX_IP_STR_LEN (46)
823
824PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_checkproxyblock2(request_rec *r, proxy_server_conf *conf,
825                                             const char *hostname, apr_sockaddr_t *addr)
826{
827    int j;
828
829    /* XXX FIXME: conf->noproxies->elts is part of an opaque structure */ view japanese
830    for (j = 0; j < conf->noproxies->nelts; j++) {
831        struct noproxy_entry *npent = (struct noproxy_entry *) conf->noproxies->elts;
832        struct apr_sockaddr_t *conf_addr;
833
834        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
835                      "checking remote machine [%s] against [%s]",
836                      hostname, npent[j].name);
837        if (ap_strstr_c(hostname, npent[j].name) || npent[j].name[0] == '*') {
838            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(00916)
839                          "connect to remote machine %s blocked: name %s "
840                          "matched", hostname, npent[j].name);
841            return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
842        }
843
844        /* No IP address checks if no IP address was passed in,
845         * i.e. the forward address proxy case, where this server does
846         * not resolve the hostname.  */ view japanese
847        if (!addr)
848            continue;
849
850        for (conf_addr = npent[j].addr; conf_addr; conf_addr = conf_addr->next) {
851            char caddr[MAX_IP_STR_LEN], uaddr[MAX_IP_STR_LEN];
852            apr_sockaddr_t *uri_addr;
853
854            if (apr_sockaddr_ip_getbuf(caddr, sizeof caddr, conf_addr))
855                continue;
856
857            for (uri_addr = addr; uri_addr; uri_addr = uri_addr->next) {
858                if (apr_sockaddr_ip_getbuf(uaddr, sizeof uaddr, uri_addr))
859                    continue;
860                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
861                              "ProxyBlock comparing %s and %s", caddr, uaddr);
862                if (!strcmp(caddr, uaddr)) {
863                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(00917)
864                                  "connect to remote machine %s blocked: "
865                                  "IP %s matched", hostname, caddr);
866                    return HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
867                }
868            }
869        }
870    }
871
872    return OK;
873}
874
875/* set up the minimal filter set */ view japanese
876PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_pre_http_request(conn_rec *c, request_rec *r)
877{
878    ap_add_input_filter("HTTP_IN", NULL, r, c);
879    return OK;
880}
881
882PROXY_DECLARE(const char *) ap_proxy_location_reverse_map(request_rec *r,
883                              proxy_dir_conf *conf, const char *url)
884{
885    proxy_req_conf *rconf;
886    struct proxy_alias *ent;
887    int i, l1, l1_orig, l2;
888    char *u;
889
890    /*
891     * XXX FIXME: Make sure this handled the ambiguous case of the :<PORT>
892     * after the hostname
893     * XXX FIXME: Ensure the /uri component is a case sensitive match
894     */ view japanese
895    if (r->proxyreq != PROXYREQ_REVERSE) {
896        return url;
897    }
898
899    l1_orig = strlen(url);
900    if (conf->interpolate_env == 1) {
901        rconf = ap_get_module_config(r->request_config, &proxy_module);
902        ent = (struct proxy_alias *)rconf->raliases->elts;
903    }
904    else {
905        ent = (struct proxy_alias *)conf->raliases->elts;
906    }
907    for (i = 0; i < conf->raliases->nelts; i++) {
908        proxy_server_conf *sconf = (proxy_server_conf *)
909            ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config, &proxy_module);
910        proxy_balancer *balancer;
911        const char *real = ent[i].real;
912
913        /* Restore the url length, if it had been changed by the code below */ view japanese
914        l1 = l1_orig;
915
916        /*
917         * First check if mapping against a balancer and see
918         * if we have such a entity. If so, then we need to
919         * find the particulars of the actual worker which may
920         * or may not be the right one... basically, we need
921         * to find which member actually handled this request.
922         */ view japanese
923        if (ap_proxy_valid_balancer_name((char *)real, 0) &&
924            (balancer = ap_proxy_get_balancer(r->pool, sconf, real, 1))) {
925            int n, l3 = 0;
926            proxy_worker **worker = (proxy_worker **)balancer->workers->elts;
927            const char *urlpart = ap_strchr_c(real + sizeof(BALANCER_PREFIX) - 1, '/');
928            if (urlpart) {
929                if (!urlpart[1])
930                    urlpart = NULL;
931                else
932                    l3 = strlen(urlpart);
933            }
934            /* The balancer comparison is a bit trickier.  Given the context
935             *   BalancerMember balancer://alias http://example.com/foo
936             *   ProxyPassReverse /bash balancer://alias/bar
937             * translate url http://example.com/foo/bar/that to /bash/that
938             */ view japanese
939            for (n = 0; n < balancer->workers->nelts; n++) {
940                l2 = strlen((*worker)->s->name_ex);
941                if (urlpart) {
942                    /* urlpart (l3) assuredly starts with its own '/' */ view japanese
943                    if ((*worker)->s->name_ex[l2 - 1] == '/')
944                        --l2;
945                    if (l1 >= l2 + l3
946                            && strncasecmp((*worker)->s->name_ex, url, l2) == 0
947                            && strncmp(urlpart, url + l2, l3) == 0) {
948                        u = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ent[i].fake, &url[l2 + l3],
949                                        NULL);
950                        return ap_is_url(u) ? u : ap_construct_url(r->pool, u, r);
951                    }
952                }
953                else if (l1 >= l2 && strncasecmp((*worker)->s->name_ex, url, l2) == 0) {
954                    /* edge case where fake is just "/"... avoid double slash */ view japanese
955                    if ((ent[i].fake[0] == '/') && (ent[i].fake[1] == 0) && (url[l2] == '/')) {
956                        u = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, &url[l2]);
957                    } else {
958                        u = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ent[i].fake, &url[l2], NULL);
959                    }
960                    return ap_is_url(u) ? u : ap_construct_url(r->pool, u, r);
961                }
962                worker++;
963            }
964        }
965        else {
966            const char *part = url;
967            l2 = strlen(real);
968            if (real[0] == '/') {
969                part = ap_strstr_c(url, "://");
970                if (part) {
971                    part = ap_strchr_c(part+3, '/');
972                    if (part) {
973                        l1 = strlen(part);
974                    }
975                    else {
976                        part = url;
977                    }
978                }
979                else {
980                    part = url;
981                }
982            }
983            if (l2 > 0 && l1 >= l2 && strncasecmp(real, part, l2) == 0) {
984                u = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, ent[i].fake, &part[l2], NULL);
985                return ap_is_url(u) ? u : ap_construct_url(r->pool, u, r);
986            }
987        }
988    }
989
990    return url;
991}
992
993/*
994 * Cookies are a bit trickier to match: we've got two substrings to worry
995 * about, and we can't just find them with strstr 'cos of case.  Regexp
996 * matching would be an easy fix, but for better consistency with all the
997 * other matches we'll refrain and use apr_strmatch to find path=/domain=
998 * and stick to plain strings for the config values.
999 */ view japanese
1000PROXY_DECLARE(const char *) ap_proxy_cookie_reverse_map(request_rec *r,
1001                              proxy_dir_conf *conf, const char *str)
1002{
1003    proxy_req_conf *rconf = ap_get_module_config(r->request_config,
1004                                                 &proxy_module);
1005    struct proxy_alias *ent;
1006    apr_size_t len = strlen(str);
1007    const char *newpath = NULL;
1008    const char *newdomain = NULL;
1009    const char *pathp;
1010    const char *domainp;
1011    const char *pathe = NULL;
1012    const char *domaine = NULL;
1013    apr_size_t l1, l2, poffs = 0, doffs = 0;
1014    int i;
1015    int ddiff = 0;
1016    int pdiff = 0;
1017    char *tmpstr, *tmpstr_orig, *token, *last, *ret;
1018
1019    if (r->proxyreq != PROXYREQ_REVERSE) {
1020        return str;
1021    }
1022
1023   /*
1024    * Find the match and replacement, but save replacing until we've done
1025    * both path and domain so we know the new strlen
1026    */ view japanese
1027    tmpstr_orig = tmpstr = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, str);
1028    while ((token = apr_strtok(tmpstr, ";", &last))) {
1029        /* skip leading spaces */ view japanese
1030        while (apr_isspace(*token)) {
1031            ++token;
1032        }
1033
1034        if (ap_cstr_casecmpn("path=", token, 5) == 0) {
1035            pathp = token + 5;
1036            poffs = pathp - tmpstr_orig;
1037            l1 = strlen(pathp);
1038            pathe = str + poffs + l1;
1039            if (conf->interpolate_env == 1) {
1040                ent = (struct proxy_alias *)rconf->cookie_paths->elts;
1041            }
1042            else {
1043                ent = (struct proxy_alias *)conf->cookie_paths->elts;
1044            }
1045            for (i = 0; i < conf->cookie_paths->nelts; i++) {
1046                l2 = strlen(ent[i].fake);
1047                if (l1 >= l2 && strncmp(ent[i].fake, pathp, l2) == 0) {
1048                    newpath = ent[i].real;
1049                    pdiff = strlen(newpath) - l1;
1050                    break;
1051                }
1052            }
1053        }
1054        else if (ap_cstr_casecmpn("domain=", token, 7) == 0) {
1055            domainp = token + 7;
1056            doffs = domainp - tmpstr_orig;
1057            l1 = strlen(domainp);
1058            domaine = str + doffs + l1;
1059            if (conf->interpolate_env == 1) {
1060                ent = (struct proxy_alias *)rconf->cookie_domains->elts;
1061            }
1062            else {
1063                ent = (struct proxy_alias *)conf->cookie_domains->elts;
1064            }
1065            for (i = 0; i < conf->cookie_domains->nelts; i++) {
1066                l2 = strlen(ent[i].fake);
1067                if (l1 >= l2 && strncasecmp(ent[i].fake, domainp, l2) == 0) {
1068                    newdomain = ent[i].real;
1069                    ddiff = strlen(newdomain) - l1;
1070                    break;
1071                }
1072            }
1073        }
1074
1075        /* Iterate the remaining tokens using apr_strtok(NULL, ...) */ view japanese
1076        tmpstr = NULL;
1077    }
1078
1079    if (newpath) {
1080        ret = apr_palloc(r->pool, len + pdiff + ddiff + 1);
1081        l1 = strlen(newpath);
1082        if (newdomain) {
1083            l2 = strlen(newdomain);
1084            if (doffs > poffs) {
1085                memcpy(ret, str, poffs);
1086                memcpy(ret + poffs, newpath, l1);
1087                memcpy(ret + poffs + l1, pathe, str + doffs - pathe);
1088                memcpy(ret + doffs + pdiff, newdomain, l2);
1089                strcpy(ret + doffs + pdiff + l2, domaine);
1090            }
1091            else {
1092                memcpy(ret, str, doffs) ;
1093                memcpy(ret + doffs, newdomain, l2);
1094                memcpy(ret + doffs + l2, domaine, str + poffs - domaine);
1095                memcpy(ret + poffs + ddiff, newpath, l1);
1096                strcpy(ret + poffs + ddiff + l1, pathe);
1097            }
1098        }
1099        else {
1100            memcpy(ret, str, poffs);
1101            memcpy(ret + poffs, newpath, l1);
1102            strcpy(ret + poffs + l1, pathe);
1103        }
1104    }
1105    else if (newdomain) {
1106            ret = apr_palloc(r->pool, len + ddiff + 1);
1107            l2 = strlen(newdomain);
1108            memcpy(ret, str, doffs);
1109            memcpy(ret + doffs, newdomain, l2);
1110            strcpy(ret + doffs + l2, domaine);
1111    }
1112    else {
1113        ret = (char *)str; /* no change */ view japanese
1114    }
1115
1116    return ret;
1117}
1118
1119/*
1120 * BALANCER related...
1121 */ view japanese
1122
1123/*
1124 * verifies that the balancer name conforms to standards.
1125 */ view japanese
1126PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_valid_balancer_name(char *name, int i)
1127{
1128    if (!i)
1129        i = sizeof(BALANCER_PREFIX)-1;
1130    return (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(name, BALANCER_PREFIX, i));
1131}
1132
1133
1134PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_balancer *) ap_proxy_get_balancer(apr_pool_t *p,
1135                                                      proxy_server_conf *conf,
1136                                                      const char *url,
1137                                                      int care)
1138{
1139    proxy_balancer *balancer;
1140    char *c, *uri = apr_pstrdup(p, url);
1141    int i;
1142    proxy_hashes hash;
1143
1144    c = strchr(uri, ':');
1145    if (c == NULL || c[1] != '/' || c[2] != '/' || c[3] == '\0') {
1146        return NULL;
1147    }
1148    /* remove path from uri */
1149    if ((c = strchr(c + 3, '/'))) {
1150        *c = '\0';
1151    }
1152    ap_str_tolower(uri);
1153    hash.def = ap_proxy_hashfunc(uri, PROXY_HASHFUNC_DEFAULT);
1154    hash.fnv = ap_proxy_hashfunc(uri, PROXY_HASHFUNC_FNV);
1155    balancer = (proxy_balancer *)conf->balancers->elts;
1156    for (i = 0; i < conf->balancers->nelts; i++) {
1157        if (balancer->hash.def == hash.def && balancer->hash.fnv == hash.fnv) {
1158            if (!care || !balancer->s->inactive) {
1159                return balancer;
1160            }
1161        }
1162        balancer++;
1163    }
1164    return NULL;
1165}
1166
1167
1168PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_update_balancer(apr_pool_t *p,
1169                                                proxy_balancer *balancer,
1170                                                const char *url)
1171{
1172    apr_uri_t puri;
1173    if (!url) {
1174        return NULL;
1175    }
1176    if (apr_uri_parse(p, url, &puri) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1177        return apr_psprintf(p, "unable to parse: %s", url);
1178    }
1179    if (puri.path && PROXY_STRNCPY(balancer->s->vpath, puri.path) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1180        return apr_psprintf(p, "balancer %s front-end virtual-path (%s) too long",
1181                            balancer->s->name, puri.path);
1182    }
1183    if (puri.hostname && PROXY_STRNCPY(balancer->s->vhost, puri.hostname) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1184        return apr_psprintf(p, "balancer %s front-end vhost name (%s) too long",
1185                            balancer->s->name, puri.hostname);
1186    }
1187    return NULL;
1188}
1189
1190#define PROXY_UNSET_NONCE '\n'
1191
1192PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_define_balancer(apr_pool_t *p,
1193                                               proxy_balancer **balancer,
1194                                               proxy_server_conf *conf,
1195                                               const char *url,
1196                                               const char *alias,
1197                                               int do_malloc)
1198{
1199    proxy_balancer_method *lbmethod;
1200    proxy_balancer_shared *bshared;
1201    char *c, *q, *uri = apr_pstrdup(p, url);
1202    const char *sname;
1203
1204    /* We should never get here without a valid BALANCER_PREFIX... */ view japanese
1205
1206    c = strchr(uri, ':');
1207    if (c == NULL || c[1] != '/' || c[2] != '/' || c[3] == '\0')
1208        return apr_psprintf(p, "Bad syntax for a balancer name (%s)", uri);
1209    /* remove path from uri */ view japanese
1210    if ((q = strchr(c + 3, '/')))
1211        *q = '\0';
1212
1213    ap_str_tolower(uri);
1214    *balancer = apr_array_push(conf->balancers);
1215    memset(*balancer, 0, sizeof(proxy_balancer));
1216
1217    /*
1218     * NOTE: The default method is byrequests - if it doesn't
1219     * exist, that's OK at this time. We check when we share and sync
1220     */ view japanese
1221    lbmethod = ap_lookup_provider(PROXY_LBMETHOD, "byrequests", "0");
1222    (*balancer)->lbmethod = lbmethod;
1223    
1224    (*balancer)->workers = apr_array_make(p, 5, sizeof(proxy_worker *));
1225#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1226    (*balancer)->gmutex = NULL;
1227    (*balancer)->tmutex = NULL;
1228#endif
1229
1230    if (do_malloc)
1231        bshared = ap_malloc(sizeof(proxy_balancer_shared));
1232    else
1233        bshared = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(proxy_balancer_shared));
1234
1235    memset(bshared, 0, sizeof(proxy_balancer_shared));
1236
1237    bshared->was_malloced = (do_malloc != 0);
1238    PROXY_STRNCPY(bshared->lbpname, "byrequests");
1239    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(bshared->name, uri) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1240        if (do_malloc) free(bshared);
1241        return apr_psprintf(p, "balancer name (%s) too long", uri);
1242    }
1243    (*balancer)->lbmethod_set = 1;
1244
1245    /*
1246     * We do the below for verification. The real sname will be
1247     * done post_config
1248     */ view japanese
1249    ap_pstr2_alnum(p, bshared->name + sizeof(BALANCER_PREFIX) - 1,
1250                   &sname);
1251    sname = apr_pstrcat(p, conf->id, "_", sname, NULL);
1252    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(bshared->sname, sname) != APR_SUCCESS) {
1253        if (do_malloc) free(bshared);
1254        return apr_psprintf(p, "balancer safe-name (%s) too long", sname);
1255    }
1256    bshared->hash.def = ap_proxy_hashfunc(bshared->name, PROXY_HASHFUNC_DEFAULT);
1257    bshared->hash.fnv = ap_proxy_hashfunc(bshared->name, PROXY_HASHFUNC_FNV);
1258    (*balancer)->hash = bshared->hash;
1259
1260    bshared->forcerecovery = 1;
1261    bshared->sticky_separator = '.';
1262    *bshared->nonce = PROXY_UNSET_NONCE;  /* impossible valid input */ view japanese
1263
1264    (*balancer)->s = bshared;
1265    (*balancer)->sconf = conf;
1266
1267    return ap_proxy_update_balancer(p, *balancer, alias);
1268}
1269
1270/*
1271 * Create an already defined balancer and free up memory.
1272 */ view japanese
1273PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_share_balancer(proxy_balancer *balancer,
1274                                                    proxy_balancer_shared *shm,
1275                                                    int i)
1276{
1277    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
1278    proxy_balancer_method *lbmethod;
1279    char *action = "copying";
1280    if (!shm || !balancer->s)
1281        return APR_EINVAL;
1282
1283    if ((balancer->s->hash.def != shm->hash.def) ||
1284        (balancer->s->hash.fnv != shm->hash.fnv)) {
1285        memcpy(shm, balancer->s, sizeof(proxy_balancer_shared));
1286        if (balancer->s->was_malloced)
1287            free(balancer->s);
1288    } else {
1289        action = "re-using";
1290    }
1291    balancer->s = shm;
1292    balancer->s->index = i;
1293    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02337)
1294                 "%s shm[%d] (0x%pp) for %s", action, i, (void *)shm,
1295                 balancer->s->name);
1296    /* the below should always succeed */ view japanese
1297    lbmethod = ap_lookup_provider(PROXY_LBMETHOD, balancer->s->lbpname, "0");
1298    if (lbmethod) {
1299        balancer->lbmethod = lbmethod;
1300        balancer->lbmethod_set = 1;
1301    } else {
1302        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02432)
1303                     "Cannot find LB Method: %s", balancer->s->lbpname);
1304        return APR_EINVAL;
1305    }
1306    if (*balancer->s->nonce == PROXY_UNSET_NONCE) {
1307        char nonce[APR_UUID_FORMATTED_LENGTH + 1];
1308        apr_uuid_t uuid;
1309
1310        /* Generate a pseudo-UUID from the PRNG to use as a nonce for
1311         * the lifetime of the process. uuid.data is a char array so
1312         * this is an adequate substitute for apr_uuid_get(). */ view japanese
1313        ap_random_insecure_bytes(uuid.data, sizeof uuid.data);
1314        apr_uuid_format(nonce, &uuid);
1315        rv = PROXY_STRNCPY(balancer->s->nonce, nonce);
1316    }
1317    return rv;
1318}
1319
1320PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_initialize_balancer(proxy_balancer *balancer, server_rec *s, apr_pool_t *p)
1321{
1322#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1323    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
1324#endif
1325    ap_slotmem_provider_t *storage = balancer->storage;
1326    apr_size_t size;
1327    unsigned int num;
1328
1329    if (!storage) {
1330        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, s, APLOGNO(00918)
1331                     "no provider for %s", balancer->s->name);
1332        return APR_EGENERAL;
1333    }
1334    /*
1335     * for each balancer we need to init the global
1336     * mutex and then attach to the shared worker shm
1337     */ view japanese
1338    if (!balancer->gmutex) {
1339        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, s, APLOGNO(00919)
1340                     "no mutex %s", balancer->s->name);
1341        return APR_EGENERAL;
1342    }
1343
1344    /* Re-open the mutex for the child. */ view japanese
1345    rv = apr_global_mutex_child_init(&(balancer->gmutex),
1346                                     apr_global_mutex_lockfile(balancer->gmutex),
1347                                     p);
1348    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1349        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, s, APLOGNO(00920)
1350                     "Failed to reopen mutex %s in child",
1351                     balancer->s->name);
1352        return rv;
1353    }
1354
1355    /* now attach */ view japanese
1356    storage->attach(&(balancer->wslot), balancer->s->sname, &size, &num, p);
1357    if (!balancer->wslot) {
1358        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, s, APLOGNO(00921) "slotmem_attach failed");
1359        return APR_EGENERAL;
1360    }
1361
1362#if APR_HAS_THREADS
1363    if (balancer->tmutex == NULL) {
1364        rv = apr_thread_mutex_create(&(balancer->tmutex), APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, p);
1365        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1366            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, s, APLOGNO(00922)
1367                         "can not create balancer thread mutex");
1368            return rv;
1369        }
1370    }
1371#endif
1372    return APR_SUCCESS;
1373}
1374
1375static proxy_worker *proxy_balancer_get_best_worker(proxy_balancer *balancer,
1376                                                    request_rec *r,
1377                                                    proxy_is_best_callback_fn_t *is_best,
1378                                                    void *baton)
1379{
1380    int i = 0;
1381    int cur_lbset = 0;
1382    int max_lbset = 0;
1383    int unusable_workers = 0;
1384    apr_pool_t *tpool = NULL;
1385    apr_array_header_t *spares = NULL;
1386    apr_array_header_t *standbys = NULL;
1387    proxy_worker *worker = NULL;
1388    proxy_worker *best_worker = NULL;
1389
1390    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r->server, APLOGNO(10122)
1391                 "proxy: Entering %s for BALANCER (%s)",
1392                 balancer->lbmethod->name, balancer->s->name);
1393
1394    apr_pool_create(&tpool, r->pool);
1395    apr_pool_tag(tpool, "proxy_lb_best");
1396
1397    spares = apr_array_make(tpool, 1, sizeof(proxy_worker*));
1398    standbys = apr_array_make(tpool, 1, sizeof(proxy_worker*));
1399
1400    /* Process lbsets in order, only replacing unusable workers in a given lbset
1401     * with available spares from the same lbset. Hot standbys will be used as a
1402     * last resort when all other workers and spares are unavailable.
1403     */ view japanese
1404    for (cur_lbset = 0; !best_worker && (cur_lbset <= max_lbset); cur_lbset++) {
1405        unusable_workers = 0;
1406        apr_array_clear(spares);
1407        apr_array_clear(standbys);
1408
1409        for (i = 0; i < balancer->workers->nelts; i++) {
1410            worker = APR_ARRAY_IDX(balancer->workers, i, proxy_worker *);
1411
1412            if (worker->s->lbset > max_lbset) {
1413                max_lbset = worker->s->lbset;
1414            }
1415
1416            if (worker->s->lbset != cur_lbset) {
1417                continue;
1418            }
1419
1420            /* A draining worker that is neither a spare nor a standby should be
1421             * considered unusable to be replaced by spares.
1422             */ view japanese
1423            if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_DRAINING(worker)) {
1424                if (!PROXY_WORKER_IS_SPARE(worker) && !PROXY_WORKER_IS_STANDBY(worker)) {
1425                    unusable_workers++;
1426                }
1427
1428                continue;
1429            }
1430
1431            /* If the worker is in error state run retry on that worker. It will
1432             * be marked as operational if the retry timeout is elapsed.  The
1433             * worker might still be unusable, but we try anyway.
1434             */ view japanese
1435            if (!PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
1436                ap_proxy_retry_worker("BALANCER", worker, r->server);
1437            }
1438
1439            if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_SPARE(worker)) {
1440                if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
1441                    APR_ARRAY_PUSH(spares, proxy_worker *) = worker;
1442                }
1443            }
1444            else if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_STANDBY(worker)) {
1445                if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
1446                    APR_ARRAY_PUSH(standbys, proxy_worker *) = worker;
1447                }
1448            }
1449            else if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
1450              if (is_best(worker, best_worker, baton)) {
1451                best_worker = worker;
1452              }
1453            }
1454            else {
1455                unusable_workers++;
1456            }
1457        }
1458
1459        /* Check if any spares are best. */ view japanese
1460        for (i = 0; (i < spares->nelts) && (i < unusable_workers); i++) {
1461          worker = APR_ARRAY_IDX(spares, i, proxy_worker *);
1462
1463          if (is_best(worker, best_worker, baton)) {
1464            best_worker = worker;
1465          }
1466        }
1467
1468        /* If no workers are available, use the standbys. */ view japanese
1469        if (!best_worker) {
1470            for (i = 0; i < standbys->nelts; i++) {
1471              worker = APR_ARRAY_IDX(standbys, i, proxy_worker *);
1472
1473              if (is_best(worker, best_worker, baton)) {
1474                best_worker = worker;
1475              }
1476            }
1477        }
1478    }
1479
1480    apr_pool_destroy(tpool);
1481
1482    if (best_worker) {
1483        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r->server, APLOGNO(10123)
1484                     "proxy: %s selected worker \"%s\" : busy %" APR_SIZE_T_FMT " : lbstatus %d",
1485                     balancer->lbmethod->name, best_worker->s->name_ex,
1486                     best_worker->s->busy, best_worker->s->lbstatus);
1487    }
1488
1489    return best_worker;
1490}
1491
1492PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_worker *) ap_proxy_balancer_get_best_worker(proxy_balancer *balancer,
1493                                                                request_rec *r,
1494                                                                proxy_is_best_callback_fn_t *is_best,
1495                                                                void *baton)
1496{
1497    return proxy_balancer_get_best_worker(balancer, r, is_best, baton);
1498}
1499
1500/*
1501 * CONNECTION related...
1502 */ view japanese
1503
1504static void socket_cleanup(proxy_conn_rec *conn)
1505{
1506    conn->sock = NULL;
1507    conn->tmp_bb = NULL;
1508    conn->connection = NULL;
1509    conn->ssl_hostname = NULL;
1510    apr_pool_clear(conn->scpool);
1511    conn->close = 0;
1512}
1513
1514static void conn_cleanup(proxy_conn_rec *conn)
1515{
1516    socket_cleanup(conn);
1517    conn->address = NULL;
1518    conn->addr = NULL;
1519    conn->hostname = NULL;
1520    conn->port = 0;
1521    conn->uds_path = NULL;
1522    if (conn->uds_pool) {
1523        apr_pool_clear(conn->uds_pool);
1524    }
1525}
1526
1527static apr_status_t conn_pool_cleanup(void *theworker)
1528{
1529    /* Signal that the child is exiting */ view japanese
1530    ((proxy_worker *)theworker)->cp = NULL;
1531    return APR_SUCCESS;
1532}
1533
1534static apr_pool_t *make_conn_subpool(apr_pool_t *p, const char *tag,
1535                                     server_rec *s)
1536{
1537    apr_pool_t *sp = NULL;
1538    apr_allocator_t *alloc;
1539    apr_thread_mutex_t *mutex;
1540    apr_status_t rv;
1541
1542    rv = apr_allocator_create(&alloc);
1543    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
1544        rv = apr_thread_mutex_create(&mutex, APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, p);
1545        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
1546            apr_allocator_mutex_set(alloc, mutex);
1547            apr_allocator_max_free_set(alloc, ap_max_mem_free);
1548            rv = apr_pool_create_ex(&sp, p, NULL, alloc);
1549        }
1550        else {
1551            apr_allocator_destroy(alloc);
1552        }
1553    }
1554    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
1555        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, rv, s, APLOGNO(10474)
1556                     "failed to create %s pool", tag);
1557        ap_abort_on_oom();
1558        return NULL; /* not reached */ view japanese
1559    }
1560    apr_allocator_owner_set(alloc, sp);
1561    apr_pool_tag(sp, tag);
1562
1563    return sp;
1564}
1565
1566static void init_conn_pool(apr_pool_t *p, proxy_worker *worker, server_rec *s)
1567{
1568    proxy_conn_pool *cp;
1569
1570    /*
1571     * Alloc from the same pool as worker.
1572     * proxy_conn_pool is permanently attached to the worker.
1573     */ view japanese
1574    cp = (proxy_conn_pool *)apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(proxy_conn_pool));
1575    worker->cp = cp;
1576
1577    /*
1578     * We need a first pool (cp->pool) to maintain the connections attached to
1579     * the worker and a second one (cp->dns_pool) to maintain the DNS addresses
1580     * in use (TTL'ed, refcounted). New connections are created as/on a subpool
1581     * of cp->pool and new addresses as/on a subpool of cp->dns_pool, such that
1582     * both leaks (the subpools can be destroyed when the connections and/or
1583     * addresses are over) and race conditions (the creation/destruction of
1584     * subpools is protected by the parent pool's mutex) can be avoided.
1585     *
1586     * cp->dns_pool is created before cp->pool because when a connection on the
1587     * latter is destroyed it might destroy an address on the former, so when
1588     * the base pools are destroyed (e.g. child exit) we thusly make sure that
1589     * cp->dns_pool and its subpools are still alive when cp->pool gets killed.
1590     *
1591     * Both cp->dns_pool and cp->pool have their own allocator/mutex too since
1592     * acquiring connections and addresses don't need to contend.
1593     */ view japanese
1594    cp->dns_pool = make_conn_subpool(p, "proxy_worker_dns", s);
1595    cp->pool = make_conn_subpool(p, "proxy_worker_cp", s);
1596
1597    /* When p is cleaning up the child is exiting, signal that to e.g. avoid
1598     * destroying the subpools explicitely in connection_destructor() when
1599     * they have been destroyed already by the reslist cleanup.
1600     */ view japanese
1601    apr_pool_pre_cleanup_register(p, worker, conn_pool_cleanup);
1602}
1603
1604PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_connection_reusable(proxy_conn_rec *conn)
1605{
1606    proxy_worker *worker = conn->worker;
1607
1608    return !(conn->close || worker->s->disablereuse);
1609}
1610
1611static proxy_conn_rec *connection_make(apr_pool_t *p, proxy_worker *worker)
1612{
1613    proxy_conn_rec *conn;
1614
1615    conn = apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(proxy_conn_rec));
1616    conn->pool = p;
1617    conn->worker = worker;
1618
1619    /*
1620     * Create another subpool that manages the data for the
1621     * socket and the connection member of the proxy_conn_rec struct as we
1622     * destroy this data more frequently than other data in the proxy_conn_rec
1623     * struct like hostname and addr (at least in the case where we have
1624     * keepalive connections that timed out).
1625     *
1626     * XXX: this is really needed only when worker->s->is_address_reusable,
1627     *      otherwise conn->scpool = conn->pool would be fine. For now we
1628     *      can't change it since it's (kind of) part of the API.
1629     */ view japanese
1630    apr_pool_create(&conn->scpool, p);
1631    apr_pool_tag(conn->scpool, "proxy_conn_scpool");
1632
1633    return conn;
1634}
1635
1636static void connection_cleanup(void *theconn)
1637{
1638    proxy_conn_rec *conn = (proxy_conn_rec *)theconn;
1639    proxy_worker *worker = conn->worker;
1640
1641    /* Sanity check: Did we already return the pooled connection? */ view japanese
1642    if (conn->inreslist) {
1643        ap_log_perror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, conn->pool, APLOGNO(00923)
1644                      "Pooled connection 0x%pp for worker %s has been"
1645                      " already returned to the connection pool.", conn,
1646                      ap_proxy_worker_name(conn->pool, worker));
1647        return;
1648    }
1649
1650    if (conn->r) {
1651        apr_pool_destroy(conn->r->pool);
1652        conn->r = NULL;
1653    }
1654
1655    /* determine if the connection should be cleared, closed or reused */ view japanese
1656    if (!worker->s->is_address_reusable) {
1657        apr_pool_t *p = conn->pool;
1658        apr_pool_clear(p);
1659        conn = connection_make(p, worker);
1660    }
1661    else if (!conn->sock
1662             || (conn->connection
1663                 && conn->connection->keepalive == AP_CONN_CLOSE)
1664             || !ap_proxy_connection_reusable(conn)) {
1665        socket_cleanup(conn);
1666    }
1667    else if (conn->is_ssl) {
1668        /* The current ssl section/dir config of the conn is not necessarily
1669         * the one it will be reused for, so while the conn is in the reslist
1670         * reset its ssl config to the worker's, until a new user sets its own
1671         * ssl config eventually in proxy_connection_create() and so on.
1672         */ view japanese
1673        ap_proxy_ssl_engine(conn->connection, worker->section_config, 1);
1674    }
1675
1676    if (worker->s->hmax && worker->cp->res) {
1677        conn->inreslist = 1;
1678        apr_reslist_release(worker->cp->res, (void *)conn);
1679    }
1680    else {
1681        worker->cp->conn = conn;
1682    }
1683}
1684
1685/* DEPRECATED */
1686PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_ssl_connection_cleanup(proxy_conn_rec *conn,
1687                                                            request_rec *r)
1688{
1689    apr_status_t rv;
1690
1691    /*
1692     * If we have an existing SSL connection it might be possible that the
1693     * server sent some SSL message we have not read so far (e.g. an SSL
1694     * shutdown message if the server closed the keepalive connection while
1695     * the connection was held unused in our pool).
1696     * So ensure that if present (=> APR_NONBLOCK_READ) it is read and
1697     * processed. We don't expect any data to be in the returned brigade.
1698     */ view japanese
1699    if (conn->sock && conn->connection) {
1700        rv = ap_get_brigade(conn->connection->input_filters, conn->tmp_bb,
1701                            AP_MODE_READBYTES, APR_NONBLOCK_READ,
1702                            HUGE_STRING_LEN);
1703        if (!APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(conn->tmp_bb)) {
1704            apr_off_t len;
1705
1706            rv = apr_brigade_length(conn->tmp_bb, 0, &len);
1707            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, rv, r,
1708                          "SSL cleanup brigade contained %"
1709                          APR_OFF_T_FMT " bytes of data.", len);
1710            apr_brigade_cleanup(conn->tmp_bb);
1711        }
1712        if ((rv != APR_SUCCESS) && !APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(rv)) {
1713            socket_cleanup(conn);
1714        }
1715    }
1716    return APR_SUCCESS;
1717}
1718
1719/* reslist constructor */ view japanese
1720static apr_status_t connection_constructor(void **resource, void *params,
1721                                           apr_pool_t *pool)
1722{
1723    apr_pool_t *p;
1724    proxy_conn_rec *conn;
1725    proxy_worker *worker = (proxy_worker *)params;
1726
1727    /*
1728     * Create a subpool for each connection
1729     * This keeps the memory consumption constant
1730     * when it's recycled or destroyed.
1731     */ view japanese
1732    apr_pool_create(&p, pool);
1733    apr_pool_tag(p, "proxy_conn_pool");
1734    conn = connection_make(p, worker);
1735    conn->inreslist = 1;
1736
1737    *resource = conn;
1738    return APR_SUCCESS;
1739}
1740
1741/* reslist destructor */ view japanese
1742static apr_status_t connection_destructor(void *resource, void *params,
1743                                          apr_pool_t *pool)
1744{
1745    proxy_worker *worker = params;
1746
1747    /* Destroy the pool only if not called from reslist_destroy */ view japanese
1748    if (worker->cp) {
1749        proxy_conn_rec *conn = resource;
1750        apr_pool_destroy(conn->pool);
1751    }
1752
1753    return APR_SUCCESS;
1754}
1755
1756/*
1757 * WORKER related...
1758 */ view japanese
1759
1760PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_worker_name(apr_pool_t *p,
1761                                           proxy_worker *worker)
1762{
1763    if (!(*worker->s->uds_path) || !p) {
1764        /* just in case */ view japanese
1765        return worker->s->name_ex;
1766    }
1767    return apr_pstrcat(p, "unix:", worker->s->uds_path, "|", worker->s->name_ex, NULL);
1768}
1769
1770PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_worker_can_upgrade(apr_pool_t *p,
1771                                               const proxy_worker *worker,
1772                                               const char *upgrade,
1773                                               const char *dflt)
1774{
1775    /* Find in worker->s->upgrade list (if any) */ view japanese
1776    const char *worker_upgrade = worker->s->upgrade;
1777    if (*worker_upgrade) {
1778        return (strcmp(worker_upgrade, "*") == 0
1779                || ap_cstr_casecmp(worker_upgrade, upgrade) == 0
1780                || ap_find_token(p, worker_upgrade, upgrade));
1781    }
1782
1783    /* Compare to the provided default (if any) */ view japanese
1784    return (dflt && ap_cstr_casecmp(dflt, upgrade) == 0);
1785}
1786
1787/*
1788 * Taken from ap_strcmp_match() :
1789 * Match = 0, NoMatch = 1, Abort = -1, Inval = -2
1790 * Based loosely on sections of wildmat.c by Rich Salz
1791 * Hmmm... shouldn't this really go component by component?
1792 *
1793 * Adds handling of the "\<any>" => "<any>" unescaping.
1794 */ view japanese
1795static int ap_proxy_strcmp_ematch(const char *str, const char *expected)
1796{
1797    apr_size_t x, y;
1798
1799    for (x = 0, y = 0; expected[y]; ++y, ++x) {
1800        if (expected[y] == '$' && apr_isdigit(expected[y + 1])) {
1801            do {
1802                y += 2;
1803            } while (expected[y] == '$' && apr_isdigit(expected[y + 1]));
1804            if (!expected[y])
1805                return 0;
1806            while (str[x]) {
1807                int ret;
1808                if ((ret = ap_proxy_strcmp_ematch(&str[x++], &expected[y])) != 1)
1809                    return ret;
1810            }
1811            return -1;
1812        }
1813        else if (!str[x]) {
1814            return -1;
1815        }
1816        else if (expected[y] == '\\' && !expected[++y]) {
1817            /* NUL is an invalid char! */ view japanese
1818            return -2;
1819        }
1820        if (str[x] != expected[y])
1821            return 1;
1822    }
1823    /* We got all the way through the worker path without a difference */ view japanese
1824    return 0;
1825}
1826
1827static int worker_matches(proxy_worker *worker,
1828                          const char *url, apr_size_t url_len,
1829                          apr_size_t min_match, apr_size_t *max_match,
1830                          unsigned int mask)
1831{
1832    apr_size_t name_len = strlen(worker->s->name_ex);
1833    if (name_len <= url_len
1834        && name_len > *max_match
1835        /* min_match is the length of the scheme://host part only of url,
1836         * so it's used as a fast path to avoid the match when url is too
1837         * small, but it's irrelevant when the worker host contains globs
1838         * (i.e. ->is_host_matchable).
1839         */ view japanese
1840        && (worker->s->is_name_matchable
1841            ? ((mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH)
1842               && (worker->s->is_host_matchable || name_len >= min_match)
1843               && !ap_proxy_strcmp_ematch(url, worker->s->name_ex))
1844            : ((mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_PREFIX)
1845               && (name_len >= min_match)
1846               && !strncmp(url, worker->s->name_ex, name_len)))) {
1847        *max_match = name_len;
1848        return 1;
1849    }
1850    return 0;
1851}
1852
1853PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_worker *) ap_proxy_get_worker_ex(apr_pool_t *p,
1854                                                     proxy_balancer *balancer,
1855                                                     proxy_server_conf *conf,
1856                                                     const char *url,
1857                                                     unsigned int mask)
1858{
1859    proxy_worker *max_worker = NULL;
1860    apr_size_t min_match, max_match = 0;
1861    apr_size_t url_len;
1862    const char *c;
1863    char *url_copy;
1864    int i;
1865
1866    if (!url) {
1867        return NULL;
1868    }
1869
1870    if (!(mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_NO_UDS)) {
1871        url = ap_proxy_de_socketfy(p, url);
1872        if (!url) {
1873            return NULL;
1874        }
1875    }
1876
1877    c = ap_strchr_c(url, ':');
1878    if (c == NULL || c[1] != '/' || c[2] != '/' || c[3] == '\0') {
1879        return NULL;
1880    }
1881
1882    url_len = strlen(url);
1883    url_copy = apr_pstrmemdup(p, url, url_len);
1884
1885    /* Default to lookup for both _PREFIX and _MATCH workers */ view japanese
1886    if (!(mask & (AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_PREFIX | AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH))) {
1887        mask |= AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_PREFIX | AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH;
1888    }
1889
1890    /*
1891     * We need to find the start of the path and
1892     * therefore we know the length of the scheme://hostname/
1893     * part to we can force-lowercase everything up to
1894     * the start of the path.
1895     */ view japanese
1896    c = ap_strchr_c(c+3, '/');
1897    if (c) {
1898        char *pathstart;
1899        pathstart = url_copy + (c - url);
1900        *pathstart = '\0';
1901        ap_str_tolower(url_copy);
1902        min_match = strlen(url_copy);
1903        *pathstart = '/';
1904    }
1905    else {
1906        ap_str_tolower(url_copy);
1907        min_match = strlen(url_copy);
1908    }
1909
1910    /*
1911     * Do a "longest match" on the worker name to find the worker that
1912     * fits best to the URL, but keep in mind that we must have at least
1913     * a minimum matching of length min_match such that
1914     * scheme://hostname[:port] matches between worker and url.
1915     */ view japanese
1916    if (balancer) {
1917        proxy_worker **worker = (proxy_worker **)balancer->workers->elts;
1918        for (i = 0; i < balancer->workers->nelts; i++, worker++) {
1919            if (worker_matches(*worker, url_copy, url_len,
1920                               min_match, &max_match, mask)) {
1921                max_worker = *worker;
1922            }
1923        }
1924    }
1925    else {
1926        proxy_worker *worker = (proxy_worker *)conf->workers->elts;
1927        for (i = 0; i < conf->workers->nelts; i++, worker++) {
1928            if (worker_matches(worker, url_copy, url_len,
1929                               min_match, &max_match, mask)) {
1930                max_worker = worker;
1931            }
1932        }
1933    }
1934
1935    return max_worker;
1936}
1937
1938PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_worker *) ap_proxy_get_worker(apr_pool_t *p,
1939                                                  proxy_balancer *balancer,
1940                                                  proxy_server_conf *conf,
1941                                                  const char *url)
1942{
1943    return ap_proxy_get_worker_ex(p, balancer, conf, url, 0);
1944}
1945
1946/*
1947 * To create a worker from scratch first we define the
1948 * specifics of the worker; this is all local data.
1949 * We then allocate space for it if data needs to be
1950 * shared. This allows for dynamic addition during
1951 * config and runtime.
1952 */ view japanese
1953PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_define_worker_ex(apr_pool_t *p,
1954                                             proxy_worker **worker,
1955                                             proxy_balancer *balancer,
1956                                             proxy_server_conf *conf,
1957                                             const char *url,
1958                                             unsigned int mask)
1959{
1960    apr_status_t rv;
1961    proxy_worker_shared *wshared;
1962    const char *ptr = NULL, *sockpath = NULL, *pdollars = NULL;
1963    apr_port_t port_of_scheme;
1964    int address_not_reusable = 0;
1965    apr_uri_t uri;
1966
1967    /*
1968     * Look to see if we are using UDS:
1969     * require format: unix:/path/foo/bar.sock|http://ignored/path2/
1970     * This results in talking http to the socket at /path/foo/bar.sock
1971     */ view japanese
1972    if (!ap_cstr_casecmpn(url, "unix:", 5)
1973            && (ptr = ap_strchr_c(url + 5, '|'))) {
1974        rv = apr_uri_parse(p, apr_pstrmemdup(p, url, ptr - url), &uri);
1975        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
1976            sockpath = ap_runtime_dir_relative(p, uri.path);
1977            ptr++;    /* so we get the scheme for the uds */ view japanese
1978        }
1979        else {
1980            ptr = url;
1981        }
1982    }
1983    else {
1984        ptr = url;
1985    }
1986
1987    if (mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH) {
1988        /* apr_uri_parse() will accept the '$' sign anywhere in the URL but
1989         * in the :port part, and we don't want scheme://host:port$1$2/path
1990         * to fail (e.g. "ProxyPassMatch ^/(a|b)(/.*)? http://host:port$2").
1991         * So we trim all the $n from the :port and prepend them in uri.path
1992         * afterward for apr_uri_unparse() to restore the original URL below.
1993         * If a dollar substitution is found in the hostname[:port] part of
1994         * the URL, reusing address and connections in the same worker is not
1995         * possible (the current implementation of active connections cache
1996         * handles/assumes a single origin server:port per worker only), so
1997         * we set address_not_reusable here during parsing to take that into
1998         * account in the worker settings below.
1999         */ view japanese
2000#define IS_REF(x) (x[0] == '$' && apr_isdigit(x[1]))
2001        const char *pos = ap_strstr_c(ptr, "://");
2002        if (pos) {
2003            pos += 3;
2004            while (*pos && *pos != ':' && *pos != '/') {
2005                if (*pos == '$') {
2006                    address_not_reusable = 1;
2007                }
2008                pos++;
2009            }
2010            if (*pos == ':') {
2011                pos++;
2012                while (*pos && !IS_REF(pos) && *pos != '/') {
2013                    pos++;
2014                }
2015                if (IS_REF(pos)) {
2016                    struct iovec vec[2];
2017                    const char *path = pos + 2;
2018                    while (*path && *path != '/') {
2019                        path++;
2020                    }
2021                    pdollars = apr_pstrmemdup(p, pos, path - pos);
2022                    vec[0].iov_base = (void *)ptr;
2023                    vec[0].iov_len = pos - ptr;
2024                    vec[1].iov_base = (void *)path;
2025                    vec[1].iov_len = strlen(path);
2026                    ptr = apr_pstrcatv(p, vec, 2, NULL);
2027                    address_not_reusable = 1;
2028                }
2029            }
2030        }
2031#undef IS_REF
2032    }
2033
2034    /* Normalize the url (worker name) */ view japanese
2035    rv = apr_uri_parse(p, ptr, &uri);
2036    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2037        return apr_pstrcat(p, "Unable to parse URL: ", url, NULL);
2038    }
2039    if (!uri.scheme) {
2040        return apr_pstrcat(p, "URL must be absolute!: ", url, NULL);
2041    }
2042    if (!uri.hostname || !*uri.hostname) {
2043        if (sockpath) {
2044            /* allow for unix:/path|http: */ view japanese
2045            uri.hostname = "localhost";
2046        }
2047        else {
2048            return apr_pstrcat(p, "URL must be absolute!: ", url, NULL);
2049        }
2050    }
2051    else {
2052        ap_str_tolower(uri.hostname);
2053    }
2054    ap_str_tolower(uri.scheme);
2055    port_of_scheme = ap_proxy_port_of_scheme(uri.scheme);
2056    if (uri.port && uri.port == port_of_scheme) {
2057        uri.port = 0;
2058    }
2059    if (pdollars) {
2060        /* Restore/prepend pdollars into the path. */ view japanese
2061        uri.path = apr_pstrcat(p, pdollars, uri.path, NULL);
2062    }
2063    ptr = apr_uri_unparse(p, &uri, APR_URI_UNP_REVEALPASSWORD);
2064
2065    /*
2066     * Workers can be associated w/ balancers or on their
2067     * own; ie: the generic reverse-proxy or a worker
2068     * in a simple ProxyPass statement. eg:
2069     *
2070     *      ProxyPass / http://www.example.com
2071     *
2072     * in which case the worker goes in the conf slot.
2073     */ view japanese
2074    if (balancer) {
2075        proxy_worker **runtime;
2076        /* recall that we get a ptr to the ptr here */ view japanese
2077        runtime = apr_array_push(balancer->workers);
2078        *worker = *runtime = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(proxy_worker));   /* right to left baby */ view japanese
2079        /* we've updated the list of workers associated with
2080         * this balancer *locally* */ view japanese
2081        balancer->wupdated = apr_time_now();
2082    } else if (conf) {
2083        *worker = apr_array_push(conf->workers);
2084    } else {
2085        /* we need to allocate space here */ view japanese
2086        *worker = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(proxy_worker));
2087    }
2088    memset(*worker, 0, sizeof(proxy_worker));
2089    
2090    /* right here we just want to tuck away the worker info.
2091     * if called during config, we don't have shm setup yet,
2092     * so just note the info for later. */ view japanese
2093    if (mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MALLOCED)
2094        wshared = ap_malloc(sizeof(proxy_worker_shared));  /* will be freed ap_proxy_share_worker */ view japanese
2095    else
2096        wshared = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(proxy_worker_shared));
2097    memset(wshared, 0, sizeof(proxy_worker_shared));
2098
2099    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->name_ex, ptr) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2100        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(10366)
2101        "Alert! worker name (%s) too long; truncated to: %s", ptr, wshared->name_ex);
2102    }
2103    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->name, ptr) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2104        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(010118)
2105        "worker name (%s) too long; truncated for legacy modules that do not use "
2106        "proxy_worker_shared->name_ex: %s", ptr, wshared->name);
2107    }
2108    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->scheme, uri.scheme) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2109        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(010117)
2110        "Alert! worker scheme (%s) too long; truncated to: %s", uri.scheme, wshared->scheme);
2111    }
2112    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->hostname_ex, uri.hostname) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2113        return apr_psprintf(p, "worker hostname (%s) too long", uri.hostname);
2114    }
2115    if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->hostname, uri.hostname) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2116        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(010118)
2117        "worker hostname (%s) too long; truncated for legacy modules that do not use "
2118        "proxy_worker_shared->hostname_ex: %s", uri.hostname, wshared->hostname);
2119    }
2120    wshared->port = (uri.port) ? uri.port : port_of_scheme;
2121    wshared->flush_packets = flush_off;
2122    wshared->flush_wait = PROXY_FLUSH_WAIT;
2123    wshared->address_ttl = (address_not_reusable) ? 0 : -1;
2124    wshared->is_address_reusable = (address_not_reusable == 0);
2125    wshared->disablereuse = (address_not_reusable != 0);
2126    wshared->lbfactor = 100;
2127    wshared->passes = 1;
2128    wshared->fails = 1;
2129    wshared->interval = apr_time_from_sec(HCHECK_WATHCHDOG_DEFAULT_INTERVAL);
2130    wshared->smax = -1;
