2131    wshared->hash.def = ap_proxy_hashfunc(wshared->name_ex, PROXY_HASHFUNC_DEFAULT);
2132    wshared->hash.fnv = ap_proxy_hashfunc(wshared->name_ex, PROXY_HASHFUNC_FNV);
2133    wshared->was_malloced = (mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MALLOCED) != 0;
2134    if (mask & AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH) {
2135        wshared->is_name_matchable = 1;
2136        wshared->is_host_matchable = (address_not_reusable != 0);
2137
2138        /* Before AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH (< 2.4.47), a regex worker with
2139         * dollar substitution was never matched against any actual URL, thus
2140         * the requests fell through the generic worker. Now if a ProyPassMatch
2141         * matches, a worker (and its parameters) is always used to determine
2142         * the properties of the connection with the origin server. So for
2143         * instance the same "timeout=" will be enforced for all the requests
2144         * matched by the same ProyPassMatch worker, which is an improvement
2145         * compared to the global/vhost [Proxy]Timeout applied by the generic
2146         * worker. Likewise, address and connection reuse is the default for
2147         * a ProyPassMatch worker with no dollar substitution, just like a
2148         * "normal" worker. However to avoid DNS and connection reuse compat
2149         * issues, connection reuse is disabled by default if there is any
2150         * substitution in the uri-path (an explicit enablereuse=on can still
2151         * opt-in), and reuse is even disabled definitively for substitutions
2152         * happening in the hostname[:port] (is_address_reusable was unset
2153         * above so it will prevent enablereuse=on to apply anyway).
2154         */ view japanese
2155        if (ap_strchr_c(wshared->name, '$')) {
2156            wshared->disablereuse = 1;
2157        }
2158    }
2159    if (sockpath) {
2160        if (PROXY_STRNCPY(wshared->uds_path, sockpath) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2161            return apr_psprintf(p, "worker uds path (%s) too long", sockpath);
2162        }
2163
2164    }
2165    else {
2166        *wshared->uds_path = '\0';
2167    }
2168    if (!balancer) {
2169        wshared->status |= PROXY_WORKER_IGNORE_ERRORS;
2170    }
2171
2172    (*worker)->hash = wshared->hash;
2173    (*worker)->context = NULL;
2174    (*worker)->cp = NULL;
2175    (*worker)->balancer = balancer;
2176    (*worker)->s = wshared;
2177
2178    return NULL;
2179}
2180
2181PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_define_worker(apr_pool_t *p,
2182                                             proxy_worker **worker,
2183                                             proxy_balancer *balancer,
2184                                             proxy_server_conf *conf,
2185                                             const char *url,
2186                                             int do_malloc)
2187{
2188    return ap_proxy_define_worker_ex(p, worker, balancer, conf, url,
2189                                     AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_PREFIX |
2190                                     (do_malloc ? AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MALLOCED
2191                                                : 0));
2192}
2193
2194/* DEPRECATED */
2195PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_define_match_worker(apr_pool_t *p,
2196                                             proxy_worker **worker,
2197                                             proxy_balancer *balancer,
2198                                             proxy_server_conf *conf,
2199                                             const char *url,
2200                                             int do_malloc)
2201{
2202    return ap_proxy_define_worker_ex(p, worker, balancer, conf, url,
2203                                     AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MATCH |
2204                                     (do_malloc ? AP_PROXY_WORKER_IS_MALLOCED
2205                                                : 0));
2206}
2207
2208/*
2209 * Create an already defined worker and free up memory
2210 */ view japanese
2211PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_share_worker(proxy_worker *worker, proxy_worker_shared *shm,
2212                                                  int i)
2213{
2214    char *action = "copying";
2215    if (!shm || !worker->s)
2216        return APR_EINVAL;
2217
2218    if ((worker->s->hash.def != shm->hash.def) ||
2219        (worker->s->hash.fnv != shm->hash.fnv)) {
2220        memcpy(shm, worker->s, sizeof(proxy_worker_shared));
2221        if (worker->s->was_malloced)
2222            free(worker->s); /* was malloced in ap_proxy_define_worker */ view japanese
2223    } else {
2224        action = "re-using";
2225    }
2226    worker->s = shm;
2227    worker->s->index = i;
2228
2229    if (APLOGdebug(ap_server_conf)) {
2230        apr_pool_t *pool;
2231        apr_pool_create(&pool, ap_server_conf->process->pool);
2232        apr_pool_tag(pool, "proxy_worker_name");
2233        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, ap_server_conf, APLOGNO(02338)
2234                     "%s shm[%d] (0x%pp) for worker: %s", action, i, (void *)shm,
2235                     ap_proxy_worker_name(pool, worker));
2236        if (pool) {
2237            apr_pool_destroy(pool);
2238        }
2239    }
2240    return APR_SUCCESS;
2241}
2242
2243PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_initialize_worker(proxy_worker *worker, server_rec *s, apr_pool_t *p)
2244{
2245    APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(http2_get_num_workers) *get_h2_num_workers;
2246    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
2247    int max_threads, minw, maxw;
2248
2249    if (worker->s->status & PROXY_WORKER_INITIALIZED) {
2250        /* The worker is already initialized */ view japanese
2251        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00924)
2252                     "worker %s shared already initialized",
2253                     ap_proxy_worker_name(p, worker));
2254    }
2255    else {
2256        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00925)
2257                     "initializing worker %s shared",
2258                     ap_proxy_worker_name(p, worker));
2259        /* Set default parameters */ view japanese
2260        if (!worker->s->retry_set) {
2261            worker->s->retry = apr_time_from_sec(PROXY_WORKER_DEFAULT_RETRY);
2262        }
2263        /* Consistently set address and connection reusabilty: when reuse
2264         * is disabled by configuration, or when the address is known already
2265         * to not be reusable for this worker (in any case, thus ignore/force
2266         * DisableReuse).
2267         */ view japanese
2268        if (!worker->s->address_ttl || (!worker->s->address_ttl_set
2269                                        && worker->s->disablereuse)) {
2270            worker->s->is_address_reusable = 0;
2271        }
2272        if (!worker->s->is_address_reusable && !worker->s->disablereuse) {
2273            /* Explicit enablereuse=on can't work in this case, warn user. */ view japanese
2274            if (worker->s->disablereuse_set) {
2275                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, s, APLOGNO(10400)
2276                             "enablereuse/disablereuse ignored for worker %s",
2277                             ap_proxy_worker_name(p, worker));
2278            }
2279            worker->s->disablereuse = 1;
2280        }
2281
2282        /*
2283         * When mod_http2 is loaded we might have more threads since it has
2284         * its own pool of processing threads.
2285         */ view japanese
2286        ap_mpm_query(AP_MPMQ_MAX_THREADS, &max_threads);
2287        get_h2_num_workers = APR_RETRIEVE_OPTIONAL_FN(http2_get_num_workers);
2288        if (get_h2_num_workers) {
2289            get_h2_num_workers(s, &minw, &maxw);
2290            /* So now the max is:
2291             *   max_threads-1 threads for HTTP/1 each requiring one connection
2292             *   + one thread for HTTP/2 requiring maxw connections
2293             */ view japanese
2294            max_threads = max_threads - 1 + maxw;
2295        }
2296        if (max_threads > 1) {
2297            /* Default hmax is max_threads to scale with the load and never
2298             * wait for an idle connection to proceed.
2299             */ view japanese
2300            if (worker->s->hmax == 0) {
2301                worker->s->hmax = max_threads;
2302            }
2303            if (worker->s->smax == -1 || worker->s->smax > worker->s->hmax) {
2304                worker->s->smax = worker->s->hmax;
2305            }
2306            /* Set min to be lower than smax */ view japanese
2307            if (worker->s->min > worker->s->smax) {
2308                worker->s->min = worker->s->smax;
2309            }
2310        }
2311        else {
2312            /* This will suppress the apr_reslist creation */ view japanese
2313            worker->s->min = worker->s->smax = worker->s->hmax = 0;
2314        }
2315    }
2316
2317    /* What if local is init'ed and shm isn't?? Even possible? */ view japanese
2318    if (worker->local_status & PROXY_WORKER_INITIALIZED) {
2319        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00926)
2320                     "worker %s local already initialized",
2321                     ap_proxy_worker_name(p, worker));
2322    }
2323    else {
2324        apr_global_mutex_lock(proxy_mutex);
2325        /* Check again after we got the lock if we are still uninitialized */ view japanese
2326        if (!(AP_VOLATILIZE_T(unsigned int, worker->local_status) & PROXY_WORKER_INITIALIZED)) {
2327            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00927)
2328                         "initializing worker %s local",
2329                         ap_proxy_worker_name(p, worker));
2330            /* Now init local worker data */ view japanese
2331#if APR_HAS_THREADS
2332            if (worker->tmutex == NULL) {
2333                rv = apr_thread_mutex_create(&(worker->tmutex), APR_THREAD_MUTEX_DEFAULT, p);
2334                if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2335                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00928)
2336                                 "can not create worker thread mutex");
2337                    apr_global_mutex_unlock(proxy_mutex);
2338                    return rv;
2339                }
2340            }
2341#endif
2342            if (worker->cp == NULL)
2343                init_conn_pool(p, worker, s);
2344            if (worker->cp == NULL) {
2345                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00929)
2346                             "can not create connection pool");
2347                apr_global_mutex_unlock(proxy_mutex);
2348                return APR_EGENERAL;
2349            }
2350
2351            if (worker->s->hmax) {
2352                rv = apr_reslist_create(&(worker->cp->res),
2353                                        worker->s->min, worker->s->smax,
2354                                        worker->s->hmax, worker->s->ttl,
2355                                        connection_constructor, connection_destructor,
2356                                        worker, worker->cp->pool);
2357
2358                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00930)
2359                    "initialized pool in child %" APR_PID_T_FMT " for (%s:%d) min=%d max=%d smax=%d",
2360                     getpid(), worker->s->hostname_ex, (int)worker->s->port,
2361                     worker->s->min, worker->s->hmax, worker->s->smax);
2362
2363                /* Set the acquire timeout */ view japanese
2364                if (rv == APR_SUCCESS && worker->s->acquire_set) {
2365                    apr_reslist_timeout_set(worker->cp->res, worker->s->acquire);
2366                }
2367
2368            }
2369            else {
2370                void *conn;
2371
2372                rv = connection_constructor(&conn, worker, worker->cp->pool);
2373                worker->cp->conn = conn;
2374
2375                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, s, APLOGNO(00931)
2376                     "initialized single connection worker in child %" APR_PID_T_FMT " for (%s:%d)",
2377                     getpid(), worker->s->hostname_ex,
2378                     (int)worker->s->port);
2379            }
2380            if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
2381                worker->local_status |= (PROXY_WORKER_INITIALIZED);
2382            }
2383        }
2384        apr_global_mutex_unlock(proxy_mutex);
2385
2386    }
2387    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
2388        worker->s->status |= (PROXY_WORKER_INITIALIZED);
2389    }
2390    return rv;
2391}
2392
2393static int ap_proxy_retry_worker(const char *proxy_function, proxy_worker *worker,
2394        server_rec *s)
2395{
2396    if (worker->s->status & PROXY_WORKER_IN_ERROR) {
2397        if (PROXY_WORKER_IS(worker, PROXY_WORKER_STOPPED)) {
2398            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(3305)
2399                         "%s: Won't retry worker (%s:%d): stopped",
2400                         proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2401                         (int)worker->s->port);
2402            return DECLINED;
2403        }
2404        if ((worker->s->status & PROXY_WORKER_IGNORE_ERRORS)
2405            || apr_time_now() > worker->s->error_time + worker->s->retry) {
2406            ++worker->s->retries;
2407            worker->s->status &= ~PROXY_WORKER_IN_ERROR;
2408            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00932)
2409                         "%s: worker for (%s:%d) has been marked for retry",
2410                         proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2411                         (int)worker->s->port);
2412            return OK;
2413        }
2414        else {
2415            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00933)
2416                         "%s: too soon to retry worker for (%s:%d)",
2417                         proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2418                         (int)worker->s->port);
2419            return DECLINED;
2420        }
2421    }
2422    else {
2423        return OK;
2424    }
2425}
2426
2427/*
2428 * In the case of the reverse proxy, we need to see if we
2429 * were passed a UDS url (eg: from mod_proxy) and adjust uds_path
2430 * as required.  
2431 */ view japanese
2432static int fixup_uds_filename(request_rec *r) 
2433{
2434    char *uds_url = r->filename + 6, *origin_url;
2435
2436    if (!strncmp(r->filename, "proxy:", 6) &&
2437            !ap_cstr_casecmpn(uds_url, "unix:", 5) &&
2438            (origin_url = ap_strchr(uds_url + 5, '|'))) {
2439        char *uds_path = NULL, *col;
2440        apr_uri_t urisock;
2441        apr_status_t rv;
2442
2443        *origin_url = '\0';
2444        rv = apr_uri_parse(r->pool, uds_url, &urisock);
2445        *origin_url++ = '|';
2446
2447        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS && urisock.path && (!urisock.hostname
2448                                                  || !urisock.hostname[0])) {
2449            uds_path = ap_runtime_dir_relative(r->pool, urisock.path);
2450        }
2451        if (!uds_path || !(col = ap_strchr(origin_url, ':'))) {
2452            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10292)
2453                    "Invalid proxy UDS filename (%s)", r->filename);
2454            apr_table_unset(r->notes, "uds_path");
2455            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2456        }
2457        apr_table_setn(r->notes, "uds_path", uds_path);
2458
2459        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
2460                "*: fixup UDS from %s: %s (%s)",
2461                r->filename, origin_url, uds_path);
2462
2463        /* The hostname part of the URL is not mandated for UDS though
2464         * the canon_handler hooks will require it. ProxyPass URLs are
2465         * fixed at load time by adding "localhost" automatically in the
2466         * worker URL, but SetHandler "proxy:unix:/udspath|scheme:[//]"
2467         * URLs are not so we have to fix it here the same way.
2468         */ view japanese
2469        if (!col[1]) {
2470            /* origin_url is "scheme:" */ view japanese
2471            r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "proxy:",
2472                                      origin_url, "//localhost",
2473                                      NULL);
2474        }
2475        /* For a SetHandler "proxy:..." in a <Location "/path">, the "/path"
2476         * is appended to r->filename, hence the below origin_url cases too:
2477         */ view japanese
2478        else if (col[1] == '/' && (col[2] != '/'    /* "scheme:/path" */ view japanese
2479                                   || col[3] == '/' /* "scheme:///path" */ view japanese
2480                                   || !col[3])) {   /* "scheme://" */ view japanese
2481            char *scheme = origin_url;
2482            *col = '\0'; /* nul terminate scheme */ view japanese
2483            if (col[2] != '/') {
2484                origin_url = col + 1;
2485            }
2486            else {
2487                origin_url = col + 3;
2488            }
2489            r->filename = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "proxy:",
2490                                      scheme, "://localhost", view japanese
2491                                      origin_url, NULL);
2492        }
2493        else {
2494            /* origin_url is normal "scheme://host/path", can overwrite
2495             * the UDS part of r->filename in place.
2496             */ view japanese
2497            memmove(uds_url, origin_url, strlen(origin_url) + 1);
2498        }
2499        return OK;
2500    }
2501
2502    apr_table_unset(r->notes, "uds_path");
2503    return DECLINED;
2504}
2505
2506/* Deprecated (unused upstream) */ view japanese
2507PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_fixup_uds_filename(request_rec *r)
2508{
2509    return fixup_uds_filename(r);
2510}
2511
2512PROXY_DECLARE(const char *) ap_proxy_interpolate(request_rec *r,
2513                                                 const char *str)
2514{
2515    /* Interpolate an env str in a configuration string
2516     * Syntax ${var} --> value_of(var)
2517     * Method: replace one var, and recurse on remainder of string
2518     * Nothing clever here, and crap like nested vars may do silly things
2519     * but we'll at least avoid sending the unwary into a loop
2520     */ view japanese
2521    const char *start;
2522    const char *end;
2523    const char *var;
2524    const char *val;
2525    const char *firstpart;
2526
2527    start = ap_strstr_c(str, "${");
2528    if (start == NULL) {
2529        return str;
2530    }
2531    end = ap_strchr_c(start+2, '}');
2532    if (end == NULL) {
2533        return str;
2534    }
2535    /* OK, this is syntax we want to interpolate.  Is there such a var ? */ view japanese
2536    var = apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool, start+2, end-(start+2));
2537    val = apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, var);
2538    firstpart = apr_pstrmemdup(r->pool, str, (start-str));
2539
2540    if (val == NULL) {
2541        return apr_pstrcat(r->pool, firstpart,
2542                           ap_proxy_interpolate(r, end+1), NULL);
2543    }
2544    else {
2545        return apr_pstrcat(r->pool, firstpart, val,
2546                           ap_proxy_interpolate(r, end+1), NULL);
2547    }
2548}
2549
2550static apr_array_header_t *proxy_vars(request_rec *r, apr_array_header_t *hdr)
2551{
2552    int i;
2553    apr_array_header_t *ret = apr_array_make(r->pool, hdr->nelts,
2554                                             sizeof (struct proxy_alias));
2555    struct proxy_alias *old = (struct proxy_alias *) hdr->elts;
2556
2557    for (i = 0; i < hdr->nelts; ++i) {
2558        struct proxy_alias *newcopy = apr_array_push(ret);
2559        newcopy->fake = (old[i].flags & PROXYPASS_INTERPOLATE)
2560                        ? ap_proxy_interpolate(r, old[i].fake) : old[i].fake;
2561        newcopy->real = (old[i].flags & PROXYPASS_INTERPOLATE)
2562                        ? ap_proxy_interpolate(r, old[i].real) : old[i].real;
2563    }
2564    return ret;
2565}
2566
2567PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_canon_url(request_rec *r)
2568{
2569    char *url, *p;
2570    int access_status;
2571    proxy_dir_conf *dconf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
2572                                                 &proxy_module);
2573
2574    if (!r->proxyreq || !r->filename || strncmp(r->filename, "proxy:", 6) != 0)
2575        return DECLINED;
2576
2577    /* Put the UDS path appart if any (and not already stripped) */ view japanese
2578    if (r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_REVERSE) {
2579        access_status = fixup_uds_filename(r);
2580        if (ap_is_HTTP_ERROR(access_status)) {
2581            return access_status;
2582        }
2583    }
2584
2585    /* Keep this after fixup_uds_filename() */ view japanese
2586    url = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->filename + 6);
2587
2588    if ((dconf->interpolate_env == 1) && (r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_REVERSE)) {
2589        /* create per-request copy of reverse proxy conf,
2590         * and interpolate vars in it
2591         */ view japanese
2592        proxy_req_conf *rconf = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(proxy_req_conf));
2593        ap_set_module_config(r->request_config, &proxy_module, rconf);
2594        rconf->raliases = proxy_vars(r, dconf->raliases);
2595        rconf->cookie_paths = proxy_vars(r, dconf->cookie_paths);
2596        rconf->cookie_domains = proxy_vars(r, dconf->cookie_domains);
2597    }
2598
2599    /* canonicalise each specific scheme */ view japanese
2600    if ((access_status = proxy_run_canon_handler(r, url))) {
2601        return access_status;
2602    }
2603
2604    p = strchr(url, ':');
2605    if (p == NULL || p == url)
2606        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2607
2608    return OK;      /* otherwise; we've done the best we can */ view japanese
2609}
2610
2611PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_pre_request(proxy_worker **worker,
2612                                        proxy_balancer **balancer,
2613                                        request_rec *r,
2614                                        proxy_server_conf *conf, char **url)
2615{
2616    int access_status;
2617
2618    access_status = proxy_run_pre_request(worker, balancer, r, conf, url);
2619    if (access_status == DECLINED && *balancer == NULL) {
2620        /* UDS path stripped from *url by proxy_fixup() already */ view japanese
2621        *worker = ap_proxy_get_worker_ex(r->pool, NULL, conf, *url,
2622                                         AP_PROXY_WORKER_NO_UDS);
2623        if (*worker) {
2624            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
2625                          "%s: found worker %s for %s",
2626                          (*worker)->s->scheme, (*worker)->s->name_ex, *url);
2627            access_status = OK;
2628        }
2629        else if (r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_PROXY) {
2630            if (conf->forward) {
2631                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
2632                              "*: found forward proxy worker for %s", *url);
2633                *worker = conf->forward;
2634                access_status = OK;
2635                /*
2636                 * The forward worker does not keep connections alive, so
2637                 * ensure that mod_proxy_http does the correct thing
2638                 * regarding the Connection header in the request.
2639                 */ view japanese
2640                apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, "proxy-nokeepalive", "1");
2641            }
2642        }
2643        else if (r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_REVERSE) {
2644            if (conf->reverse) {
2645                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
2646                              "*: using default reverse proxy worker for %s "
2647                              "(no keepalive)", *url);
2648                *worker = conf->reverse;
2649                access_status = OK;
2650                /*
2651                 * The reverse worker does not keep connections alive, so
2652                 * ensure that mod_proxy_http does the correct thing
2653                 * regarding the Connection header in the request.
2654                 */ view japanese
2655                apr_table_setn(r->subprocess_env, "proxy-nokeepalive", "1");
2656            }
2657        }
2658    }
2659    else if (access_status == DECLINED && *balancer != NULL) {
2660        /* All the workers are busy */ view japanese
2661        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00934)
2662                      "all workers are busy.  Unable to serve %s", *url);
2663        access_status = HTTP_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE;
2664    }
2665
2666    return access_status;
2667}
2668
2669PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_post_request(proxy_worker *worker,
2670                                         proxy_balancer *balancer,
2671                                         request_rec *r,
2672                                         proxy_server_conf *conf)
2673{
2674    int access_status = OK;
2675    if (balancer) {
2676        access_status = proxy_run_post_request(worker, balancer, r, conf);
2677        if (access_status == DECLINED) {
2678            access_status = OK; /* no post_request handler available */ view japanese
2679            /* TODO: recycle direct worker */ view japanese
2680        }
2681    }
2682
2683    return access_status;
2684}
2685
2686/* DEPRECATED */ view japanese
2687PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_connect_to_backend(apr_socket_t **newsock,
2688                                               const char *proxy_function,
2689                                               apr_sockaddr_t *backend_addr,
2690                                               const char *backend_name,
2691                                               proxy_server_conf *conf,
2692                                               request_rec *r)
2693{
2694    apr_status_t rv;
2695    int connected = 0;
2696    int loglevel;
2697
2698    while (backend_addr && !connected) {
2699        if ((rv = apr_socket_create(newsock, backend_addr->family,
2700                                    SOCK_STREAM, 0, r->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
2701            loglevel = backend_addr->next ? APLOG_DEBUG : APLOG_ERR;
2702            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, r, APLOGNO(00935)
2703                          "%s: error creating fam %d socket for target %s",
2704                          proxy_function, backend_addr->family, backend_name);
2705            /*
2706             * this could be an IPv6 address from the DNS but the
2707             * local machine won't give us an IPv6 socket; hopefully the
2708             * DNS returned an additional address to try
2709             */ view japanese
2710            backend_addr = backend_addr->next;
2711            continue;
2712        }
2713
2714        if (conf->recv_buffer_size > 0 &&
2715            (rv = apr_socket_opt_set(*newsock, APR_SO_RCVBUF,
2716                                     conf->recv_buffer_size))) {
2717            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00936)
2718                          "apr_socket_opt_set(SO_RCVBUF): Failed to set "
2719                          "ProxyReceiveBufferSize, using default");
2720        }
2721
2722        rv = apr_socket_opt_set(*newsock, APR_TCP_NODELAY, 1);
2723        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && rv != APR_ENOTIMPL) {
2724            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00937)
2725                          "apr_socket_opt_set(APR_TCP_NODELAY): "
2726                          "Failed to set");
2727        }
2728
2729        /* Set a timeout on the socket */
2730        if (conf->timeout_set) {
2731            apr_socket_timeout_set(*newsock, conf->timeout);
2732        }
2733        else {
2734            apr_socket_timeout_set(*newsock, r->server->timeout);
2735        }
2736
2737        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
2738                      "%s: fam %d socket created to connect to %s",
2739                      proxy_function, backend_addr->family, backend_name);
2740
2741        if (conf->source_address) {
2742            apr_sockaddr_t *local_addr;
2743            /* Make a copy since apr_socket_bind() could change
2744             * conf->source_address, which we don't want.
2745             */ view japanese
2746            local_addr = apr_pmemdup(r->pool, conf->source_address,
2747                                     sizeof(apr_sockaddr_t));
2748            local_addr->pool = r->pool;
2749            rv = apr_socket_bind(*newsock, local_addr);
2750            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2751                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(00938)
2752                              "%s: failed to bind socket to local address",
2753                              proxy_function);
2754            }
2755        }
2756
2757        /* make the connection out of the socket */
2758        rv = apr_socket_connect(*newsock, backend_addr);
2759
2760        /* if an error occurred, loop round and try again */
2761        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2762            apr_socket_close(*newsock);
2763            loglevel = backend_addr->next ? APLOG_DEBUG : APLOG_ERR;
2764            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, r, APLOGNO(00939)
2765                          "%s: attempt to connect to %pI (%s) failed",
2766                          proxy_function, backend_addr, backend_name);
2767            backend_addr = backend_addr->next;
2768            continue;
2769        }
2770        connected = 1;
2771    }
2772    return connected ? 0 : 1;
2773}
2774
2775PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_acquire_connection(const char *proxy_function,
2776                                               proxy_conn_rec **conn,
2777                                               proxy_worker *worker,
2778                                               server_rec *s)
2779{
2780    apr_status_t rv;
2781
2782    if (!PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
2783        /* Retry the worker */ view japanese
2784        ap_proxy_retry_worker(proxy_function, worker, s);
2785
2786        if (!PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
2787            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00940)
2788                         "%s: disabled connection for (%s:%d)",
2789                         proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2790                         (int)worker->s->port);
2791            return HTTP_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE;
2792        }
2793    }
2794
2795    if (worker->s->hmax && worker->cp->res) {
2796        rv = apr_reslist_acquire(worker->cp->res, (void **)conn);
2797    }
2798    else {
2799        /* create the new connection if the previous was destroyed */ view japanese
2800        if (!worker->cp->conn) {
2801            rv = connection_constructor((void **)conn, worker, worker->cp->pool);
2802        }
2803        else {
2804            *conn = worker->cp->conn;
2805            worker->cp->conn = NULL;
2806            rv = APR_SUCCESS;
2807        }
2808    }
2809
2810    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2811        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00941)
2812                     "%s: failed to acquire connection for (%s:%d)",
2813                     proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2814                     (int)worker->s->port);
2815        return HTTP_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE;
2816    }
2817    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00942)
2818                 "%s: has acquired connection for (%s:%d)",
2819                 proxy_function, worker->s->hostname_ex,
2820                 (int)worker->s->port);
2821
2822    (*conn)->worker = worker;
2823    (*conn)->inreslist = 0;
2824
2825    return OK;
2826}
2827
2828PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_release_connection(const char *proxy_function,
2829                                               proxy_conn_rec *conn,
2830                                               server_rec *s)
2831{
2832    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00943)
2833                "%s: has released connection for (%s:%d)",
2834                proxy_function, conn->worker->s->hostname_ex,
2835                (int)conn->worker->s->port);
2836    connection_cleanup(conn);
2837
2838    return OK;
2839}
2840
2841static APR_INLINE void proxy_address_inc(proxy_address *address)
2842{
2843    apr_uint32_t old = apr_atomic_inc32(&address->refcount);
2844    ap_assert(old > 0 && old < APR_UINT32_MAX);
2845}
2846
2847static APR_INLINE void proxy_address_dec(proxy_address *address)
2848{
2849    /* Use _add32(, -1) since _dec32()'s returned value does not help */ view japanese
2850    apr_uint32_t old = apr_atomic_add32(&address->refcount, -1);
2851    ap_assert(old > 0);
2852    if (old == 1) {
2853        apr_pool_destroy(address->addr->pool);
2854    }
2855}
2856
2857static apr_status_t proxy_address_cleanup(void *address)
2858{
2859    proxy_address_dec(address);
2860    return APR_SUCCESS;
2861}
2862
2863static APR_INLINE proxy_address *worker_address_get(proxy_worker *worker)
2864{
2865    /* No _readptr() so let's _casptr(, NULL, NULL) instead */ view japanese
2866    return apr_atomic_casptr((void *)&worker->address, NULL, NULL);
2867}
2868
2869/* XXX: Call when PROXY_THREAD_LOCK()ed only! */ view japanese
2870static APR_INLINE void worker_address_set(proxy_worker *worker,
2871                                          proxy_address *to)
2872{
2873    proxy_address *old = apr_atomic_xchgptr((void *)&worker->address, to);
2874    if (old && old != to) {
2875        proxy_address_dec(old);
2876    }
2877}
2878
2879static apr_status_t worker_address_resolve(proxy_worker *worker,
2880                                           apr_sockaddr_t **paddr,
2881                                           const char *hostname,
2882                                           apr_port_t hostport,
2883                                           const char *proxy_function,
2884                                           request_rec *r, server_rec *s)
2885{
2886    apr_status_t rv;
2887    apr_pool_t *pool = NULL;
2888
2889    apr_pool_create(&pool, worker->cp->dns_pool);
2890    rv = apr_sockaddr_info_get(paddr, hostname, APR_UNSPEC,
2891                               hostport, 0, pool);
2892    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2893        if (r && !s) {
2894            proxyerror_core(r, HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
2895                            apr_pstrcat(pool,
2896                                        "DNS lookup failure for: ",
2897                                        hostname, NULL),
2898                            rv);
2899        }
2900        else if (r) {
2901            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(10477)
2902                          "%s: resolving worker %s address",
2903                          proxy_function, hostname);
2904        }
2905        else {
2906            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, s, APLOGNO(10478)
2907                         "%s: resolving worker %s address",
2908                         proxy_function, hostname);
2909        }
2910        apr_pool_destroy(pool);
2911        return rv;
2912    }
2913
2914    if (r ? APLOGrdebug(r) : APLOGdebug(s)) {
2915        char *addrs = NULL;
2916        apr_sockaddr_t *addr = *paddr;
2917        for (; addr; addr = addr->next) {
2918            addrs = apr_psprintf(pool, "%s%s%pI",
2919                                 addrs ? addrs : "",
2920                                 addrs ? ", " : "",
2921                                 addr);
2922        }
2923        if (r) {
2924            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(10479)
2925                          "%s: %s resolved to %s",
2926                          proxy_function, hostname, addrs);
2927        }
2928        else {
2929            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(10480)
2930                         "%s: %s resolved to %s",
2931                         proxy_function, hostname, addrs);
2932        }
2933    }
2934
2935    return APR_SUCCESS;
2936}
2937
2938static int proxy_addrs_equal(const apr_sockaddr_t *addr1,
2939                             const apr_sockaddr_t *addr2)
2940{
2941    const apr_sockaddr_t *base2 = addr2, *pos2;
2942    while (addr1 && addr2) {
2943        for (pos2 = base2; pos2; pos2 = pos2->next) {
2944            if (apr_sockaddr_equal(pos2, addr1)) {
2945                break;
2946            }
2947        }
2948        if (!pos2) {
2949            return 0;
2950        }
2951        addr1 = addr1->next;
2952        addr2 = addr2->next;
2953    }
2954    if (addr1 || addr2) {
2955        return 0;
2956    }
2957    return 1;
2958}
2959
2960PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_determine_address(const char *proxy_function,
2961                                                       proxy_conn_rec *conn,
2962                                                       const char *hostname,
2963                                                       apr_port_t hostport,
2964                                                       unsigned int flags,
2965                                                       request_rec *r,
2966                                                       server_rec *s)
2967{
2968    proxy_worker *worker = conn->worker;
2969    apr_status_t rv;
2970
2971    /*
2972     * Worker can have the single constant backend adress.
2973     * The single DNS lookup is used once per worker.
2974     * If dynamic change is needed then set the addr to NULL
2975     * inside dynamic config to force the lookup.
2976     * The worker's addressTTL parameter may also be configured
2977     * to perform the DNS lookups only when the TTL expires,
2978     * or each time if that TTL is zero.
2979     */ view japanese
2980    if (!worker->s->is_address_reusable) {
2981        conn->hostname = apr_pstrdup(conn->pool, hostname);
2982        conn->port = hostport;
2983
2984        rv = apr_sockaddr_info_get(&conn->addr, hostname, APR_UNSPEC,
2985                                   hostport, 0, conn->pool);
2986        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
2987            if (r && !s) {
2988                proxyerror_core(r, HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
2989                                apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "DNS lookup failure for: ",
2990                                            hostname, NULL), rv);
2991            }
2992            else if (r) {
2993                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(10475)
2994                              "%s: resolving backend %s address",
2995                              proxy_function, hostname);
2996            }
2997            else {
2998                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, s, APLOGNO(10476)
2999                              "%s: resolving backend %s address",
3000                              proxy_function, hostname);
3001            }
3002            return rv;
3003        }
3004    }
3005    else {
3006        apr_sockaddr_t *addr = NULL;
3007        proxy_address *address = NULL;
3008        apr_int32_t ttl = worker->s->address_ttl;
3009        apr_uint32_t now = 0;
3010
3011        if (flags & PROXY_DETERMINE_ADDRESS_CHECK) {
3012            /* The caller wants to check if the address changed, return
3013             * APR_EEXIST if not, otherwise fall through to update the
3014             * worker's for everyone to switch.
3015             */ view japanese
3016            if (!conn->addr) {
3017                /* Need something to compare with */ view japanese
3018                return APR_EINVAL;
3019            }
3020            rv = worker_address_resolve(worker, &addr,
3021                                        hostname, hostport,
3022                                        proxy_function, r, s);
3023            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3024                return rv;
3025            }
3026            if (proxy_addrs_equal(conn->addr, addr)) {
3027                apr_pool_destroy(addr->pool);
3028                return APR_EEXIST;
3029            }
3030        }
3031
3032        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(ttl != 0);
3033        if (ttl > 0) {
3034            /* TODO: use a monotonic clock here */ view japanese
3035            now = apr_time_sec(apr_time_now() - *proxy_start_time);
3036        }
3037
3038        /* Addresses are refcounted, destroyed when their refcount reaches 0.
3039         *
3040         * One ref is taken by worker->address as the worker's current/latest
3041         * address, it's dropped when that address expires/changes (see below).
3042         * The other refs are taken by the connections when using/switching to
3043         * the current worker address (also below), they are dropped when the
3044         * conns are destroyed (by the reslist though it should never happen
3045         * if hmax is greater than the number of threads) OR for an expired
3046         * conn->address when it's replaced by the new worker->address below.
3047         *
3048         * Dereferencing worker->address requires holding the worker mutex or
3049         * some concurrent connection processing might change/destroy it at any
3050         * time. So only conn->address is safe to dereference anywhere (unless
3051         * NULL..) since it has at least the lifetime of the connection.
3052         */ view japanese
3053        if (!addr) {
3054            address = worker_address_get(worker);
3055        }
3056        if (!address
3057            || conn->address != address
3058            || apr_atomic_read32(&address->expiry) <= now) {
3059            PROXY_THREAD_LOCK(worker);
3060
3061            /* Re-check while locked, might be a new address already */ view japanese
3062            if (!addr) {
3063                address = worker_address_get(worker);
3064            }
3065            if (!address || apr_atomic_read32(&address->expiry) <= now) {
3066                if (!addr) {
3067                    rv = worker_address_resolve(worker, &addr,
3068                                                hostname, hostport,
3069                                                proxy_function, r, s);
3070                    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3071                        PROXY_THREAD_UNLOCK(worker);
3072                        return rv;
3073                    }
3074
3075                    /* Recompute "now" should the DNS be slow
3076                     * TODO: use a monotonic clock here
3077                     */ view japanese
3078                    now = apr_time_sec(apr_time_now() - *proxy_start_time);
3079                }
3080
3081                address = apr_pcalloc(addr->pool, sizeof(*address));
3082                address->hostname = apr_pstrdup(addr->pool, hostname);
3083                address->hostport = hostport;
3084                address->addr = addr;
3085
3086                if (ttl > 0) {
3087                    /* We keep each worker's expiry date shared accross all the
3088                     * children so that they update their address at the same
3089                     * time, regardless of whether a specific child forced an
3090                     * address to expire at some point (for connect() issues).
3091                     */ view japanese
3092                    address->expiry = apr_atomic_read32(&worker->s->address_expiry);
3093                    if (address->expiry <= now) {
3094                        apr_uint32_t prev, next = (now + ttl) - (now % ttl);
3095                        do {
3096                            prev = apr_atomic_cas32(&worker->s->address_expiry,
3097                                                    next, address->expiry);
3098                            if (prev == address->expiry) {
3099                                address->expiry = next;
3100                                break;
3101                            }
3102                            address->expiry = prev;
3103                        } while (prev <= now);
3104                    }
3105                }
3106                else {
3107                    /* Never expires */ view japanese
3108                    address->expiry = APR_UINT32_MAX;
3109                }
3110
3111                /* One ref is for worker->address in any case */ view japanese
3112                if (worker->address || worker->cp->addr) {
3113                    apr_atomic_set32(&address->refcount, 1);
3114                }
3115                else {
3116                    /* Set worker->cp->addr once for compat with third-party
3117                     * modules. This addr never changed before and can't change
3118                     * underneath users now because of some TTL configuration.
3119                     * So we take one more ref for worker->cp->addr to remain
3120                     * allocated forever (though it might not be up to date..).
3121                     * Modules should use conn->addr instead of worker->cp-addr
3122                     * to get the actual address used by each conn, determined
3123                     * at connect() time.
3124                     */ view japanese
3125                    apr_atomic_set32(&address->refcount, 2);
3126                    worker->cp->addr = address->addr;
3127                }
3128
3129                /* Publish the changes. The old worker address (if any) is no
3130                 * longer used by this worker, it will be destroyed now if the
3131                 * worker is the last user (refcount == 1) or by the last conn
3132                 * using it (refcount > 1).
3133                 */ view japanese
3134                worker_address_set(worker, address);
3135            }
3136
3137            /* Take the ref for conn->address (before dropping the mutex so to
3138             * let no chance for this address be killed before it's used!)
3139             */ view japanese
3140            proxy_address_inc(address);
3141
3142            PROXY_THREAD_UNLOCK(worker);
3143
3144            /* Release the old conn address */ view japanese
3145            if (conn->address) {
3146                /* On Windows and OS/2, apr_socket_connect() called from
3147                 * ap_proxy_connect_backend() does a simple pointer copy of
3148                 * its given conn->addr[->next] into conn->sock->remote_addr.
3149                 * Thus conn->addr cannot be freed if the conn->sock should be
3150                 * kept alive (same new and old addresses) and the old address
3151                 * is still in conn->sock->remote_addr. In this case we rather
3152                 * delay the release of the old address by moving the cleanup
3153                 * to conn->scpool such that it runs when the socket is closed.
3154                 * In any other case, including other platforms, just release
3155                 * the old address now since conn->sock->remote_addr is either
3156                 * obsolete (socket forcibly closed) or a copy on conn->scpool
3157                 * already (not a dangling pointer).
3158                 */ view japanese
3159                int keep_addr_alive = 0,
3160                    keep_conn_alive = (conn->sock && conn->addr &&
3161                                       proxy_addrs_equal(conn->addr,
3162                                                         address->addr));
3163                if (keep_conn_alive) {
3164#if defined(WIN32) || defined(OS2)
3165                    apr_sockaddr_t *remote_addr = NULL;
3166                    apr_socket_addr_get(&remote_addr, APR_REMOTE, conn->sock);
3167                    for (addr = conn->addr; addr; addr = addr->next) {
3168                        if (addr == remote_addr) {
3169                            keep_addr_alive = 1;
3170                            break;
3171                        }
3172                    }
3173#else
3174                    /* Nothing to do, keep_addr_alive = 0 */ view japanese
3175#endif
3176                }
3177                else if (conn->sock && (r ? APLOGrdebug(r) : APLOGdebug(s))) {
3178                    apr_sockaddr_t *local_addr = NULL;
3179                    apr_sockaddr_t *remote_addr = NULL;
3180                    apr_socket_addr_get(&local_addr, APR_LOCAL, conn->sock);
3181                    apr_socket_addr_get(&remote_addr, APR_REMOTE, conn->sock);
3182                    if (r) {
3183                        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(10481)
3184                                      "%s: closing connection to %s (%pI<>%pI) on "
3185                                      "address change", proxy_function, hostname,
3186                                      local_addr, remote_addr);
3187                    }
3188                    else {
3189                        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(10482)
3190                                     "%s: closing connection to %s (%pI<>%pI) on "
3191                                     "address change", proxy_function, hostname,
3192                                     local_addr, remote_addr);
3193                    }
3194                }
3195                if (keep_addr_alive) {
3196                    apr_pool_cleanup_kill(conn->pool, conn->address,
3197                                          proxy_address_cleanup);
3198                    apr_pool_cleanup_register(conn->scpool, conn->address,
3199                                              proxy_address_cleanup,
3200                                              apr_pool_cleanup_null);
3201                }
3202                else {
3203                    apr_pool_cleanup_run(conn->pool, conn->address,
3204                                         proxy_address_cleanup);
3205                    if (!keep_conn_alive) {
3206                        conn_cleanup(conn);
3207                    }
3208                }
3209            }
3210
3211            /* Use the new address */ view japanese
3212            apr_pool_cleanup_register(conn->pool, address,
3213                                      proxy_address_cleanup,
3214                                      apr_pool_cleanup_null);
3215            conn->address = address;
3216            conn->hostname = address->hostname;
3217            conn->port = address->hostport;
3218            conn->addr = address->addr;
3219        }
3220    }
3221
3222    return APR_SUCCESS;
3223}
3224
3225PROXY_DECLARE(int)
3226ap_proxy_determine_connection(apr_pool_t *p, request_rec *r,
3227                              proxy_server_conf *conf,
3228                              proxy_worker *worker,
3229                              proxy_conn_rec *conn,
3230                              apr_uri_t *uri,
3231                              char **url,
3232                              const char *proxyname,
3233                              apr_port_t proxyport,
3234                              char *server_portstr,
3235                              int server_portstr_size)
3236{
3237    int server_port;
3238    const char *uds_path;
3239
3240    /*
3241     * Break up the URL to determine the host to connect to
3242     */ view japanese
3243
3244    /* we break the URL into host, port, uri */ view japanese
3245    if (APR_SUCCESS != apr_uri_parse(p, *url, uri)) {
3246        return ap_proxyerror(r, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST,
3247                             apr_pstrcat(p,"URI cannot be parsed: ", *url,
3248                                         NULL));
3249    }
3250
3251    if (!uri->hostname) {
3252        return ap_proxyerror(r, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST,
3253                             apr_pstrcat(p,"URI has no hostname: ", *url,
3254                                         NULL));
3255    }
3256
3257    if (!uri->port) {
3258        uri->port = ap_proxy_port_of_scheme(uri->scheme);
3259    }
3260
3261    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00944)
3262                 "connecting %s to %s:%d", *url, uri->hostname, uri->port);
3263
3264    /* Close a possible existing socket if we are told to do so */ view japanese
3265    if (conn->close) {
3266        socket_cleanup(conn);
3267    }
3268
3269    /*
3270     * allocate these out of the specified connection pool
3271     * The scheme handler decides if this is permanent or
3272     * short living pool.
3273     */ view japanese
3274    /* Unless we are connecting the backend via a (forward Proxy)Remote, we
3275     * have to use the original form of the URI (non absolute), but this is
3276     * also the case via a remote proxy using the CONNECT method since the
3277     * original request (and URI) is to be embedded in the body.
3278     */ view japanese
3279    if (!proxyname || conn->is_ssl) {
3280        *url = apr_pstrcat(p, uri->path, uri->query ? "?" : "",
3281                           uri->query ? uri->query : "",
3282                           uri->fragment ? "#" : "",
3283                           uri->fragment ? uri->fragment : "", NULL);
3284    }
3285    /*
3286     * Figure out if our passed in proxy_conn_rec has a usable
3287     * address cached.
3288     *
3289     * TODO: Handle this much better... 
3290     *
3291     * XXX: If generic workers are ever address-reusable, we need 
3292     *      to check host and port on the conn and be careful about
3293     *      spilling the cached addr from the worker.
3294     */ view japanese
3295    uds_path = (*worker->s->uds_path
3296                ? worker->s->uds_path
3297                : apr_table_get(r->notes, "uds_path"));
3298    if (uds_path) {
3299        if (!conn->uds_path || strcmp(conn->uds_path, uds_path) != 0) {
3300            apr_pool_t *pool = conn->pool;
3301            if (conn->uds_path) {
3302                conn_cleanup(conn);
3303                if (!conn->uds_pool) {
3304                    apr_pool_create(&conn->uds_pool, worker->cp->dns_pool);
3305                }
3306                pool = conn->uds_pool;
3307            }
3308            /*
3309             * In UDS cases, some structs are NULL. Protect from de-refs
3310             * and provide info for logging at the same time.
3311             */ view japanese
3312#if APR_HAVE_SOCKADDR_UN
3313            apr_sockaddr_info_get(&conn->addr, uds_path, APR_UNIX, 0, 0, pool);
3314            if (conn->addr && conn->addr->hostname) {
3315                conn->uds_path = conn->addr->hostname;
3316            }
3317            else {
3318                conn->uds_path = apr_pstrdup(pool, uds_path);
3319            }
3320#else
3321            apr_sockaddr_info_get(&conn->addr, NULL, APR_UNSPEC, 0, 0, pool);
3322            conn->uds_path = apr_pstrdup(pool, uds_path);
3323#endif
3324            conn->hostname = apr_pstrdup(pool, uri->hostname);
3325            conn->port = uri->port;
3326        }
3327        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(02545)
3328                     "%s: has determined UDS as %s (for %s:%hu)",
3329                     uri->scheme, conn->uds_path, conn->hostname, conn->port);
3330    }
3331    else {
3332        remote_connect_info *connect_info = NULL;
3333        const char *hostname = uri->hostname;
3334        apr_port_t hostport = uri->port;
3335
3336        if (proxyname) {
3337            hostname = proxyname;
3338            hostport = proxyport;
3339
3340            /*
3341             * If we have a remote proxy and the protocol is HTTPS,
3342             * then we need to prepend a HTTP CONNECT request before
3343             * sending our actual HTTPS requests.
3344             */ view japanese
3345            if (conn->is_ssl) {
3346                const char *proxy_auth;
3347
3348                /* Do we want to pass Proxy-Authorization along?
3349                 * If we haven't used it, then YES
3350                 * If we have used it then MAYBE: RFC2616 says we MAY propagate it.
3351                 * So let's make it configurable by env.
3352                 * The logic here is the same used in mod_proxy_http.
3353                 */ view japanese
3354                proxy_auth = apr_table_get(r->notes, "proxy-basic-creds");
3355                if (proxy_auth == NULL
3356                    && (r->user == NULL /* we haven't yet authenticated */ view japanese
3357                        || apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "Proxy-Chain-Auth"))) {
3358                    proxy_auth = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Proxy-Authorization");
3359                }
3360                if (proxy_auth != NULL && proxy_auth[0] == '\0') {
3361                    proxy_auth = NULL;
3362                }
3363
3364                /* Save our real backend data for using it later during HTTP CONNECT */ view japanese
3365                connect_info = conn->forward;
3366                if (!connect_info
3367                    /* reset connect info if they changed */ view japanese
3368                    || connect_info->target_port != uri->port
3369                    || ap_cstr_casecmp(connect_info->target_host, uri->hostname) != 0
3370                    || (connect_info->proxy_auth != NULL) != (proxy_auth != NULL)
3371                    || (connect_info->proxy_auth != NULL && proxy_auth != NULL &&
3372                        strcmp(connect_info->proxy_auth, proxy_auth) != 0)) {
3373                    apr_pool_t *fwd_pool = conn->pool;
3374                    if (worker->s->is_address_reusable) {
3375                        if (conn->fwd_pool) {
3376                            apr_pool_clear(conn->fwd_pool);
3377                        }
3378                        else {
3379                            apr_pool_create(&conn->fwd_pool, conn->pool);
3380                        }
3381                        fwd_pool = conn->fwd_pool;
3382                    }
3383                    connect_info = apr_pcalloc(fwd_pool, sizeof(*connect_info));
3384                    connect_info->target_host = apr_pstrdup(fwd_pool, uri->hostname);
3385                    connect_info->target_port = uri->port;
3386                    if (proxy_auth) {
3387                        connect_info->proxy_auth = apr_pstrdup(fwd_pool, proxy_auth);
3388                    }
3389                    conn->forward = NULL;
3390                }
3391            }
3392        }
3393
3394        /* Close the connection if the remote proxy or origin server don't match */ view japanese
3395        if (conn->forward != connect_info
3396            || (conn->hostname 
3397                && (conn->port != hostport
3398                    || ap_cstr_casecmp(conn->hostname, hostname) != 0))) {
3399            conn_cleanup(conn);
3400            conn->forward = connect_info;
3401        }
3402
3403        /* Resolve the connection address with the determined hostname/port */ view japanese
3404        if (ap_proxy_determine_address(uri->scheme, conn, hostname, hostport,
3405                                       0, r, NULL)) {
3406            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
3407        }
3408    }
3409
3410    /* Get the server port for the Via headers */ view japanese
3411    server_port = ap_get_server_port(r);
3412    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(server_portstr_size > 0);
3413    if (ap_is_default_port(server_port, r)) {
3414        server_portstr[0] = '\0';
3415    }
3416    else {
3417        apr_snprintf(server_portstr, server_portstr_size, ":%d",
3418                     server_port);
3419    }
3420
3421    /* check if ProxyBlock directive on this host */ view japanese
3422    if (OK != ap_proxy_checkproxyblock2(r, conf, uri->hostname, 
3423                                       proxyname ? NULL : conn->addr)) {
3424        return ap_proxyerror(r, HTTP_FORBIDDEN,
3425                             "Connect to remote machine blocked");
3426    }
3427    /*
3428     * When SSL is configured, determine the hostname (SNI) for the request
3429     * and save it in conn->ssl_hostname. Close any reused connection whose
3430     * SNI differs.
3431     */ view japanese
3432    if (conn->is_ssl) {
3433        proxy_dir_conf *dconf;
3434        const char *ssl_hostname;
3435        /*
3436         * In the case of ProxyPreserveHost on use the hostname of
3437         * the request if present otherwise use the one from the
3438         * backend request URI.
3439         */ view japanese
3440        dconf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &proxy_module);
3441        if (dconf->preserve_host) {
3442            ssl_hostname = r->hostname;
3443        }
3444        else if (conn->forward) {
3445            ssl_hostname = ((remote_connect_info *)conn->forward)->target_host;
3446        }
3447        else {
3448            ssl_hostname = conn->hostname;
3449        }
3450        /*
3451         * Close if a SNI is in use but this request requires no or
3452         * a different one, or no SNI is in use but one is required.
3453         */ view japanese
3454        if ((conn->ssl_hostname && (!ssl_hostname ||
3455                                    strcasecmp(conn->ssl_hostname,
3456                                               ssl_hostname) != 0)) ||
3457                (!conn->ssl_hostname && ssl_hostname && conn->sock)) {
3458            socket_cleanup(conn);
3459        }
3460        if (conn->ssl_hostname == NULL) {
3461            conn->ssl_hostname = apr_pstrdup(conn->scpool, ssl_hostname);
3462        }
3463    }
3464    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00947)
3465                 "connecting %s to %pI (%s:%hu)", *url,
3466                 conn->addr, conn->hostname, conn->port);
3467    return OK;
3468}
3469
3470#define USE_ALTERNATE_IS_CONNECTED 1
3471
3472#if !defined(APR_MSG_PEEK) && defined(MSG_PEEK)
3473#define APR_MSG_PEEK MSG_PEEK
3474#endif
3475
3476#if USE_ALTERNATE_IS_CONNECTED && defined(APR_MSG_PEEK)
3477PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_socket_connected(apr_socket_t *socket)
3478{
3479    apr_pollfd_t pfds[1];
3480    apr_status_t status;
3481    apr_int32_t  nfds;
3482
3483    pfds[0].reqevents = APR_POLLIN;
3484    pfds[0].desc_type = APR_POLL_SOCKET;
3485    pfds[0].desc.s = socket;
3486
3487    do {
3488        status = apr_poll(&pfds[0], 1, &nfds, 0);
3489    } while (APR_STATUS_IS_EINTR(status));
3490
3491    if (status == APR_SUCCESS && nfds == 1 &&
3492        pfds[0].rtnevents == APR_POLLIN) {
3493        apr_sockaddr_t unused;
3494        apr_size_t len = 1;
3495        char buf[1];
3496        /* The socket might be closed in which case
3497         * the poll will return POLLIN.
3498         * If there is no data available the socket
3499         * is closed.
3500         */ view japanese
3501        status = apr_socket_recvfrom(&unused, socket, APR_MSG_PEEK,
3502                                     &buf[0], &len);
3503        if (status == APR_SUCCESS && len)
3504            return 1;
3505        else
3506            return 0;
3507    }
3508    else if (APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(status) || APR_STATUS_IS_TIMEUP(status)) {
3509        return 1;
3510    }
3511    return 0;
3512
3513}
3514#else
3515PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_is_socket_connected(apr_socket_t *sock)
3516
3517{
3518    apr_size_t buffer_len = 1;
3519    char test_buffer[1];
3520    apr_status_t socket_status;
3521    apr_interval_time_t current_timeout;
3522
3523    /* save timeout */ view japanese
3524    apr_socket_timeout_get(sock, &current_timeout);
3525    /* set no timeout */ view japanese
3526    apr_socket_timeout_set(sock, 0);
3527    socket_status = apr_socket_recv(sock, test_buffer, &buffer_len);
3528    /* put back old timeout */ view japanese
3529    apr_socket_timeout_set(sock, current_timeout);
3530    if (APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(socket_status)
3531        || APR_STATUS_IS_ECONNRESET(socket_status)) {
3532        return 0;
3533    }
3534    else {
3535        return 1;
3536    }
3537}
3538#endif /* USE_ALTERNATE_IS_CONNECTED */ view japanese
3539
3540
3541/*
3542 * Send a HTTP CONNECT request to a remote proxy.
3543 * The proxy is given by "backend", the target server
3544 * is contained in the "forward" member of "backend".
3545 */ view japanese
3546static apr_status_t send_http_connect(proxy_conn_rec *backend,
3547                                      server_rec *s)
3548{
3549    int status;
3550    apr_size_t nbytes;
3551    apr_size_t left;
3552    int complete = 0;
3553    char buffer[HUGE_STRING_LEN];
3554    char drain_buffer[HUGE_STRING_LEN];
3555    remote_connect_info *connect_info = backend->forward;
3556    int len = 0;
3557
3558    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00948)
3559                 "CONNECT: sending the CONNECT request for %s:%d "
3560                 "to the remote proxy %pI (%s)",
3561                 connect_info->target_host, connect_info->target_port,
3562                 backend->addr, backend->hostname);
3563    /* Create the CONNECT request */ view japanese
3564    nbytes = apr_snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer),
3565                          "CONNECT %s:%d HTTP/1.0" CRLF,
3566                          connect_info->target_host, connect_info->target_port);
3567    /* Add proxy authorization from the configuration, or initial
3568     * request if necessary */ view japanese
3569    if (connect_info->proxy_auth != NULL) {
3570        nbytes += apr_snprintf(buffer + nbytes, sizeof(buffer) - nbytes,
3571                               "Proxy-Authorization: %s" CRLF,
3572                               connect_info->proxy_auth);
3573    }
3574    /* Set a reasonable agent and send everything */ view japanese
3575    nbytes += apr_snprintf(buffer + nbytes, sizeof(buffer) - nbytes,
3576                           "Proxy-agent: %s" CRLF CRLF,
3577                           ap_get_server_banner());
3578    ap_xlate_proto_to_ascii(buffer, nbytes);
3579    apr_socket_send(backend->sock, buffer, &nbytes);
3580
3581    /* Receive the whole CONNECT response */ view japanese
3582    left = sizeof(buffer) - 1;
3583    /* Read until we find the end of the headers or run out of buffer */ view japanese
3584    do {
3585        nbytes = left;
3586        status = apr_socket_recv(backend->sock, buffer + len, &nbytes);
3587        len += nbytes;
3588        left -= nbytes;
3589        buffer[len] = '\0';
3590        if (strstr(buffer + len - nbytes, CRLF_ASCII CRLF_ASCII) != NULL) {
3591            ap_xlate_proto_from_ascii(buffer, len);
3592            complete = 1;
3593            break;
3594        }
3595    } while (status == APR_SUCCESS && left > 0);
3596    /* Drain what's left */ view japanese
3597    if (!complete) {
3598        nbytes = sizeof(drain_buffer) - 1;
3599        while (status == APR_SUCCESS && nbytes) {
3600            status = apr_socket_recv(backend->sock, drain_buffer, &nbytes);
3601            drain_buffer[nbytes] = '\0';
3602            nbytes = sizeof(drain_buffer) - 1;
3603            if (strstr(drain_buffer, CRLF_ASCII CRLF_ASCII) != NULL) {
3604                break;
3605            }
3606        }
3607    }
3608
3609    /* Check for HTTP_OK response status */ view japanese
3610    if (status == APR_SUCCESS) {
3611        unsigned int major, minor;
3612        /* Only scan for three character status code */ view japanese
3613        char code_str[4];
3614
3615        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00949)
3616                     "send_http_connect: response from the remote proxy: %s",
3617                     buffer);
3618
3619        /* Extract the returned code */ view japanese
3620        if (sscanf(buffer, "HTTP/%u.%u %3s", &major, &minor, code_str) == 3) {
3621            status = atoi(code_str);
3622            if (status == HTTP_OK) {
3623                status = APR_SUCCESS;
3624            }
3625            else {
3626                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00950)
3627                             "send_http_connect: the remote proxy returned code is '%s'",
3628                             code_str);
3629                status = APR_INCOMPLETE;
3630            }
3631        }
3632    }
3633
3634    return(status);
3635}
3636
3637
3638/* TODO: In APR 2.x: Extend apr_sockaddr_t to possibly be a path !!! */ view japanese
3639PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_connect_uds(apr_socket_t *sock,
3640                                                 const char *uds_path,
3641                                                 apr_pool_t *p)
3642{
3643#if APR_HAVE_SYS_UN_H
3644    apr_status_t rv;
3645    apr_os_sock_t rawsock;
3646    apr_interval_time_t t;
3647    struct sockaddr_un *sa;
3648    apr_socklen_t addrlen, pathlen;
3649
3650    rv = apr_os_sock_get(&rawsock, sock);
3651    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3652        return rv;
3653    }
3654
3655    rv = apr_socket_timeout_get(sock, &t);
3656    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3657        return rv;
3658    }
3659
3660    pathlen = strlen(uds_path);
3661    /* copy the UDS path (including NUL) to the sockaddr_un */ view japanese
3662    addrlen = APR_OFFSETOF(struct sockaddr_un, sun_path) + pathlen;
3663    sa = (struct sockaddr_un *)apr_palloc(p, addrlen + 1);
3664    memcpy(sa->sun_path, uds_path, pathlen + 1);
3665    sa->sun_family = AF_UNIX;
3666
3667    do {
3668        rv = connect(rawsock, (struct sockaddr*)sa, addrlen);
3669    } while (rv == -1 && (rv = errno) == EINTR);
3670
3671    if (rv && rv != EISCONN) {
3672        if ((rv == EINPROGRESS || rv == EALREADY) && (t > 0))  {
3673#if APR_MAJOR_VERSION < 2
3674            rv = apr_wait_for_io_or_timeout(NULL, sock, 0);
3675#else
3676            rv = apr_socket_wait(sock, APR_WAIT_WRITE);
3677#endif
3678        }
3679        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3680            return rv;
3681        }
3682    }
3683
3684    return APR_SUCCESS;
3685#else
3686    return APR_ENOTIMPL;
3687#endif
3688}
3689
3690PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_check_connection(const char *scheme,
3691                                                      proxy_conn_rec *conn,
3692                                                      server_rec *server,
3693                                                      unsigned max_blank_lines,
3694                                                      int flags)
3695{
3696    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
3697    proxy_worker *worker = conn->worker;
3698
3699    if (!PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
3700        /*
3701         * The worker is in error likely done by a different thread / process
3702         * e.g. for a timeout or bad status. We should respect this and should
3703         * not continue with a connection via this worker even if we got one.
3704         */
3705        rv = APR_EINVAL;
3706    }
3707    else if (conn->connection) {
3708        /* We have a conn_rec, check the full filter stack for things like
3709         * SSL alert/shutdown, filters aside data...
3710         */ view japanese
3711        rv = ap_check_pipeline(conn->connection, conn->tmp_bb,
3712                               max_blank_lines);
3713        apr_brigade_cleanup(conn->tmp_bb);
3714        if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
3715            /* Some data available, the caller might not want them. */ view japanese
3716            if (flags & PROXY_CHECK_CONN_EMPTY) {
3717                rv = APR_ENOTEMPTY;
3718            }
3719        }
3720        else if (APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(rv)) {
3721            /* Filter chain is OK and empty, yet we can't determine from
3722             * ap_check_pipeline (actually ap_core_input_filter) whether
3723             * an empty non-blocking read is EAGAIN or EOF on the socket
3724             * side (it's always SUCCESS), so check it explicitly here.
3725             */ view japanese
3726            if (ap_proxy_is_socket_connected(conn->sock)) {
3727                rv = APR_SUCCESS;
3728            }
3729            else {
3730                rv = APR_EPIPE;
3731            }
3732        }
3733    }
3734    else if (conn->sock) {
3735        /* For modules working with sockets directly, check it. */ view japanese
3736        if (!ap_proxy_is_socket_connected(conn->sock)) {
3737            rv = APR_EPIPE;
3738        }
3739    }
3740    else {
3741        rv = APR_ENOSOCKET;
3742    }
3743
3744    if (rv == APR_SUCCESS) {
3745        if (APLOGtrace2(server)) {
3746            apr_sockaddr_t *local_addr = NULL;
3747            apr_socket_addr_get(&local_addr, APR_LOCAL, conn->sock);
3748            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, server,
3749                         "%s: reusing backend connection %pI<>%pI",
3750                         scheme, local_addr, conn->addr);
3751        }
3752    }
3753    else if (conn->sock) {
3754        /* This clears conn->scpool (and associated data), so backup and
3755         * restore any ssl_hostname for this connection set earlier by
3756         * ap_proxy_determine_connection().
3757         */ view japanese
3758        char ssl_hostname[PROXY_WORKER_RFC1035_NAME_SIZE];
3759        if (rv == APR_EINVAL
3760                || !conn->ssl_hostname
3761                || PROXY_STRNCPY(ssl_hostname, conn->ssl_hostname)) {
3762            ssl_hostname[0] = '\0';
3763        }
3764
3765        socket_cleanup(conn);
3766        if (rv != APR_ENOTEMPTY) {
3767            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, server, APLOGNO(00951)
3768                         "%s: backend socket is disconnected.", scheme);
3769        }
3770        else {
3771            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_INFO, 0, server, APLOGNO(03408)
3772                         "%s: reusable backend connection is not empty: "
3773                         "forcibly closed", scheme);
3774        }
3775
3776        if (ssl_hostname[0]) {
3777            conn->ssl_hostname = apr_pstrdup(conn->scpool, ssl_hostname);
3778        }
3779    }
3780
3781    return rv;
3782}
3783
3784PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_connect_backend(const char *proxy_function,
3785                                            proxy_conn_rec *conn,
3786                                            proxy_worker *worker,
3787                                            server_rec *s)
3788{
3789    apr_status_t rv;
3790    int loglevel;
3791    remote_connect_info *connect_info = conn->forward;
3792    apr_sockaddr_t *backend_addr;
3793    /* the local address to use for the outgoing connection */ view japanese
3794    apr_sockaddr_t *local_addr;
3795    apr_socket_t *newsock;
3796    void *sconf = s->module_config;
3797    int address_reusable = worker->s->is_address_reusable;
3798    int did_dns_lookup = 0;
3799    proxy_server_conf *conf =
3800        (proxy_server_conf *) ap_get_module_config(sconf, &proxy_module);
3801
3802    rv = ap_proxy_check_connection(proxy_function, conn, s, 0, 0);
3803    if (rv == APR_EINVAL) {
3804        return DECLINED;
3805    }
3806
3807    /* We'll set conn->addr to the address actually connect()ed, so if the
3808     * network connection is not reused (per ap_proxy_check_connection()
3809     * above) we need to reset conn->addr to the first resolved address
3810     * and try to connect it first.
3811     */ view japanese
3812    if (conn->address && rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3813        conn->addr = conn->address->addr;
3814    }
3815    backend_addr = conn->addr;
3816
3817    while (rv != APR_SUCCESS && (backend_addr || conn->uds_path)) {
3818#if APR_HAVE_SYS_UN_H
3819        if (conn->uds_path)
3820        {
3821            rv = apr_socket_create(&newsock, AF_UNIX, SOCK_STREAM, 0,
3822                                   conn->scpool);
3823            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3824                loglevel = APLOG_ERR;
3825                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, s, APLOGNO(02453)
3826                             "%s: error creating Unix domain socket "
3827                             "%s (%s:%hu)",
3828                             proxy_function,
3829                             conn->uds_path,
3830                             conn->hostname, conn->port);
3831                break;
3832            }
3833            conn->connection = NULL;
3834
3835            rv = ap_proxy_connect_uds(newsock, conn->uds_path, conn->scpool);
3836            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3837                apr_socket_close(newsock);
3838                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(02454)
3839                             "%s: attempt to connect to Unix domain socket "
3840                             "%s (%s:%hu) failed",
3841                             proxy_function, conn->uds_path,
3842                             conn->hostname, conn->port);
3843                break;
3844            }
3845
3846            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(02823)
3847                         "%s: connection established with Unix domain socket "
3848                         "%s (%s:%hu)",
3849                         proxy_function,
3850                         conn->uds_path,
3851                         conn->hostname, conn->port);
3852        }
3853        else
3854#endif
3855        {
3856            if ((rv = apr_socket_create(&newsock, backend_addr->family,
3857                                        SOCK_STREAM, APR_PROTO_TCP,
3858                                        conn->scpool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
3859                loglevel = backend_addr->next ? APLOG_DEBUG : APLOG_ERR;
3860                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, s, APLOGNO(00952)
3861                             "%s: error creating fam %d socket to %pI for "
3862                             "(%s:%hu)",
3863                             proxy_function,
3864                             backend_addr->family, backend_addr,
3865                             conn->hostname, conn->port);
3866                /*
3867                 * this could be an IPv6 address from the DNS but the
3868                 * local machine won't give us an IPv6 socket; hopefully the
3869                 * DNS returned an additional address to try
3870                 */ view japanese
3871                backend_addr = backend_addr->next;
3872                continue;
3873            }
3874            conn->connection = NULL;
3875
3876            if (worker->s->recv_buffer_size > 0 &&
3877                (rv = apr_socket_opt_set(newsock, APR_SO_RCVBUF,
3878                                         worker->s->recv_buffer_size))) {
3879                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00953)
3880                             "apr_socket_opt_set(SO_RCVBUF): Failed to set "
3881                             "ProxyReceiveBufferSize, using default");
3882            }
3883
3884            rv = apr_socket_opt_set(newsock, APR_TCP_NODELAY, 1);
3885            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS && rv != APR_ENOTIMPL) {
3886                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00954)
3887                             "apr_socket_opt_set(APR_TCP_NODELAY): "
3888                             "Failed to set");
3889            }
3890
3891            /* Set a timeout for connecting to the backend on the socket */ view japanese
3892            if (worker->s->conn_timeout_set) {
3893                apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, worker->s->conn_timeout);
3894            }
3895            else if (worker->s->timeout_set) {
3896                apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, worker->s->timeout);
3897            }
3898            else if (conf->timeout_set) {
3899                apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, conf->timeout);
3900            }
3901            else {
3902                apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, s->timeout);
3903            }
3904            /* Set a keepalive option */ view japanese
3905            if (worker->s->keepalive) {
3906                if ((rv = apr_socket_opt_set(newsock,
3907                                             APR_SO_KEEPALIVE, 1)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
3908                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00955)
3909                                 "apr_socket_opt_set(SO_KEEPALIVE): Failed to set"
3910                                 " Keepalive");
3911                }
3912            }
3913            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, s,
3914                         "%s: fam %d socket created for %pI (%s:%hu)",
