3915                         proxy_function, backend_addr->family, backend_addr,
3916                         conn->hostname, conn->port);
3917
3918            if (conf->source_address_set) {
3919                local_addr = apr_pmemdup(conn->scpool, conf->source_address,
3920                                         sizeof(apr_sockaddr_t));
3921                local_addr->pool = conn->scpool;
3922                rv = apr_socket_bind(newsock, local_addr);
3923                if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3924                    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, s, APLOGNO(00956)
3925                                 "%s: failed to bind socket to local address",
3926                                 proxy_function);
3927                }
3928            }
3929
3930            /* make the connection out of the socket */ view japanese
3931            rv = apr_socket_connect(newsock, backend_addr);
3932
3933            /* if an error occurred, loop round and try again */ view japanese
3934            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
3935                apr_socket_close(newsock);
3936                loglevel = backend_addr->next ? APLOG_DEBUG : APLOG_ERR;
3937                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, s, APLOGNO(00957)
3938                             "%s: attempt to connect to %pI (%s:%hu) failed",
3939                             proxy_function, backend_addr,
3940                             conn->hostname, conn->port);
3941                backend_addr = backend_addr->next;
3942                /*
3943                 * If we run out of resolved IP's when connecting and if
3944                 * we cache the resolution in the worker the resolution
3945                 * might have changed. Hence try a DNS lookup to see if this
3946                 * helps.
3947                 */ view japanese
3948                if (!backend_addr && address_reusable && !did_dns_lookup) {
3949                    /* Issue a new DNS lookup to check if the address changed,
3950                     * in which case (SUCCESS) restart the loop with the new
3951                     * one(s), otherwise leave (nothing we can do about it).
3952                     */ view japanese
3953                    if (ap_proxy_determine_address(proxy_function, conn,
3954                                                   conn->hostname, conn->port,
3955                                                   PROXY_DETERMINE_ADDRESS_CHECK,
3956                                                   NULL, s) == APR_SUCCESS) {
3957                        backend_addr = conn->addr;
3958                    }
3959
3960                    /*
3961                     * In case of an error backend_addr will be NULL which
3962                     * is enough to leave the loop. If successful we'll retry
3963                     * the new addresses only once.
3964                     */ view japanese
3965                    did_dns_lookup = 1;
3966                }
3967                continue;
3968            }
3969
3970            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(02824)
3971                         "%s: connection established with %pI (%s:%hu)",
3972                         proxy_function, backend_addr,
3973                         conn->hostname, conn->port);
3974
3975            /* Set the actual sockaddr we are connected to */ view japanese
3976            conn->addr = backend_addr;
3977        }
3978
3979        /* Set a timeout on the socket */ view japanese
3980        if (worker->s->timeout_set) {
3981            apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, worker->s->timeout);
3982        }
3983        else if (conf->timeout_set) {
3984            apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, conf->timeout);
3985        }
3986        else {
3987             apr_socket_timeout_set(newsock, s->timeout);
3988        }
3989
3990        conn->sock = newsock;
3991
3992        /*
3993         * For HTTP CONNECT we need to prepend CONNECT request before
3994         * sending our actual HTTPS requests.
3995         */ view japanese
3996        if (connect_info) {
3997            rv = send_http_connect(conn, s);
3998            /* If an error occurred, loop round and try again */ view japanese
3999            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4000                conn->sock = NULL;
4001                apr_socket_close(newsock);
4002                loglevel = backend_addr->next ? APLOG_DEBUG : APLOG_ERR;
4003                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, loglevel, rv, s, APLOGNO(00958)
4004                             "%s: attempt to connect to %s:%hu "
4005                             "via http CONNECT through %pI (%s:%hu) failed",
4006                             proxy_function,
4007                             connect_info->target_host, connect_info->target_port,
4008                             backend_addr, conn->hostname, conn->port);
4009                backend_addr = backend_addr->next;
4010                continue;
4011            }
4012        }
4013    }
4014
4015    if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(worker)) {
4016        /*
4017         * Put the entire worker to error state if
4018         * the PROXY_WORKER_IGNORE_ERRORS flag is not set.
4019         * Although some connections may be alive
4020         * no further connections to the worker could be made
4021         */ view japanese
4022        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4023            if (!(worker->s->status & PROXY_WORKER_IGNORE_ERRORS)) {
4024                worker->s->error_time = apr_time_now();
4025                worker->s->status |= PROXY_WORKER_IN_ERROR;
4026                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, s, APLOGNO(00959)
4027                    "ap_proxy_connect_backend disabling worker for (%s:%d) "
4028                    "for %" APR_TIME_T_FMT "s",
4029                    worker->s->hostname_ex, (int)worker->s->port,
4030                    apr_time_sec(worker->s->retry));
4031            }
4032        }
4033        else {
4034            if (worker->s->retries) {
4035                /*
4036                 * A worker came back. So here is where we need to
4037                 * either reset all params to initial conditions or
4038                 * apply some sort of aging
4039                 */ view japanese
4040            }
4041            worker->s->error_time = 0;
4042            worker->s->retries = 0;
4043        }
4044    }
4045    else {
4046        /*
4047         * The worker is in error likely done by a different thread / process
4048         * e.g. for a timeout or bad status. We should respect this and should
4049         * not continue with a connection via this worker even if we got one.
4050         */ view japanese
4051        rv = APR_EINVAL;
4052    }
4053    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4054        socket_cleanup(conn);
4055        return DECLINED;
4056    }
4057    return OK;
4058}
4059
4060static apr_status_t connection_shutdown(void *theconn)
4061{
4062    proxy_conn_rec *conn = (proxy_conn_rec *)theconn;
4063    conn_rec *c = conn->connection;
4064    if (c) {
4065        if (!c->aborted) {
4066            apr_interval_time_t saved_timeout = 0;
4067            apr_socket_timeout_get(conn->sock, &saved_timeout);
4068            if (saved_timeout) {
4069                apr_socket_timeout_set(conn->sock, 0);
4070            }
4071
4072            (void)ap_shutdown_conn(c, 0);
4073            c->aborted = 1;
4074
4075            if (saved_timeout) {
4076                apr_socket_timeout_set(conn->sock, saved_timeout);
4077            }
4078        }
4079
4080        ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, c, APLOGNO(02642)
4081                      "proxy: connection shutdown");
4082    }
4083    return APR_SUCCESS;
4084}
4085
4086
4087static int proxy_connection_create(const char *proxy_function,
4088                                   proxy_conn_rec *conn,
4089                                   request_rec *r, server_rec *s)
4090{
4091    ap_conf_vector_t *per_dir_config = (r) ? r->per_dir_config
4092                                           : conn->worker->section_config;
4093    apr_sockaddr_t *backend_addr = conn->addr;
4094    apr_interval_time_t current_timeout;
4095    apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc;
4096    int rc = OK;
4097
4098    if (conn->connection) {
4099        if (conn->is_ssl) {
4100            /* on reuse, reinit the SSL connection dir config with the current
4101             * r->per_dir_config, the previous one was reset on release.
4102             */ view japanese
4103            ap_proxy_ssl_engine(conn->connection, per_dir_config, 1);
4104        }
4105        return OK;
4106    }
4107
4108    if (conn->sock) {
4109        bucket_alloc = apr_bucket_alloc_create(conn->scpool);
4110        conn->tmp_bb = apr_brigade_create(conn->scpool, bucket_alloc);
4111        /*
4112         * The socket is now open, create a new backend server connection
4113         */ view japanese
4114        conn->connection = ap_run_create_connection(conn->scpool, s, conn->sock,
4115                                                    0, NULL, bucket_alloc);
4116    }
4117    if (!conn->connection) {
4118        /*
4119         * the peer reset the connection already; ap_run_create_connection()
4120         * closed the socket
4121         */ view japanese
4122        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0,
4123                     s, APLOGNO(00960) "%s: an error occurred creating a "
4124                     "new connection to %pI (%s)%s",
4125                     proxy_function, backend_addr, conn->hostname,
4126                     conn->sock ? "" : " (not connected)");
4127        rc = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4128        goto cleanup;
4129    }
4130
4131    /* For ssl connection to backend */ view japanese
4132    if (conn->is_ssl) {
4133        if (!ap_proxy_ssl_engine(conn->connection, per_dir_config, 1)) {
4134            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0,
4135                         s, APLOGNO(00961) "%s: failed to enable ssl support "
4136                         "for %pI (%s)", proxy_function,
4137                         backend_addr, conn->hostname);
4138            rc = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4139            goto cleanup;
4140        }
4141        if (conn->ssl_hostname) {
4142            /* Set a note on the connection about what CN is requested,
4143             * such that mod_ssl can check if it is requested to do so.
4144             */ view japanese
4145            ap_log_cerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, conn->connection, 
4146                          "%s: set SNI to %s for (%s)", proxy_function,
4147                          conn->ssl_hostname, conn->hostname);
4148            apr_table_setn(conn->connection->notes, "proxy-request-hostname",
4149                           conn->ssl_hostname);
4150        }
4151    }
4152    else {
4153        /* TODO: See if this will break FTP */ view japanese
4154        ap_proxy_ssl_engine(conn->connection, per_dir_config, 0);
4155    }
4156
4157    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00962)
4158                 "%s: connection complete to %pI (%s)",
4159                 proxy_function, backend_addr, conn->hostname);
4160
4161    /*
4162     * save the timeout of the socket because core_pre_connection
4163     * will set it to base_server->timeout
4164     * (core TimeOut directive).
4165     */ view japanese
4166    apr_socket_timeout_get(conn->sock, &current_timeout);
4167    /* set up the connection filters */ view japanese
4168    rc = ap_run_pre_connection(conn->connection, conn->sock);
4169    if (rc != OK && rc != DONE) {
4170        conn->connection->aborted = 1;
4171        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(00963)
4172                     "%s: pre_connection setup failed (%d)",
4173                     proxy_function, rc);
4174        goto cleanup;
4175    }
4176    apr_socket_timeout_set(conn->sock, current_timeout);
4177
4178    /* Shutdown the connection before closing it (eg. SSL connections
4179     * need to be close-notify-ed).
4180     */ view japanese
4181    apr_pool_pre_cleanup_register(conn->scpool, conn, connection_shutdown);
4182
4183    return OK;
4184
4185cleanup:
4186    socket_cleanup(conn);
4187    return rc;
4188}
4189
4190PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_connection_create_ex(const char *proxy_function,
4191                                                 proxy_conn_rec *conn,
4192                                                 request_rec *r)
4193{
4194    return proxy_connection_create(proxy_function, conn, r, r->server);
4195}
4196
4197PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_connection_create(const char *proxy_function,
4198                                              proxy_conn_rec *conn,
4199                                              conn_rec *c, server_rec *s)
4200{
4201    (void) c; /* unused */ view japanese
4202    return proxy_connection_create(proxy_function, conn, NULL, s);
4203}
4204
4205int ap_proxy_lb_workers(void)
4206{
4207    /*
4208     * Since we can't resize the scoreboard when reconfiguring, we
4209     * have to impose a limit on the number of workers, we are
4210     * able to reconfigure to.
4211     */ view japanese
4212    if (!lb_workers_limit)
4213        lb_workers_limit = proxy_lb_workers + PROXY_DYNAMIC_BALANCER_LIMIT;
4214    return lb_workers_limit;
4215}
4216
4217static APR_INLINE int error_code_overridden(const int *elts, int nelts,
4218                                            int code)
4219{
4220    int min = 0;
4221    int max = nelts - 1;
4222    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(max >= 0);
4223
4224    while (min < max) {
4225        int mid = (min + max) / 2;
4226        int val = elts[mid];
4227
4228        if (val < code) {
4229            min = mid + 1;
4230        }
4231        else if (val > code) {
4232            max = mid - 1;
4233        }
4234        else {
4235            return 1;
4236        }
4237    }
4238
4239    return elts[min] == code;
4240}
4241
4242PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_should_override(proxy_dir_conf *conf, int code)
4243{
4244    if (!conf->error_override) 
4245        return 0;
4246
4247    if (apr_is_empty_array(conf->error_override_codes))
4248        return ap_is_HTTP_ERROR(code);
4249
4250    /* Since error_override_codes is sorted, apply binary search. */ view japanese
4251    return error_code_overridden((int *)conf->error_override_codes->elts,
4252                                 conf->error_override_codes->nelts,
4253                                 code);
4254}
4255
4256PROXY_DECLARE(void) ap_proxy_backend_broke(request_rec *r,
4257                                           apr_bucket_brigade *brigade)
4258{
4259    apr_bucket *e;
4260    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
4261
4262    r->no_cache = 1;
4263    /*
4264     * If this is a subrequest, then prevent also caching of the main
4265     * request.
4266     */ view japanese
4267    if (r->main)
4268        r->main->no_cache = 1;
4269    e = ap_bucket_error_create(HTTP_BAD_GATEWAY, NULL, c->pool,
4270                               c->bucket_alloc);
4271    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(brigade, e);
4272    e = apr_bucket_eos_create(c->bucket_alloc);
4273    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(brigade, e);
4274}
4275
4276/*
4277 * Provide a string hashing function for the proxy.
4278 * We offer 2 methods: one is the APR model but we
4279 * also provide our own, based on either FNV or SDBM.
4280 * The reason is in case we want to use both to ensure no
4281 * collisions.
4282 */ view japanese
4283PROXY_DECLARE(unsigned int)
4284ap_proxy_hashfunc(const char *str, proxy_hash_t method)
4285{
4286    if (method == PROXY_HASHFUNC_APR) {
4287        apr_ssize_t slen = strlen(str);
4288        return apr_hashfunc_default(str, &slen);
4289    }
4290    else if (method == PROXY_HASHFUNC_FNV) {
4291        /* FNV model */ view japanese
4292        unsigned int hash;
4293        const unsigned int fnv_prime = 0x811C9DC5;
4294        for (hash = 0; *str; str++) {
4295            hash *= fnv_prime;
4296            hash ^= (*str);
4297        }
4298        return hash;
4299    }
4300    else { /* method == PROXY_HASHFUNC_DEFAULT */ view japanese
4301        /* SDBM model */ view japanese
4302        unsigned int hash;
4303        for (hash = 0; *str; str++) {
4304            hash = (*str) + (hash << 6) + (hash << 16) - hash;
4305        }
4306        return hash;
4307    }
4308}
4309
4310PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_set_wstatus(char c, int set, proxy_worker *w)
4311{
4312    unsigned int *status = &w->s->status;
4313    char flag = toupper(c);
4314    proxy_wstat_t *pwt = proxy_wstat_tbl;
4315    while (pwt->bit) {
4316        if (flag == pwt->flag) {
4317            if (set)
4318                *status |= pwt->bit;
4319            else
4320                *status &= ~(pwt->bit);
4321            return APR_SUCCESS;
4322        }
4323        pwt++;
4324    }
4325    return APR_EINVAL;
4326}
4327
4328PROXY_DECLARE(char *) ap_proxy_parse_wstatus(apr_pool_t *p, proxy_worker *w)
4329{
4330    char *ret = "";
4331    unsigned int status = w->s->status;
4332    proxy_wstat_t *pwt = proxy_wstat_tbl;
4333    while (pwt->bit) {
4334        if (status & pwt->bit)
4335            ret = apr_pstrcat(p, ret, pwt->name, NULL);
4336        pwt++;
4337    }
4338    if (!*ret) {
4339        ret = "??? ";
4340    }
4341    if (PROXY_WORKER_IS_USABLE(w))
4342        ret = apr_pstrcat(p, ret, "Ok ", NULL);
4343    return ret;
4344}
4345
4346PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_sync_balancer(proxy_balancer *b, server_rec *s,
4347                                                    proxy_server_conf *conf)
4348{
4349    proxy_worker **workers;
4350    int i;
4351    int index;
4352    proxy_worker_shared *shm;
4353    proxy_balancer_method *lbmethod;
4354    ap_slotmem_provider_t *storage = b->storage;
4355
4356    if (b->s->wupdated <= b->wupdated)
4357        return APR_SUCCESS;
4358    /* balancer sync */ view japanese
4359    lbmethod = ap_lookup_provider(PROXY_LBMETHOD, b->s->lbpname, "0");
4360    if (lbmethod) {
4361        b->lbmethod = lbmethod;
4362    } else {
4363        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_CRIT, 0, s, APLOGNO(02433)
4364                     "Cannot find LB Method: %s", b->s->lbpname);
4365        return APR_EINVAL;
4366    }
4367
4368    /* worker sync */ view japanese
4369
4370    /*
4371     * Look thru the list of workers in shm
4372     * and see which one(s) we are lacking...
4373     * again, the cast to unsigned int is safe
4374     * since our upper limit is always max_workers
4375     * which is int.
4376     */ view japanese
4377    for (index = 0; index < b->max_workers; index++) {
4378        int found;
4379        apr_status_t rv;
4380        if ((rv = storage->dptr(b->wslot, (unsigned int)index, (void *)&shm)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
4381            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_EMERG, rv, s, APLOGNO(00965) "worker slotmem_dptr failed");
4382            return APR_EGENERAL;
4383        }
4384        /* account for possible "holes" in the slotmem
4385         * (eg: slots 0-2 are used, but 3 isn't, but 4-5 is)
4386         */ view japanese
4387        if (!shm->hash.def || !shm->hash.fnv)
4388            continue;
4389        found = 0;
4390        workers = (proxy_worker **)b->workers->elts;
4391        for (i = 0; i < b->workers->nelts; i++, workers++) {
4392            proxy_worker *worker = *workers;
4393            if (worker->hash.def == shm->hash.def && worker->hash.fnv == shm->hash.fnv) {
4394                found = 1;
4395                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(02402)
4396                             "re-grabbing shm[%d] (0x%pp) for worker: %s", i, (void *)shm,
4397                             ap_proxy_worker_name(conf->pool, worker));
4398                break;
4399            }
4400        }
4401        if (!found) {
4402            proxy_worker **runtime;
4403            /* XXX: a thread mutex is maybe enough here */ view japanese
4404            apr_global_mutex_lock(proxy_mutex);
4405            runtime = apr_array_push(b->workers);
4406            *runtime = apr_pcalloc(conf->pool, sizeof(proxy_worker));
4407            apr_global_mutex_unlock(proxy_mutex);
4408            (*runtime)->hash = shm->hash;
4409            (*runtime)->balancer = b;
4410            (*runtime)->s = shm;
4411
4412            rv = ap_proxy_initialize_worker(*runtime, s, conf->pool);
4413            if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
4414                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_EMERG, rv, s, APLOGNO(00966) "Cannot init worker");
4415                return rv;
4416            }
4417            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, s, APLOGNO(02403)
4418                         "grabbing shm[%d] (0x%pp) for worker: %s", i, (void *)shm,
4419                         (*runtime)->s->name_ex);
4420        }
4421    }
4422    if (b->s->need_reset) {
4423        if (b->lbmethod && b->lbmethod->reset)
4424            b->lbmethod->reset(b, s);
4425        b->s->need_reset = 0;
4426    }
4427    b->wupdated = b->s->wupdated;
4428    return APR_SUCCESS;
4429}
4430
4431PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_worker_shared *) ap_proxy_find_workershm(ap_slotmem_provider_t *storage,
4432                                                               ap_slotmem_instance_t *slot,
4433                                                               proxy_worker *worker,
4434                                                               unsigned int *index)
4435{
4436    proxy_worker_shared *shm;
4437    unsigned int i, limit;
4438    limit = storage->num_slots(slot);
4439    for (i = 0; i < limit; i++) {
4440        if (storage->dptr(slot, i, (void *)&shm) != APR_SUCCESS) {
4441            return NULL;
4442        }
4443        if ((worker->s->hash.def == shm->hash.def) &&
4444            (worker->s->hash.fnv == shm->hash.fnv)) {
4445            *index = i;
4446            return shm;
4447        }
4448    }
4449    return NULL;
4450}
4451
4452PROXY_DECLARE(proxy_balancer_shared *) ap_proxy_find_balancershm(ap_slotmem_provider_t *storage,
4453                                                                 ap_slotmem_instance_t *slot,
4454                                                                 proxy_balancer *balancer,
4455                                                                 unsigned int *index)
4456{
4457    proxy_balancer_shared *shm;
4458    unsigned int i, limit;
4459    limit = storage->num_slots(slot);
4460    for (i = 0; i < limit; i++) {
4461        if (storage->dptr(slot, i, (void *)&shm) != APR_SUCCESS) {
4462            return NULL;
4463        }
4464        if ((balancer->s->hash.def == shm->hash.def) &&
4465            (balancer->s->hash.fnv == shm->hash.fnv)) {
4466            *index = i;
4467            return shm;
4468        }
4469    }
4470    return NULL;
4471}
4472
4473typedef struct header_connection {
4474    apr_pool_t *pool;
4475    apr_array_header_t *array;
4476    const char *first;
4477    unsigned int closed:1;
4478} header_connection;
4479
4480static int find_conn_headers(void *data, const char *key, const char *val)
4481{
4482    header_connection *x = data;
4483    const char *name;
4484
4485    do {
4486        while (*val == ',' || *val == ';') {
4487            val++;
4488        }
4489        name = ap_get_token(x->pool, &val, 0);
4490        if (!strcasecmp(name, "close")) {
4491            x->closed = 1;
4492        }
4493        if (!x->first) {
4494            x->first = name;
4495        }
4496        else {
4497            const char **elt;
4498            if (!x->array) {
4499                x->array = apr_array_make(x->pool, 4, sizeof(char *));
4500            }
4501            elt = apr_array_push(x->array);
4502            *elt = name;
4503        }
4504    } while (*val);
4505
4506    return 1;
4507}
4508
4509/**
4510 * Remove all headers referred to by the Connection header.
4511 */ view japanese
4512static int ap_proxy_clear_connection(request_rec *r, apr_table_t *headers)
4513{
4514    const char **name;
4515    header_connection x;
4516
4517    x.pool = r->pool;
4518    x.array = NULL;
4519    x.first = NULL;
4520    x.closed = 0;
4521
4522    apr_table_unset(headers, "Proxy-Connection");
4523
4524    apr_table_do(find_conn_headers, &x, headers, "Connection", NULL);
4525    if (x.first) {
4526        /* fast path - no memory allocated for one header */ view japanese
4527        apr_table_unset(headers, "Connection");
4528        apr_table_unset(headers, x.first);
4529    }
4530    if (x.array) {
4531        /* two or more headers */ view japanese
4532        while ((name = apr_array_pop(x.array))) {
4533            apr_table_unset(headers, *name);
4534        }
4535    }
4536
4537    return x.closed;
4538}
4539
4540PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_create_hdrbrgd(apr_pool_t *p,
4541                                            apr_bucket_brigade *header_brigade,
4542                                            request_rec *r,
4543                                            proxy_conn_rec *p_conn,
4544                                            proxy_worker *worker,
4545                                            proxy_server_conf *conf,
4546                                            apr_uri_t *uri,
4547                                            char *url, char *server_portstr,
4548                                            char **old_cl_val,
4549                                            char **old_te_val)
4550{
4551    int rc = OK;
4552    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
4553    int counter;
4554    char *buf;
4555    apr_table_t *saved_headers_in = r->headers_in;
4556    const char *saved_host = apr_table_get(saved_headers_in, "Host");
4557    const apr_array_header_t *headers_in_array;
4558    const apr_table_entry_t *headers_in;
4559    apr_bucket *e;
4560    int force10 = 0, do_100_continue = 0;
4561    conn_rec *origin = p_conn->connection;
4562    const char *host, *creds, *val;
4563    proxy_dir_conf *dconf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &proxy_module);
4564
4565    /*
4566     * HTTP "Ping" test? Easiest is 100-Continue. However:
4567     * To be compliant, we only use 100-Continue for requests with bodies.
4568     * We also make sure we won't be talking HTTP/1.0 as well.
4569     */ view japanese
4570    if (apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "force-proxy-request-1.0")) {
4571        force10 = 1;
4572    }
4573    else if (apr_table_get(r->notes, "proxy-100-continue")
4574             || PROXY_SHOULD_PING_100_CONTINUE(worker, r)) {
4575        do_100_continue = 1;
4576    }
4577    if (force10 || apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "proxy-nokeepalive")) {
4578        if (origin) {
4579            origin->keepalive = AP_CONN_CLOSE;
4580        }
4581        p_conn->close = 1;
4582    }
4583
4584    if (force10) {
4585        buf = apr_pstrcat(p, r->method, " ", url, " HTTP/1.0" CRLF, NULL);
4586    }
4587    else {
4588        buf = apr_pstrcat(p, r->method, " ", url, " HTTP/1.1" CRLF, NULL);
4589    }
4590    ap_xlate_proto_to_ascii(buf, strlen(buf));
4591    e = apr_bucket_pool_create(buf, strlen(buf), p, c->bucket_alloc);
4592    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(header_brigade, e);
4593
4594    /*
4595     * Make a copy on r->headers_in for the request we make to the backend,
4596     * modify the copy in place according to our configuration and connection
4597     * handling, use it to fill in the forwarded headers' brigade, and finally
4598     * restore the saved/original ones in r->headers_in.
4599     *
4600     * Note: We need to take r->pool for apr_table_copy as the key / value
4601     * pairs in r->headers_in have been created out of r->pool and
4602     * p might be (and actually is) a longer living pool.
4603     * This would trigger the bad pool ancestry abort in apr_table_copy if
4604     * apr is compiled with APR_POOL_DEBUG.
4605     *
4606     * icing: if p indeed lives longer than r->pool, we should allocate
4607     * all new header values from r->pool as well and avoid leakage.
4608     */ view japanese
4609    r->headers_in = apr_table_copy(r->pool, saved_headers_in);
4610
4611    /* Return the original Transfer-Encoding and/or Content-Length values
4612     * then drop the headers, they must be set by the proxy handler based
4613     * on the actual body being forwarded.
4614     */ view japanese
4615    if ((*old_te_val = (char *)apr_table_get(r->headers_in,
4616                                             "Transfer-Encoding"))) {
4617        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Transfer-Encoding");
4618    }
4619    if ((*old_cl_val = (char *)apr_table_get(r->headers_in,
4620                                             "Content-Length"))) {
4621        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Content-Length");
4622    }
4623
4624    /* Clear out hop-by-hop request headers not to forward */ view japanese
4625    if (ap_proxy_clear_connection(r, r->headers_in) < 0) {
4626        rc = HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
4627        goto cleanup;
4628    }
4629
4630    /* RFC2616 13.5.1 says we should strip these */ view japanese
4631    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Keep-Alive");
4632    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Upgrade");
4633    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Trailer");
4634    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "TE");
4635
4636    /* Compute Host header */ view japanese
4637    if (dconf->preserve_host == 0) {
4638        if (!uri->hostname) {
4639            rc = HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
4640            goto cleanup;
4641        }
4642        if (ap_strchr_c(uri->hostname, ':')) { /* if literal IPv6 address */ view japanese
4643            if (uri->port_str && uri->port != DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT) {
4644                host = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "[", uri->hostname, "]:",
4645                                   uri->port_str, NULL);
4646            } else {
4647                host = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "[", uri->hostname, "]", NULL);
4648            }
4649        } else {
4650            if (uri->port_str && uri->port != DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT) {
4651                host = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, uri->hostname, ":",
4652                                   uri->port_str, NULL);
4653            } else {
4654                host = uri->hostname;
4655            }
4656        }
4657        apr_table_setn(r->headers_in, "Host", host);
4658    }
4659    else {
4660        /* don't want to use r->hostname as the incoming header might have a
4661         * port attached, let's use the original header.
4662         */ view japanese
4663        host = saved_host;
4664        if (!host) {
4665            host =  r->server->server_hostname;
4666            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, r, APLOGNO(01092)
4667                          "no HTTP 0.9 request (with no host line) "
4668                          "on incoming request and preserve host set "
4669                          "forcing hostname to be %s for uri %s",
4670                          host, r->uri);
4671            apr_table_setn(r->headers_in, "Host", host);
4672        }
4673    }
4674
4675    /* handle Via */ view japanese
4676    if (conf->viaopt == via_block) {
4677        /* Block all outgoing Via: headers */ view japanese
4678        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Via");
4679    } else if (conf->viaopt != via_off) {
4680        const char *server_name = ap_get_server_name(r);
4681        /* If USE_CANONICAL_NAME_OFF was configured for the proxy virtual host,
4682         * then the server name returned by ap_get_server_name() is the
4683         * origin server name (which does make too much sense with Via: headers)
4684         * so we use the proxy vhost's name instead.
4685         */ view japanese
4686        if (server_name == r->hostname)
4687            server_name = r->server->server_hostname;
4688        /* Create a "Via:" request header entry and merge it */ view japanese
4689        /* Generate outgoing Via: header with/without server comment: */ view japanese
4690        apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "Via",
4691                         (conf->viaopt == via_full)
4692                         ? apr_psprintf(p, "%d.%d %s%s (%s)",
4693                                        HTTP_VERSION_MAJOR(r->proto_num),
4694                                        HTTP_VERSION_MINOR(r->proto_num),
4695                                        server_name, server_portstr,
4696                                        AP_SERVER_BASEVERSION)
4697                         : apr_psprintf(p, "%d.%d %s%s",
4698                                        HTTP_VERSION_MAJOR(r->proto_num),
4699                                        HTTP_VERSION_MINOR(r->proto_num),
4700                                        server_name, server_portstr)
4701                         );
4702    }
4703
4704    /* Use HTTP/1.1 100-Continue as quick "HTTP ping" test
4705     * to backend
4706     */ view japanese
4707    if (do_100_continue) {
4708        /* Add the Expect header if not already there. */ view japanese
4709        if (!(val = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Expect"))
4710            || (ap_cstr_casecmp(val, "100-Continue") != 0 /* fast path */ view japanese
4711                && !ap_find_token(r->pool, val, "100-Continue"))) {
4712            apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "Expect", "100-Continue");
4713        }
4714    }
4715    else {
4716        /* XXX: we should strip the 100-continue token only from the
4717         * Expect header, but are there others actually used anywhere?
4718         */ view japanese
4719        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Expect");
4720    }
4721
4722    /* X-Forwarded-*: handling
4723     *
4724     * XXX Privacy Note:
4725     * -----------------
4726     *
4727     * These request headers are only really useful when the mod_proxy
4728     * is used in a reverse proxy configuration, so that useful info
4729     * about the client can be passed through the reverse proxy and on
4730     * to the backend server, which may require the information to
4731     * function properly.
4732     *
4733     * In a forward proxy situation, these options are a potential
4734     * privacy violation, as information about clients behind the proxy
4735     * are revealed to arbitrary servers out there on the internet.
4736     *
4737     * The HTTP/1.1 Via: header is designed for passing client
4738     * information through proxies to a server, and should be used in
4739     * a forward proxy configuration instead of X-Forwarded-*. See the
4740     * ProxyVia option for details.
4741     */ view japanese
4742    if (dconf->add_forwarded_headers) {
4743        if (PROXYREQ_REVERSE == r->proxyreq) {
4744            /* Add X-Forwarded-For: so that the upstream has a chance to
4745             * determine, where the original request came from.
4746             */ view japanese
4747            apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "X-Forwarded-For",
4748                             r->useragent_ip);
4749
4750            /* Add X-Forwarded-Host: so that upstream knows what the
4751             * original request hostname was.
4752             */ view japanese
4753            if (saved_host) {
4754                apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "X-Forwarded-Host",
4755                                 saved_host);
4756            }
4757
4758            /* Add X-Forwarded-Server: so that upstream knows what the
4759             * name of this proxy server is (if there are more than one)
4760             * XXX: This duplicates Via: - do we strictly need it?
4761             */ view japanese
4762            apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "X-Forwarded-Server",
4763                             r->server->server_hostname);
4764        }
4765    }
4766
4767    /* Do we want to strip Proxy-Authorization ?
4768     * If we haven't used it, then NO
4769     * If we have used it then MAYBE: RFC2616 says we MAY propagate it.
4770     * So let's make it configurable by env.
4771     */ view japanese
4772    if (r->user != NULL /* we've authenticated */ view japanese
4773        && !apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "Proxy-Chain-Auth")) {
4774        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Proxy-Authorization");
4775    }
4776
4777    /* for sub-requests, ignore freshness/expiry headers */ view japanese
4778    if (r->main) {
4779        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Match");
4780        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Modified-Since");
4781        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Range");
4782        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Unmodified-Since");
4783        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-None-Match");
4784    }
4785
4786    creds = apr_table_get(r->notes, "proxy-basic-creds");
4787    if (creds) {
4788        apr_table_mergen(r->headers_in, "Proxy-Authorization", creds);
4789    }
4790
4791    /* run hook to fixup the request we are about to send */ view japanese
4792    proxy_run_fixups(r);
4793
4794    /* We used to send `Host: ` always first, so let's keep it that
4795     * way. No telling which legacy backend is relying on this.
4796     * If proxy_run_fixups() changed the value, use it (though removal
4797     * is ignored).
4798     */ view japanese
4799    val = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Host");
4800    if (val) {
4801        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Host");
4802        host = val;
4803    }
4804    buf = apr_pstrcat(p, "Host: ", host, CRLF, NULL);
4805    ap_xlate_proto_to_ascii(buf, strlen(buf));
4806    e = apr_bucket_pool_create(buf, strlen(buf), p, c->bucket_alloc);
4807    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(header_brigade, e);
4808
4809    /* Append the (remaining) headers to the brigade */ view japanese
4810    headers_in_array = apr_table_elts(r->headers_in);
4811    headers_in = (const apr_table_entry_t *) headers_in_array->elts;
4812    for (counter = 0; counter < headers_in_array->nelts; counter++) {
4813        if (headers_in[counter].key == NULL
4814            || headers_in[counter].val == NULL) {
4815            continue;
4816        }
4817
4818        buf = apr_pstrcat(p, headers_in[counter].key, ": ",
4819                          headers_in[counter].val, CRLF,
4820                          NULL);
4821        ap_xlate_proto_to_ascii(buf, strlen(buf));
4822        e = apr_bucket_pool_create(buf, strlen(buf), p, c->bucket_alloc);
4823        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(header_brigade, e);
4824    }
4825
4826cleanup:
4827    r->headers_in = saved_headers_in;
4828    return rc;
4829}
4830
4831PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_prefetch_input(request_rec *r,
4832                                           proxy_conn_rec *backend,
4833                                           apr_bucket_brigade *input_brigade,
4834                                           apr_read_type_e block,
4835                                           apr_off_t *bytes_read,
4836                                           apr_off_t max_read)
4837{
4838    apr_pool_t *p = r->pool;
4839    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
4840    apr_bucket_brigade *temp_brigade;
4841    apr_status_t status;
4842    apr_off_t bytes;
4843
4844    *bytes_read = 0;
4845    if (max_read < APR_BUCKET_BUFF_SIZE) {
4846        max_read = APR_BUCKET_BUFF_SIZE;
4847    }
4848
4849    /* Prefetch max_read bytes
4850     *
4851     * This helps us avoid any election of C-L v.s. T-E
4852     * request bodies, since we are willing to keep in
4853     * memory this much data, in any case.  This gives
4854     * us an instant C-L election if the body is of some
4855     * reasonable size.
4856     */ view japanese
4857    temp_brigade = apr_brigade_create(p, input_brigade->bucket_alloc);
4858
4859    /* Account for saved input, if any. */ view japanese
4860    apr_brigade_length(input_brigade, 0, bytes_read);
4861
4862    /* Ensure we don't hit a wall where we have a buffer too small for
4863     * ap_get_brigade's filters to fetch us another bucket, surrender
4864     * once we hit 80 bytes (an arbitrary value) less than max_read.
4865     */ view japanese
4866    while (*bytes_read < max_read - 80
4867           && (APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(input_brigade)
4868               || !APR_BUCKET_IS_EOS(APR_BRIGADE_LAST(input_brigade)))) {
4869        status = ap_get_brigade(r->input_filters, temp_brigade,
4870                                AP_MODE_READBYTES, block,
4871                                max_read - *bytes_read);
4872        /* ap_get_brigade may return success with an empty brigade
4873         * for a non-blocking read which would block
4874         */ view japanese
4875        if (block == APR_NONBLOCK_READ
4876                && ((status == APR_SUCCESS && APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(temp_brigade))
4877                    || APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(status))) {
4878            break;
4879        }
4880        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
4881            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01095)
4882                          "prefetch request body failed to %pI (%s)"
4883                          " from %s (%s)", backend->addr,
4884                          backend->hostname ? backend->hostname : "",
4885                          c->client_ip, c->remote_host ? c->remote_host : "");
4886            return ap_map_http_request_error(status, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST);
4887        }
4888
4889        apr_brigade_length(temp_brigade, 1, &bytes);
4890        *bytes_read += bytes;
4891
4892        /*
4893         * Save temp_brigade in input_brigade. (At least) in the SSL case
4894         * temp_brigade contains transient buckets whose data would get
4895         * overwritten during the next call of ap_get_brigade in the loop.
4896         * ap_save_brigade ensures these buckets to be set aside.
4897         * Calling ap_save_brigade with NULL as filter is OK, because
4898         * input_brigade already has been created and does not need to get
4899         * created by ap_save_brigade.
4900         */ view japanese
4901        status = ap_save_brigade(NULL, &input_brigade, &temp_brigade, p);
4902        if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
4903            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01096)
4904                          "processing prefetched request body failed"
4905                          " to %pI (%s) from %s (%s)", backend->addr,
4906                          backend->hostname ? backend->hostname : "",
4907                          c->client_ip, c->remote_host ? c->remote_host : "");
4908            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
4909        }
4910    }
4911
4912    return OK;
4913}
4914
4915PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_read_input(request_rec *r,
4916                                       proxy_conn_rec *backend,
4917                                       apr_bucket_brigade *bb,
4918                                       apr_off_t max_read)
4919{
4920    apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc = bb->bucket_alloc;
4921    apr_read_type_e block = (backend->connection) ? APR_NONBLOCK_READ
4922                                                  : APR_BLOCK_READ;
4923    apr_status_t status;
4924    int rv;
4925
4926    for (;;) {
4927        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb);
4928        status = ap_get_brigade(r->input_filters, bb, AP_MODE_READBYTES,
4929                                block, max_read);
4930        if (block == APR_BLOCK_READ
4931                || (!(status == APR_SUCCESS && APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(bb))
4932                    && !APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(status))) {
4933            break;
4934        }
4935
4936        /* Flush and retry (blocking) */ view japanese
4937        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb);
4938        rv = ap_proxy_pass_brigade(bucket_alloc, r, backend,
4939                                   backend->connection, bb, 1);
4940        if (rv != OK) {
4941            return rv;
4942        }
4943        block = APR_BLOCK_READ;
4944    }
4945
4946    if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
4947        conn_rec *c = r->connection;
4948        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(02608)
4949                      "read request body failed to %pI (%s)"
4950                      " from %s (%s)", backend->addr,
4951                      backend->hostname ? backend->hostname : "",
4952                      c->client_ip, c->remote_host ? c->remote_host : "");
4953        return ap_map_http_request_error(status, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST);
4954    }
4955
4956    return OK;
4957}
4958
4959PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_spool_input(request_rec *r,
4960                                        proxy_conn_rec *backend,
4961                                        apr_bucket_brigade *input_brigade,
4962                                        apr_off_t *bytes_spooled,
4963                                        apr_off_t max_mem_spool)
4964{
4965    apr_pool_t *p = r->pool;
4966    int seen_eos = 0, rv = OK;
4967    apr_status_t status = APR_SUCCESS;
4968    apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc = input_brigade->bucket_alloc;
4969    apr_bucket_brigade *body_brigade;
4970    apr_bucket *e;
4971    apr_off_t bytes, fsize = 0;
4972    apr_file_t *tmpfile = NULL;
4973
4974    *bytes_spooled = 0;
4975    body_brigade = apr_brigade_create(p, bucket_alloc);
4976
4977    do {
4978        if (APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(input_brigade)) {
4979            rv = ap_proxy_read_input(r, backend, input_brigade,
4980                                     HUGE_STRING_LEN);
4981            if (rv != OK) {
4982                return rv;
4983            }
4984        }
4985
4986        /* If this brigade contains EOS, either stop or remove it. */ view japanese
4987        if (APR_BUCKET_IS_EOS(APR_BRIGADE_LAST(input_brigade))) {
4988            seen_eos = 1;
4989        }
4990
4991        apr_brigade_length(input_brigade, 1, &bytes);
4992
4993        if (*bytes_spooled + bytes > max_mem_spool) {
4994            /* can't spool any more in memory; write latest brigade to disk */ view japanese
4995            if (tmpfile == NULL) {
4996                const char *temp_dir;
4997                char *template;
4998
4999                status = apr_temp_dir_get(&temp_dir, p);
5000                if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
5001                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01089)
5002                                  "search for temporary directory failed");
5003                    return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5004                }
5005                apr_filepath_merge(&template, temp_dir,
5006                                   "modproxy.tmp.XXXXXX",
5007                                   APR_FILEPATH_NATIVE, p);
5008                status = apr_file_mktemp(&tmpfile, template, 0, p);
5009                if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
5010                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01090)
5011                                  "creation of temporary file in directory "
5012                                  "%s failed", temp_dir);
5013                    return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5014                }
5015            }
5016            for (e = APR_BRIGADE_FIRST(input_brigade);
5017                 e != APR_BRIGADE_SENTINEL(input_brigade);
5018                 e = APR_BUCKET_NEXT(e)) {
5019                const char *data;
5020                apr_size_t bytes_read, bytes_written;
5021
5022                apr_bucket_read(e, &data, &bytes_read, APR_BLOCK_READ);
5023                status = apr_file_write_full(tmpfile, data, bytes_read, &bytes_written);
5024                if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
5025                    const char *tmpfile_name;
5026
5027                    if (apr_file_name_get(&tmpfile_name, tmpfile) != APR_SUCCESS) {
5028                        tmpfile_name = "(unknown)";
5029                    }
5030                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01091)
5031                                  "write to temporary file %s failed",
5032                                  tmpfile_name);
5033                    return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5034                }
5035                AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(bytes_read == bytes_written);
5036                fsize += bytes_written;
5037            }
5038            apr_brigade_cleanup(input_brigade);
5039        }
5040        else {
5041
5042            /*
5043             * Save input_brigade in body_brigade. (At least) in the SSL case
5044             * input_brigade contains transient buckets whose data would get
5045             * overwritten during the next call of ap_get_brigade in the loop.
5046             * ap_save_brigade ensures these buckets to be set aside.
5047             * Calling ap_save_brigade with NULL as filter is OK, because
5048             * body_brigade already has been created and does not need to get
5049             * created by ap_save_brigade.
5050             */ view japanese
5051            status = ap_save_brigade(NULL, &body_brigade, &input_brigade, p);
5052            if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
5053                return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5054            }
5055
5056        }
5057
5058        *bytes_spooled += bytes;
5059    } while (!seen_eos);
5060
5061    APR_BRIGADE_CONCAT(input_brigade, body_brigade);
5062    if (tmpfile) {
5063        apr_brigade_insert_file(input_brigade, tmpfile, 0, fsize, p);
5064    }
5065    if (apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "proxy-sendextracrlf")) {
5066        e = apr_bucket_immortal_create(CRLF_ASCII, 2, bucket_alloc);
5067        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(input_brigade, e);
5068    }
5069    if (tmpfile) {
5070        /* We dropped metadata buckets when spooling to tmpfile,
5071         * terminate with EOS to allow for flushing in a one go.
5072         */ view japanese
5073        e = apr_bucket_eos_create(bucket_alloc);
5074        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(input_brigade, e);
5075    }
5076    return OK;
5077}
5078
5079PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_pass_brigade(apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc,
5080                                         request_rec *r, proxy_conn_rec *p_conn,
5081                                         conn_rec *origin, apr_bucket_brigade *bb,
5082                                         int flush)
5083{
5084    apr_status_t status;
5085    apr_off_t transferred;
5086
5087    if (flush) {
5088        apr_bucket *e = apr_bucket_flush_create(bucket_alloc);
5089        APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, e);
5090    }
5091    apr_brigade_length(bb, 0, &transferred);
5092    if (transferred != -1)
5093        p_conn->worker->s->transferred += transferred;
5094    status = ap_pass_brigade(origin->output_filters, bb);
5095    /* Cleanup the brigade now to avoid buckets lifetime
5096     * issues in case of error returned below. */ view japanese
5097    apr_brigade_cleanup(bb);
5098    if (status != APR_SUCCESS) {
5099        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, status, r, APLOGNO(01084)
5100                      "pass request body failed to %pI (%s)",
5101                      p_conn->addr, p_conn->hostname);
5102        if (origin->aborted) {
5103            const char *ssl_note;
5104
5105            if (((ssl_note = apr_table_get(origin->notes, "SSL_connect_rv"))
5106                 != NULL) && (strcmp(ssl_note, "err") == 0)) {
5107                return ap_proxyerror(r, HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,
5108                                     "Error during SSL Handshake with"
5109                                     " remote server");
5110            }
5111            return APR_STATUS_IS_TIMEUP(status) ? HTTP_GATEWAY_TIME_OUT : HTTP_BAD_GATEWAY;
5112        }
5113        else {
5114            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
5115        }
5116    }
5117    return OK;
5118}
5119
5120/* Fill in unknown schemes from apr_uri_port_of_scheme() */ view japanese
5121
5122typedef struct proxy_schemes_t {
5123    const char *name;
5124    apr_port_t default_port;
5125} proxy_schemes_t ;
5126
5127static proxy_schemes_t pschemes[] =
5128{
5129    {"fcgi",     8000},
5130    {"ajp",      AJP13_DEF_PORT},
5131    {"scgi",     SCGI_DEF_PORT},
5132    {"h2c",      DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT},
5133    {"h2",       DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT},
5134    {"ws",       DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT},
5135    {"wss",      DEFAULT_HTTPS_PORT},
5136    { NULL, 0xFFFF }     /* unknown port */ view japanese
5137};
5138
5139PROXY_DECLARE(apr_port_t) ap_proxy_port_of_scheme(const char *scheme)
5140{
5141    if (scheme) {
5142        apr_port_t port;
5143        if ((port = apr_uri_port_of_scheme(scheme)) != 0) {
5144            return port;
5145        } else {
5146            proxy_schemes_t *pscheme;
5147            for (pscheme = pschemes; pscheme->name != NULL; ++pscheme) {
5148                if (ap_cstr_casecmp(scheme, pscheme->name) == 0) {
5149                    return pscheme->default_port;
5150                }
5151            }
5152        }
5153    }
5154    return 0;
5155}
5156
5157static APR_INLINE int ap_filter_should_yield(ap_filter_t *f)
5158{
5159    return f->c->data_in_output_filters;
5160}
5161
5162static APR_INLINE int ap_filter_output_pending(conn_rec *c)
5163{
5164    ap_filter_t *f = c->output_filters;
5165    while (f->next) {
5166        f = f->next;
5167    }
5168    if (f->frec->filter_func.out_func(f, NULL)) {
5169        return AP_FILTER_ERROR;
5170    }
5171    return c->data_in_output_filters ? OK : DECLINED;
5172}
5173
5174PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_buckets_lifetime_transform(request_rec *r,
5175                                                      apr_bucket_brigade *from,
5176                                                      apr_bucket_brigade *to)
5177{
5178    apr_bucket *e;
5179    apr_bucket *new;
5180    const char *data;
5181    apr_size_t bytes;
5182    apr_status_t rv = APR_SUCCESS;
5183    apr_bucket_alloc_t *bucket_alloc = to->bucket_alloc;
5184
5185    apr_brigade_cleanup(to);
5186    for (e = APR_BRIGADE_FIRST(from);
5187         e != APR_BRIGADE_SENTINEL(from);
5188         e = APR_BUCKET_NEXT(e)) {
5189        if (!APR_BUCKET_IS_METADATA(e)) {
5190            apr_bucket_read(e, &data, &bytes, APR_BLOCK_READ);
5191            new = apr_bucket_transient_create(data, bytes, bucket_alloc);
5192            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(to, new);
5193        }
5194        else if (APR_BUCKET_IS_FLUSH(e)) {
5195            new = apr_bucket_flush_create(bucket_alloc);
5196            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(to, new);
5197        }
5198        else if (APR_BUCKET_IS_EOS(e)) {
5199            new = apr_bucket_eos_create(bucket_alloc);
5200            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(to, new);
5201        }
5202        else {
5203            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(03304)
5204                          "Unhandled bucket type of type %s in"
5205                          " ap_proxy_buckets_lifetime_transform", e->type->name);
5206            rv = APR_EGENERAL;
5207        }
5208    }
5209    return rv;
5210}
5211
5212/* An arbitrary large value to address pathological case where we keep
5213 * reading from one side only, without scheduling the other direction for
5214 * too long. This can happen with large MTU and small read buffers, like
5215 * micro-benchmarking huge files bidirectional transfer with client, proxy
5216 * and backend on localhost for instance. Though we could just ignore the
5217 * case and let the sender stop by itself at some point when/if it needs to
5218 * receive data, or the receiver stop when/if it needs to send...
5219 */ view japanese
5220#define PROXY_TRANSFER_MAX_READS 10000
5221
5222PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections(
5223                                                       request_rec *r,
5224                                                       conn_rec *c_i,
5225                                                       conn_rec *c_o,
5226                                                       apr_bucket_brigade *bb_i,
5227                                                       apr_bucket_brigade *bb_o,
5228                                                       const char *name,
5229                                                       int *sent,
5230                                                       apr_off_t bsize,
5231                                                       int flags)
5232{
5233    apr_status_t rv;
5234    int flush_each = 0;
5235    unsigned int num_reads = 0;
5236#ifdef DEBUGGING
5237    apr_off_t len;
5238#endif
5239
5240    /*
5241     * Compat: since FLUSH_EACH is default (and zero) for legacy reasons, we
5242     * pretend it's no FLUSH_AFTER nor YIELD_PENDING flags, the latter because
5243     * flushing would defeat the purpose of checking for pending data (hence
5244     * determine whether or not the output chain/stack is full for stopping).
5245     */ view japanese
5246    if (!(flags & (AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_FLUSH_AFTER |
5247                   AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_YIELD_PENDING))) {
5248        flush_each = 1;
5249    }
5250
5251    for (;;) {
5252        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb_i);
5253        rv = ap_get_brigade(c_i->input_filters, bb_i, AP_MODE_READBYTES,
5254                            APR_NONBLOCK_READ, bsize);
5255        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5256            if (!APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(rv) && !APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(rv)) {
5257                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(03308)
5258                              "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections: "
5259                              "error on %s - ap_get_brigade",
5260                              name);
5261                if (rv == APR_INCOMPLETE) {
5262                    /* Don't return APR_INCOMPLETE, it'd mean "should yield"
5263                     * for the caller, while it means "incomplete body" here
5264                     * from ap_http_filter(), which is an error.
5265                     */ view japanese
5266                    rv = APR_EGENERAL;
5267                }
5268            }
5269            break;
5270        }
5271
5272        if (c_o->aborted) {
5273            apr_brigade_cleanup(bb_i);
5274            flags &= ~AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_FLUSH_AFTER;
5275            rv = APR_EPIPE;
5276            break;
5277        }
5278        if (APR_BRIGADE_EMPTY(bb_i)) {
5279            break;
5280        }
5281#ifdef DEBUGGING
5282        len = -1;
5283        apr_brigade_length(bb_i, 0, &len);
5284        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(03306)
5285                      "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections: "
5286                      "read %" APR_OFF_T_FMT
5287                      " bytes from %s", len, name);
5288#endif
5289        if (sent) {
5290            *sent = 1;
5291        }
5292        ap_proxy_buckets_lifetime_transform(r, bb_i, bb_o);
5293        if (flush_each) {
5294            apr_bucket *b;
5295            /*
5296             * Do not use ap_fflush here since this would cause the flush
5297             * bucket to be sent in a separate brigade afterwards which
5298             * causes some filters to set aside the buckets from the first
5299             * brigade and process them when FLUSH arrives in the second
5300             * brigade. As set asides of our transformed buckets involve
5301             * memory copying we try to avoid this. If we have the flush
5302             * bucket in the first brigade they directly process the
5303             * buckets without setting them aside.
5304             */ view japanese
5305            b = apr_bucket_flush_create(bb_o->bucket_alloc);
5306            APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb_o, b);
5307        }
5308        rv = ap_pass_brigade(c_o->output_filters, bb_o);
5309        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb_o);
5310        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5311            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(03307)
5312                          "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections: "
5313                          "error on %s - ap_pass_brigade",
5314                          name);
5315            flags &= ~AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_FLUSH_AFTER;
5316            break;
5317        }
5318
5319        /* Yield if the output filters stack is full? This is to avoid
5320         * blocking and give the caller a chance to POLLOUT async.
5321         */ view japanese
5322        if ((flags & AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_YIELD_PENDING)
5323                && ap_filter_should_yield(c_o->output_filters)) {
5324            int rc = ap_filter_output_pending(c_o);
5325            if (rc == OK) {
5326                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
5327                              "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections: "
5328                              "yield (output pending)");
5329                rv = APR_INCOMPLETE;
5330                break;
5331            }
5332            if (rc != DECLINED) {
5333                rv = AP_FILTER_ERROR;
5334                break;
5335            }
5336        }
5337
5338        /* Yield if we keep hold of the thread for too long? This gives
5339         * the caller a chance to schedule the other direction too.
5340         */ view japanese
5341        if ((flags & AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_YIELD_MAX_READS)
5342                && ++num_reads > PROXY_TRANSFER_MAX_READS) {
5343            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r,
5344                          "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections: "
5345                          "yield (max reads)");
5346            rv = APR_SUCCESS;
5347            break;
5348        }
5349    }
5350
5351    if (flags & AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_FLUSH_AFTER) {
5352        ap_fflush(c_o->output_filters, bb_o);
5353        apr_brigade_cleanup(bb_o);
5354    }
5355    apr_brigade_cleanup(bb_i);
5356
5357    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, rv, r,
5358                  "ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections complete (%s %pI)",
5359                  (c_i == r->connection) ? "to" : "from",
5360                  (c_i == r->connection) ? c_o->client_addr
5361                                         : c_i->client_addr);
5362
5363    if (APR_STATUS_IS_EAGAIN(rv)) {
5364        rv = APR_SUCCESS;
5365    }
5366    return rv;
5367}
5368
5369struct proxy_tunnel_conn {
5370    /* the other side of the tunnel */ view japanese
5371    struct proxy_tunnel_conn *other;
5372
5373    conn_rec *c;
5374    const char *name;
5375
5376    apr_pollfd_t *pfd;
5377    apr_bucket_brigade *bb;
5378
5379    unsigned int down_in:1,
5380                 down_out:1;
5381};
5382
5383PROXY_DECLARE(apr_status_t) ap_proxy_tunnel_create(proxy_tunnel_rec **ptunnel,
5384                                                   request_rec *r, conn_rec *c_o,
5385                                                   const char *scheme)
5386{
5387    apr_status_t rv;
5388    conn_rec *c_i = r->connection;
5389    apr_interval_time_t client_timeout = -1, origin_timeout = -1;
5390    proxy_tunnel_rec *tunnel;
5391
5392    *ptunnel = NULL;
5393
5394    tunnel = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(*tunnel));
5395
5396    rv = apr_pollset_create(&tunnel->pollset, 2, r->pool, APR_POLLSET_NOCOPY);
5397    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5398        return rv;
5399    }
5400
5401    tunnel->r = r;
5402    tunnel->scheme = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, scheme);
5403    tunnel->client = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(struct proxy_tunnel_conn));
5404    tunnel->origin = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(struct proxy_tunnel_conn));
5405    tunnel->pfds = apr_array_make(r->pool, 2, sizeof(apr_pollfd_t));
5406    tunnel->read_buf_size = ap_get_read_buf_size(r);
5407    tunnel->client->other = tunnel->origin;
5408    tunnel->origin->other = tunnel->client;
5409    tunnel->timeout = -1;
5410
5411    tunnel->client->c = c_i;
5412    tunnel->client->name = "client";
5413    tunnel->client->bb = apr_brigade_create(c_i->pool, c_i->bucket_alloc);
5414    tunnel->client->pfd = &APR_ARRAY_PUSH(tunnel->pfds, apr_pollfd_t);
5415    tunnel->client->pfd->p = r->pool;
5416    tunnel->client->pfd->desc_type = APR_NO_DESC;
5417    rv = ap_get_pollfd_from_conn(tunnel->client->c,
5418                                 tunnel->client->pfd, &client_timeout);
5419    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5420        return rv;
5421    }
5422    tunnel->client->pfd->client_data = tunnel->client;
5423    if (tunnel->client->pfd->desc_type == APR_POLL_SOCKET) {
5424        apr_socket_opt_set(tunnel->client->pfd->desc.s, APR_SO_NONBLOCK, 1);
5425    }
5426
5427    tunnel->origin->c = c_o;
5428    tunnel->origin->name = "origin";
5429    tunnel->origin->bb = apr_brigade_create(c_o->pool, c_o->bucket_alloc);
5430    tunnel->origin->pfd = &APR_ARRAY_PUSH(tunnel->pfds, apr_pollfd_t);
5431    tunnel->origin->pfd->p = r->pool;
5432    tunnel->origin->pfd->desc_type = APR_POLL_SOCKET;
5433    tunnel->origin->pfd->desc.s = ap_get_conn_socket(c_o);
5434    tunnel->origin->pfd->client_data = tunnel->origin;
5435    apr_socket_timeout_get(tunnel->origin->pfd->desc.s, &origin_timeout);
5436    apr_socket_opt_set(tunnel->origin->pfd->desc.s, APR_SO_NONBLOCK, 1);
5437
5438    /* Defaults to the largest timeout of both connections */ view japanese
5439    tunnel->timeout = (client_timeout >= 0 && client_timeout > origin_timeout ?
5440                       client_timeout : origin_timeout);
5441
5442    /* No coalescing filters */ view japanese
5443    ap_remove_output_filter_byhandle(c_i->output_filters,
5444                                     "SSL/TLS Coalescing Filter");
5445    ap_remove_output_filter_byhandle(c_o->output_filters,
5446                                     "SSL/TLS Coalescing Filter");
5447
5448    /* Bidirectional non-HTTP stream will confuse mod_reqtimeoout */ view japanese
5449    ap_remove_input_filter_byhandle(c_i->input_filters, "reqtimeout");
5450
5451    /* The input/output filter stacks should contain connection filters only */ view japanese
5452    r->input_filters = r->proto_input_filters = c_i->input_filters;
5453    r->output_filters = r->proto_output_filters = c_i->output_filters;
5454
5455    /* Won't be reused after tunneling */ view japanese
5456    c_i->keepalive = AP_CONN_CLOSE;
5457    c_o->keepalive = AP_CONN_CLOSE;
5458
5459    /* Disable half-close forwarding for this request? */ view japanese
5460    if (apr_table_get(r->subprocess_env, "proxy-nohalfclose")) {
5461        tunnel->nohalfclose = 1;
5462    }
5463
5464    if (tunnel->client->pfd->desc_type == APR_POLL_SOCKET) {
5465        /* Both ends are sockets, the poll strategy is:
5466         * - poll both sides POLLOUT
5467         * - when one side is writable, remove the POLLOUT
5468         *   and add POLLIN to the other side.
5469         * - tunnel arriving data, remove POLLIN from the source
5470         *   again and add POLLOUT to the receiving side
5471         * - on EOF on read, remove the POLLIN from that side
5472         * Repeat until both sides are down */ view japanese
5473        tunnel->client->pfd->reqevents = APR_POLLOUT | APR_POLLERR;
5474        tunnel->origin->pfd->reqevents = APR_POLLOUT | APR_POLLERR;
5475        if ((rv = apr_pollset_add(tunnel->pollset, tunnel->origin->pfd)) ||
5476            (rv = apr_pollset_add(tunnel->pollset, tunnel->client->pfd))) {
5477            return rv;
5478        }
5479    }
5480    else if (tunnel->client->pfd->desc_type == APR_POLL_FILE) {
5481        /* Input is a PIPE fd, the poll strategy is:
5482         * - always POLLIN on origin
5483         * - use socket strategy described above for client only
5484         * otherwise the same
5485         */ view japanese
5486        tunnel->client->pfd->reqevents = 0;
5487        tunnel->origin->pfd->reqevents = APR_POLLIN | APR_POLLHUP |
5488                                         APR_POLLOUT | APR_POLLERR;
5489        if ((rv = apr_pollset_add(tunnel->pollset, tunnel->origin->pfd))) {
5490            return rv;
5491        }
5492    }
5493    else {
5494        /* input is already closed, unsual, but we know nothing about
5495         * the tunneled protocol. */ view japanese
5496        tunnel->client->down_in = 1;
5497        tunnel->origin->pfd->reqevents = APR_POLLIN | APR_POLLHUP;
5498        if ((rv = apr_pollset_add(tunnel->pollset, tunnel->origin->pfd))) {
5499            return rv;
5500        }
5501    }
5502
5503    *ptunnel = tunnel;
5504    return APR_SUCCESS;
5505}
5506
5507static void add_pollset(apr_pollset_t *pollset, apr_pollfd_t *pfd,
5508                        apr_int16_t events)
5509{
5510    apr_status_t rv;
5511
5512    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT((pfd->reqevents & events) == 0);
5513
5514    if (pfd->reqevents) {
5515        rv = apr_pollset_remove(pollset, pfd);
5516        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5517            AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(1);
5518        }
5519    }
5520
5521    if (events & APR_POLLIN) {
5522        events |= APR_POLLHUP;
5523    }
5524    pfd->reqevents |= events | APR_POLLERR;
5525    rv = apr_pollset_add(pollset, pfd);
5526    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5527        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(1);
5528    }
5529}
5530
5531static void del_pollset(apr_pollset_t *pollset, apr_pollfd_t *pfd,
5532                        apr_int16_t events)
5533{
5534    apr_status_t rv;
5535
5536    AP_DEBUG_ASSERT((pfd->reqevents & events) != 0);
5537
5538    rv = apr_pollset_remove(pollset, pfd);
5539    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5540        AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
5541        return;
5542    }
5543
5544    if (events & APR_POLLIN) {
5545        events |= APR_POLLHUP;
5546    }
5547    if (pfd->reqevents & ~(events | APR_POLLERR)) {
5548        pfd->reqevents &= ~events;
5549        rv = apr_pollset_add(pollset, pfd);
5550        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5551            AP_DEBUG_ASSERT(0);
5552            return;
5553        }
5554    }
5555    else {
5556        pfd->reqevents = 0;
5557    }
5558}
5559
5560static int proxy_tunnel_forward(proxy_tunnel_rec *tunnel,
5561                                 struct proxy_tunnel_conn *in)
5562{
5563    struct proxy_tunnel_conn *out = in->other;
5564    apr_status_t rv;
5565    int sent = 0;
5566
5567    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, tunnel->r,
5568                  "proxy: %s: %s input ready",
5569                  tunnel->scheme, in->name);
5570
5571    rv = ap_proxy_transfer_between_connections(tunnel->r,
5572                                               in->c, out->c,
5573                                               in->bb, out->bb,
5574                                               in->name, &sent,
5575                                               tunnel->read_buf_size,
5576                                           AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_YIELD_PENDING |
5577                                           AP_PROXY_TRANSFER_YIELD_MAX_READS);
5578    if (sent && out == tunnel->client) {
5579        tunnel->replied = 1;
5580    }
5581    if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5582        if (APR_STATUS_IS_INCOMPLETE(rv)) {
5583            /* Pause POLLIN while waiting for POLLOUT on the other
5584             * side, hence avoid filling the output filters even
5585             * more to avoid blocking there.
5586             */ view japanese
5587            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE5, 0, tunnel->r,
5588                          "proxy: %s: %s wait writable",
5589                          tunnel->scheme, out->name);
5590        }
5591        else if (APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(rv)) {
5592            /* Stop POLLIN and wait for POLLOUT (flush) on the
5593             * other side to shut it down.
5594             */ view japanese
5595            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, 0, tunnel->r,
5596                          "proxy: %s: %s read shutdown",
5597                          tunnel->scheme, in->name);
5598            if (tunnel->nohalfclose) {
5599                /* No half-close forwarding, we are done both ways as
5600                 * soon as one side shuts down.
5601                 */ view japanese
5602                return DONE;
5603            }
5604            in->down_in = 1;
5605        }
5606        else {
5607            /* Real failure, bail out */
5608            return HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5609        }
5610
5611        del_pollset(tunnel->pollset, in->pfd, APR_POLLIN);
5612        if (out->pfd->desc_type == APR_POLL_SOCKET) {
5613            /* if the output is a SOCKET, we can stop polling the input
5614             * until the output signals POLLOUT again. */ view japanese
5615            add_pollset(tunnel->pollset, out->pfd, APR_POLLOUT);
5616        }
5617        else {
5618            /* We can't use POLLOUT in this direction for the only
5619             * APR_POLL_FILE case we have so far (mod_h2's "signal" pipe),
5620             * we assume that the client's ouput filters chain will block/flush
5621             * if necessary (i.e. no pending data), hence that the origin
5622             * is EOF when reaching here. This direction is over. */ view japanese
5623            ap_assert(in->down_in && APR_STATUS_IS_EOF(rv));
5624            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, 0, tunnel->r,
5625                          "proxy: %s: %s write shutdown",
5626                          tunnel->scheme, out->name);
5627            out->down_out = 1;
5628        }
5629    }
5630
5631    return OK;
5632}
5633
5634PROXY_DECLARE(int) ap_proxy_tunnel_run(proxy_tunnel_rec *tunnel)
5635{
5636    int status = OK, rc;
5637    request_rec *r = tunnel->r;
5638    apr_pollset_t *pollset = tunnel->pollset;
5639    struct proxy_tunnel_conn *client = tunnel->client,
5640                             *origin = tunnel->origin;
5641    apr_interval_time_t timeout = tunnel->timeout >= 0 ? tunnel->timeout : -1;
5642    const char *scheme = tunnel->scheme;
5643    apr_status_t rv;
5644
5645    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE1, 0, r, APLOGNO(10212)
5646                  "proxy: %s: tunnel running (timeout %lf)",
5647                  scheme, timeout >= 0 ? (double)timeout / APR_USEC_PER_SEC
5648                                       : (double)-1.0);
5649
5650    /* Loop until both directions of the connection are closed,
5651     * or a failure occurs.
5652     */ view japanese
5653    do {
5654        const apr_pollfd_t *results;
5655        apr_int32_t nresults, i;
5656
5657        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, r,
5658                      "proxy: %s: polling (client=%hx, origin=%hx)",
5659                      scheme, client->pfd->reqevents, origin->pfd->reqevents);
5660        do {
5661            rv = apr_pollset_poll(pollset, timeout, &nresults, &results);
5662        } while (APR_STATUS_IS_EINTR(rv));
5663
5664        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
5665            if (APR_STATUS_IS_TIMEUP(rv)) {
5666                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE2, 0, r, APLOGNO(10213)
5667                              "proxy: %s: polling timed out "
5668                              "(client=%hx, origin=%hx)",
5669                              scheme, client->pfd->reqevents,
5670                              origin->pfd->reqevents);
5671                status = HTTP_GATEWAY_TIME_OUT;
5672            }
5673            else {
5674                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, rv, r, APLOGNO(10214)
5675                              "proxy: %s: polling failed", scheme);
5676                status = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5677            }
5678            goto done;
5679        }
5680
5681        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, r, APLOGNO(10215)
5682                      "proxy: %s: woken up, %i result(s)", scheme, nresults);
5683
5684        for (i = 0; i < nresults; i++) {
5685            const apr_pollfd_t *pfd = &results[i];
5686            struct proxy_tunnel_conn *tc = pfd->client_data;
5687
5688            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, r,
5689                          "proxy: %s: #%i: %s: %hx/%hx", scheme, i,
5690                          tc->name, pfd->rtnevents, tc->pfd->reqevents);
5691
5692            /* sanity check */ view japanese
5693            if (pfd->desc.s != client->pfd->desc.s
5694                    && pfd->desc.s != origin->pfd->desc.s) {
5695                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10222)
5696                              "proxy: %s: unknown socket in pollset", scheme);
5697                status = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5698                goto done;
5699            }
5700
5701            if (!(pfd->rtnevents & (APR_POLLIN  | APR_POLLOUT |
5702                                    APR_POLLHUP | APR_POLLERR))) {
5703                /* this catches POLLNVAL etc.. */ view japanese
5704                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10220)
5705                              "proxy: %s: polling events error (%x)",
5706                              scheme, pfd->rtnevents);
5707                status = HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR;
5708                goto done;
5709            }
5710
5711            /* We want to write if we asked for POLLOUT and got:
5712             * - POLLOUT: the socket is ready for write;
5713             * - !POLLIN: the socket is in error state (POLLERR) so we let
5714             *   the user know by failing the write and log, OR the socket
5715             *   is shutdown for read already (POLLHUP) so we have to
5716             *   shutdown for write.
5717             */ view japanese
5718            if ((tc->pfd->reqevents & APR_POLLOUT)
5719                    && ((pfd->rtnevents & APR_POLLOUT)
5720                        || !(tc->pfd->reqevents & APR_POLLIN)
5721                        || !(pfd->rtnevents & (APR_POLLIN | APR_POLLHUP)))) {
5722                struct proxy_tunnel_conn *out = tc, *in = tc->other;
5723
5724                ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE8, 0, r,
5725                              "proxy: %s: %s output ready",
5726                              scheme, out->name);
5727
5728                rc = ap_filter_output_pending(out->c);
5729                if (rc == OK) {
5730                    /* Keep polling out (only) */ view japanese
5731                    continue;
5732                }
5733                if (rc != DECLINED) {
5734                    /* Real failure, bail out */ view japanese
5735                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(10221)
5736                                  "proxy: %s: %s flushing failed (%i)",
5737                                  scheme, out->name, rc);
5738                    status = rc;
5739                    goto done;
5740                }
5741
5742                /* No more pending data. If the other side is not readable
5743                 * anymore it's time to shutdown for write (this direction
5744                 * is over). Otherwise back to normal business.
5745                 */ view japanese
5746                del_pollset(pollset, out->pfd, APR_POLLOUT);
5747                if (in->down_in) {
5748                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE3, 0, r,
5749                                  "proxy: %s: %s write shutdown",
5750                                  scheme, out->name);
5751                    apr_socket_shutdown(out->pfd->desc.s, 1);
5752                    out->down_out = 1;
5753                }
5754                else {
5755                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_TRACE5, 0, r,
5756                                  "proxy: %s: %s resume writable",
5757                                  scheme, out->name);
5758                    add_pollset(pollset, in->pfd, APR_POLLIN);
5759
5760                    /* Flush any pending input data now, we don't know when
5761                     * the next POLLIN will trigger and retaining data might
5762                     * deadlock the underlying protocol. We don't check for
5763                     * pending data first with ap_filter_input_pending() since
5764                     * the read from proxy_tunnel_forward() is nonblocking
5765                     * anyway and returning OK if there's no data.
5766                     */ view japanese
5767                    rc = proxy_tunnel_forward(tunnel, in);
5768                    if (rc != OK) {
5769                        status = rc;
5770                        goto done;
5771                    }
5772                }
5773            }
5774
5775            /* We want to read if we asked for POLLIN|HUP and got:
5776             * - POLLIN|HUP: the socket is ready for read or EOF (POLLHUP);
5777             * - !POLLOUT: the socket is in error state (POLLERR) so we let
5778             *   the user know by failing the read and log.
